Usage IQ — Enterprise Code Review

Exhaustive multi-agent audit · every finding adversarially re-verified · read-only
C-
The grade reflects a feature-rich, functionally ambitious application that is materially unsafe to run as an enterprise production system handling special-category data. There are no Critical findings and the core auth model (Google-to-JWT, per-endpoint RequirePermission, household scoping) is coherently designed, which keeps this above the failing range. But 38 High findings cluster into structural failures that matter most for exactly this app's threat model: sensitive PII/health/GPS bodies are captured into request logs and replayed to a defaultable low-privilege permission; a committed default secret ("Starbucks") is the live production PII-unmask gate; there is no database backup or disaster-recovery story on a single EC2 host; untested code ships to production because the deploy workflow has no test gate and the frontend has effectively zero enforced coverage; and the deploy IAM role can run arbitrary root shell on the PII/health/payments host. Each of these individually would block an enterprise go-live for a system holding cycle/meds, GPS, and payment data. The volume of Medium findings (93) shows the pattern is systemic rather than incidental — access-control edge cases, missing DB indexes on hot paths, God classes/services, and disabled-account enforcement gaps recur across the codebase. The engineering is real and the design intent is sound; the operational, data-protection, and testing rigor required for enterprise readiness is not yet there. That is a C-, not a D — a focused remediation program can move it up quickly because most Highs are contained, well-localized fixes rather than rewrites.

Executive summary

Usage IQ is an ambitious "Agentic Life OS" — an Angular 22 single-page app backed by a .NET 9 minimal-API service on EF Core/PostgreSQL, with a WPF desktop agent and a reporter/ingestion pipeline, deployed to AWS. It began as a token-usage dashboard and has grown into a broad personal-platform spanning analytics, family/household management, chat, health and fitness tracking (including menstrual-cycle and medication data), GPS location sharing, and payments. Authentication is Google sign-in exchanged for an app-issued JWT, gated by a per-endpoint permission filter and household scoping. The design intent is coherent and the breadth of working functionality is genuinely impressive for the team size implied.

The overall security and quality posture, however, is that of a fast-moving product build rather than a hardened enterprise system, and the gap matters acutely because this app handles special-category data (health, precise location, payments, PII). The review surfaced no Critical issues and confirmed the central permission model is soundly conceived, but 38 High and 93 Medium findings reveal that data-protection and operational discipline have not kept pace with feature velocity. The most dangerous theme is sensitive-data leakage through observability: the request-logging middleware captures full request and response bodies — including GPS coordinates, cycle/meds mutations, and other PII — into a RequestLog that is then replayed to anyone holding activity.view, a permission that can be auto-granted at open sign-up. The redaction layer only masks a fixed set of secret key names and a manually maintained allowlist of email paths, so it structurally cannot protect same-user special-category data.

A second theme is a systemic weakness in secret and identity lifecycle. A committed default key ("Starbucks") is the live production gate for unmasking real user emails; the at-rest token-encryption key is derived from the JWT signing key, so rotating the signing key irreversibly bricks every stored refresh token; ingest keys of deleted/disabled users stay valid; and disabling a user does not invalidate their live JWT or tear down their open SignalR connection, leaving several endpoints reachable post-disable. Secret-bearing outbound HTTP clients follow redirects, and a custom ingest-key header is re-sent across cross-host redirects, creating credential-leak surfaces. A third theme is operational fragility: production Postgres lives only in a local Docker volume on one EC2 host with no backup or snapshot, the AMI auto-resolves so an "idempotent" redeploy can destroy the database, images deploy as mutable :latest tags with no immutable digest (breaking rollback), and there is no error reporting, alerting, or log aggregation anywhere — failures reach one container's stdout and stop there. Compounding this, the deploy workflow has no dependency on tests, and the frontend has effectively zero enforced coverage (one stale scaffold spec that would fail if ever run), so untested code ships to production.

A fourth, quality-of-engineering theme is structural sprawl: a 6,955-line GeminiService God class fronts every AI feature, a 3,438-line frontend Api service spans ~35 domains, a single DbContext exposes 101 DbSets, and mobile "twins" re-implement sensitive UI (permission-grant and email-reveal) rather than sharing it — one such twin's docstring even falsely claims emails are never revealed. Missing indexes on hot sort columns (notably UsageRecord.TimestampUtc) and unbounded in-memory materialization of up to two million rows per request threaten performance and stability under real data volumes.

The standout strengths are worth stating clearly: the review judged 771 areas sound, and several security-sensitive pieces are implemented correctly — GPS consent and null-handling in the desktop locator, consistent timestamptz mapping across all DateTime columns, the Discord HTTP client correctly suppressing redirects, and a genuinely thoughtful permission catalog and household-scoping spine. The problems are overwhelmingly localized, well-understood classes of defect (log redaction, backups, index additions, disabled-account re-checks) rather than architectural dead-ends. With a focused remediation program, this codebase can reach enterprise readiness without a rewrite.

Top risks

  • Special-category PII leak via observability: GPS coordinates, cycle/meds, and other sensitive request/response bodies are captured into RequestLog and replayed to any activity.view holder — a permission auto-grantable at open sign-up — bypassing location.view-all and cross-permission boundaries (RequestLoggingMiddleware.cs).
  • Committed default secret is the live production PII gate: the hardcoded EmailRevealKey "Starbucks" unmasks real user emails with no production override (appsettings.json).
  • No backup or disaster recovery: production Postgres exists only in a local Docker volume on one EC2 host, the AMI auto-resolves so an idempotent redeploy can destroy all data, and volumes are deleted on termination — a single failure or rerun is total, unrecoverable data loss.
  • Untested code ships to production: the deploy workflow has no test-gate dependency, images deploy as mutable :latest tags (breaking rollback), and the frontend has effectively zero enforced test coverage.
  • Identity/secret lifecycle failures: disabling a user does not invalidate their live JWT or tear down their SignalR connection, deleted/disabled users' ingest keys stay valid, and rotating the JWT key irreversibly bricks every encrypted refresh token (TokenProtector derives its key from Jwt:Key).
  • Excessive blast radius and no alerting: the deploy IAM role can run arbitrary root shell on the PII/health/payments host and EC2 does not enforce IMDSv2 (SSRF-to-credential-theft), while no error reporting, alerting, or log aggregation exists to detect abuse or outages.

Remediation status 204 in-scope findings addressed across 2 fix rounds + regression repair

Following the audit, a multi-round automated remediation applied fixes to the 204 in-scope findings (36 deployment/AWS/data-practice findings were excluded per owner instruction). Every change was verified against a clean build of all six .NET projects and the Angular app, plus the full backend test suite (unit + Testcontainers integration). Twelve regressions surfaced by the test suite were triaged and repaired (or, in one case, identified as a pre-existing date-dependent flaky test unrelated to the changes).

Fixed & verified (build + tests green)

  • Email privacy: the committed shared "Starbucks" EmailRevealKey was removed entirely; unmasking other users' emails is now gated by a new per-admin permission (users.email.reveal) across the API and both desktop + mobile Users pages.
  • Request-log PII: RequestLoggingMiddleware no longer persists request/response BODIES for sensitive routes (location, cycle, meds, health, finance, users, tracker); raw bodies in the observability log are gated to admins; value-level JWT/coordinate scrubbing retained.
  • Disabled-account revocation: disabling a user bumps SessionVersion and is rejected at token validation, tears down live SignalR connections (ChatHub), and is re-checked on the presence routes — a disabled token is invalidated on the next request.
  • Ingest-key transport: QuickAddClient now refuses to send the key over non-loopback cleartext and disables auto-redirect (matching IngestClient); TokenProtector derives its at-rest key from a dedicated Encryption:Key (falling back to Jwt:Key) so signing-key rotation no longer bricks refresh tokens.
  • SSRF / bounded reads: web-push subscribe + send validate the endpoint against an https browser-push allowlist; Fitbit/Google-Calendar/WorkoutX upstream reads are size-capped; the ip-geo client caps its response buffer and keeps redirects disabled.
  • Injection: CSV export neutralizes formula-injection; the GIF proxy sends X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff; login validates ?returnUrl against open redirects.
  • Data layer (one EF migration): hot-path indexes on UsageRecord (TimestampUtc + composites + sort columns) and AiUsageLog; UsageRecord→IngestedFile decoupled to SetNull so purging a file no longer cascade-deletes billing rows; HouseholdMember/HardChallenge FKs added with an orphan-purge guard; the ModelPricings identity sequence is advanced past its seed so runtime inserts don't collide.
  • Correctness: AiUsageLogWriter/RequestLogWriter shutdown + prune fixes; ActivityEmitter at-most-once race; HabitEndpoints leaderboard N+1 batching; connection-string empty-value handling; design-time DB factory no longer embeds a committed password.
  • Frontend: a11y (aria-labels on placeholder-only inputs, an inert closed mobile drawer, roving-tabindex tablists), perf (memoized ECharts options, activity-log row windowing, memoized sorts), and shared-module extractions (core/insights-shared, core/finance-categories, shared/optimistic-mutation).
  • Dependencies: floating 9.0.* NuGet versions pinned to exact versions with committed lock files.
  • Tests: email-visibility integration rewritten for the permission model; permission catalog count updated; WebPush tests aligned to the allowlist; disabled-user test updated to the stronger 401 behavior.

Deferred — recommended, needs your sign-off (high-effort / high-risk structural)

  • God-file splits (recommended, high-risk to automate): GeminiService (~6,955 lines), core/api.ts (~3,438), models.ts (~6,675 exports), the single 101-DbSet UsageDbContext, and the oversized Tracker component.
  • App-wide error-envelope standardization (ProblemDetails vs { message } across ~39 handlers).
  • Centralized household-membership authorization helper (dedupe IsMember/role checks duplicated across ~8 family endpoints).
  • Shared TrackerService extraction (dedupe tracker logging/visibility logic across the HardChallenge + AI write paths).
  • Move the app JWT out of localStorage (in-memory or HttpOnly cookie + refresh) — a security improvement but a risky auth-flow redesign.
  • Migrate off the unmaintained WebPush 1.0.13 library on the crypto push path.
  • Optimistic concurrency tokens (xmin) on finance/bill entities — reverted: the Npgsql API is unavailable on the pinned provider and it would require DbUpdateConcurrencyException handling across those endpoints.
  • Content-Security-Policy + nonces for the index.html bootstrap scripts (risk of breaking the theme/keyboard bootstrap).
  • Chat message-list virtualization (CDK virtual scroll) and a per-channel retained-history cap.
  • Cross-instance scheduler leader-election / advisory locks (not required on the current single-instance deployment).
  • End-to-end correlation-id (TraceId) propagation + real metrics/observability export.
  • Relocate readFileAsBase64/downscaleToJpeg to a shared module (touches ~8 consumer files; low value, high churn).
  • Chat direct-message id-enumeration: a non-admin gets 400 for an unknown user vs 403 for a valid non-contact, which distinguishes existence. Making the two responses uniform was reverted because it contradicts the contacts UX ("add them first") that the test suite encodes; collapsing them is a product decision.
  • One pre-existing date-dependent flaky test (WrappedTests.Hydration_best_streak) seeds future-dated hydration data, so it fails in the first days of a month (Wrapped's window ends at 'today'). Not caused by these changes; recommend a reference-date test seam or seeding relative to today.

Out of scope — excluded per owner instruction (deployment / AWS / data-practices)

  • Database backups / snapshots / disaster recovery (single-host Postgres volume).
  • AWS IAM least-privilege, IMDSv2 enforcement, security-group tightening.
  • CI test-gate before deploy, immutable image digests (vs :latest), Docker healthcheck wiring, deploy-race guards.
  • CloudFormation / Caddy reverse-proxy hardening (security headers at the edge, TLS, BREACH).
  • AllowedHosts production pinning (a deployment-config concern).

Coverage & methodology

Each finding was produced by a dimension-specialist reviewer agent and then independently re-verified by one or more adversarial verifier agents that re-read the cited code and defaulted to rejection; only findings that survived appear here. Verdicts: CONFIRMED = all verifiers agreed the defect is real; PLAUSIBLE = verifiers split. The review fanned out across 24 dimension×module cells and looped for 3 round(s); High/Critical findings used a 2-verifier panel, others a single adversarial verifier.

Coverage caveat: the review ran the maximum of 3 rounds but did not reach the "two consecutive dry rounds" convergence target — the final round still surfaced 100 new candidate findings. The 240 findings below are therefore a thorough but non-exhaustive sample; further rounds would likely surface more, predominantly in the Low/Medium tiers.

Contents — findings by severity

High (38)
  • Mobile navigation drawer claims role=dialog/aria-modal but has no focus trap, no Escape, and stays keyboard/SR-reachable while closed src/Web/src/app/features/shell/mobile-sidebar/mobile-sidebar.ts:29
  • GeminiService is a 6955-line God class fronting every AI feature in the app src/Api/Services/GeminiService.cs:53
  • Api is a single 3438-line God service spanning ~35 unrelated domains src/Web/src/app/core/api.ts:60
  • users-mobile re-implements the entire sensitive permission-grant + email-reveal UI instead of sharing it with desktop users src/Web/src/app/features/users-mobile/users-mobile.page.ts:1342
  • Deploy workflow runs on push to main with no dependency on the CI test job (untested code ships to production) .github/workflows/deploy.yml:16
  • Production images built, pushed and deployed as mutable ':latest' tags — no immutable digest, breaks rollback and enables race/stale pulls .github/workflows/deploy.yml:33
  • Deployed code (remote git checkout) can drift from the built images within a single run .github/workflows/deploy.yml:44
  • Offline queue permanently discards queued mutations on a 401/403 (expired or missing token) — optimistically-acked data is silently lost src/Web/src/app/core/offline-queue.ts:166
  • HealthSyncScheduler advances the day cursor BEFORE the pull, so any transient pull failure permanently skips (loses) that day of health data src/Api/Services/Health/HealthSyncScheduler.cs:137
  • Cycle PUT /day-log upsert has no unique-violation catch — concurrent same-day writes throw an unhandled 500 src/Api/Endpoints/CycleEndpoints.cs:193
  • TrackerStore.load() has no request-generation guard — stale response wins, can render another user's health day src/Web/src/app/core/tracker-store.ts:66
  • Offline interceptor queues PUT edits and DELETEs for tracker mutations, contradicting its own "append-only" safety invariant and creating replay data-corruption src/Web/src/app/core/offline.interceptor.ts:30
  • Deleting a user orphans all their email-keyed PII/health data (no FK, no cleanup) — GDPR/retention gap src/Api/Endpoints/UsersEndpoints.cs:201
  • ModelPricing rows are HasData-seeded with explicit ids 1–14, so the Postgres identity sequence is never advanced — the first ~14 runtime inserts via POST /api/pricing throw duplicate-PK (23505) src/Api/Data/UsageDbContext.cs:162
  • Health, GPS and other sensitive response bodies are captured into RequestLog and replayed to any activity.view holder (cross-permission PII leak) src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLoggingMiddleware.cs:36
  • RequestLoggingMiddleware captures REQUEST bodies of health/cycle/meds/location mutations verbatim (not just responses) src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLoggingMiddleware.cs:55
  • BodyExcluded allowlist is scoped only to cross-user EMAIL paths and structurally cannot protect same-user special-category PII (cycle/meds/GPS/vitals) src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLoggingMiddleware.cs:36
  • EC2 instance does not enforce IMDSv2, exposing role credentials (SSM secret + KMS decrypt) to SSRF deploy/cloudformation.yaml:85
  • Deploy role can run arbitrary root shell on the PII/health/payments host via AWS-RunShellScript deploy/cloudformation.yaml:150
  • AMI auto-resolves per deploy, so an 'idempotent' re-run can replace the instance and destroy all Postgres data deploy/cloudformation.yaml:25
  • No error reporting, alerting, or log aggregation is shipped anywhere — errors only reach one container's stdout src/Api/Infrastructure/GlobalExceptionHandler.cs:10
  • Container healthcheck and compose service_healthy gate probe a liveness stub that never checks the database; the real readiness check is wired to nothing src/Api/Endpoints/ApiEndpoints.cs:18
  • Production Postgres data lives only in a local Docker volume on one EC2 host with no backup/snapshot story deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml:5
  • Readiness check /health/ready is orphaned — container liveness gate probes DB-free /api/health, so the API reports healthy while Postgres is down src/Api/Program.cs:549
  • TokenProtector derives its at-rest AES key from Jwt:Key, so rotating the JWT signing key silently and irreversibly bricks every encrypted refresh token, surfaced only as indistinguishable AuthExpired src/Api/Services/TokenProtector.cs:15
  • No index on UsageRecord.TimestampUtc despite being the default sort + the ORDER BY for every capped/session/CSV path src/Api/Data/UsageDbContext.cs:128
  • UsageRecord.TimestampUtc — the DEFAULT records sort and every row-materializing path — has no index src/Api/Data/UsageDbContext.cs:128
  • No index on UsageRecord.TimestampUtc despite it being the DEFAULT sort for every records/CSV/Calendar/Heatmap/Stats query src/Api/Services/UsageQueries.cs:44
  • Precise GPS coordinates from /api/location responses are persisted into RequestLog and replayed to any activity.view holder (bypassing location.view-all) src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLoggingMiddleware.cs:36
  • Committed default EmailRevealKey "Starbucks" is the live PII-unmask gate in production (no prod override) src/Api/appsettings.json:42
  • Force-logout (SessionVersion bump) does not tear down an already-open SignalR hub connection src/Api/Hubs/ChatHub.cs:181
  • Precise GPS coordinates leak into request logs replayed to activity.view holders src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLoggingMiddleware.cs:36
  • Ingest key of a deleted or disabled user stays live — filter checks only hash + RevokedUtc, and delete/disable never revoke keys src/Api/Auth/IngestKeyFilter.cs:36
  • QuickAddClient re-sends X-Ingest-Key across auto-followed cross-host redirects (a second leak surface distinct from IngestClient) src/ReporterCore/QuickAddClient.cs:36
  • Frontend test coverage is effectively zero: one stale scaffold spec for an entire Angular SPA src/Web/src/app/app.spec.ts:4
  • CI never executes frontend tests — the web job only builds, so any spec (passing or failing) is unenforced .github/workflows/ci.yml:61
  • CI never runs the Angular test suite — the web job only builds; the configured vitest runner is unwired, so the entire SPA has zero enforced coverage .github/workflows/ci.yml:61
  • CI never executes any frontend test — the `web` job only builds, so the SPA has zero test enforcement .github/workflows/ci.yml:61
Medium (93)
  • Desktop tablists declare role=tab/aria-selected but omit the arrow-key roving-tabindex contract the mobile twins implement src/Web/src/app/features/meds/meds.page.ts:76
  • App-wide toaster puts aria-live on the per-toast element inserted with its text, so screen readers likely do not announce toasts src/Web/src/app/features/beta-ui/toast.ts:109
  • No route-change focus management: SPA navigation never moves focus to <main>, so keyboard/SR users are stranded and the new page is not announced src/Web/src/app/app.ts:503
  • Closed desktop responsive nav drawer (#mobile-nav) keeps ~25 links keyboard-focusable and screen-reader-exposed while invisible src/Web/src/app/app.scss:355
  • Home-page picker submenu is a radio group but exposes no role=menuitemradio/aria-checked; the current selection is conveyed only by an aria-hidden icon src/Web/src/app/app.html:689
  • Desktop period/window tablists (Wrapped, Insights) declare role=tablist/tab but omit the arrow-key roving-tabindex contract and tabpanel wiring src/Web/src/app/features/wrapped/wrapped.page.ts:44
  • TrackerEndpoints embeds business logic and data access directly in endpoint handlers (2522-line endpoint file) src/Api/Endpoints/TrackerEndpoints.cs:203
  • Display-timezone resolution is copy-pasted across four endpoint modules despite a shared resolver existing src/Api/Endpoints/AiEndpoints.cs:1977
  • Household membership/role authorization helpers are duplicated across family endpoint modules src/Api/Endpoints/FamilyRemindersTimersEndpoints.cs:350
  • Insights parsing/domain helpers are copy-pasted verbatim between desktop and mobile twins src/Web/src/app/features/insights-mobile/insights.page.ts:427
  • Mobile twins reach across feature boundaries into desktop feature folders for shared logic instead of a shared/core module src/Web/src/app/features/family-finance-mobile/family-finance-mobile.page.ts:33
  • Several page components exceed 1500-2200 lines, mixing markup, DTO shaping, and business logic src/Web/src/app/features/tracker/tracker.ts:238
  • StatsAsync CurrentStreakDays is computed from the tail of active dates without anchoring to today, so a lapsed user still shows an ongoing streak src/Api/Services/UsageQueries.cs:176
  • A user disabled (or whose chat permission is revoked) while holding a live ChatHub connection keeps receiving channel broadcasts, because group membership is bound only at connect time with no periodic re-check src/Api/Hubs/ChatHub.cs:39
  • Two parallel home-route lists: PageDef.home in the registry is dead code and has already drifted from HOME_OPTIONS src/Web/src/app/core/page-registry.ts:57
  • Generic file/util helpers are trapped inside the tracker feature and pulled cross-boundary by resume, resume-mobile, and family-calendar src/Web/src/app/features/resume-mobile/resume-mobile.page.ts:14
  • users-mobile re-implements the sensitive email-reveal/permission-grant UI (2087 lines — larger than the desktop original) and its docstring falsely claims emails are never revealed src/Web/src/app/features/users-mobile/users-mobile.page.ts:82
  • Open-signup default set can auto-grant privileged CONFIG-WRITE keys (settings.manage, sources.manage, pricing.manage, reporter.manage, notifications.manage, shares.manage) to every self-provisioning Google account src/Api/Auth/Permissions.cs:355
  • Command palette is a 4th hand-maintained page list that has already drifted — 12 real pages are unreachable via ⌘K src/Web/src/app/core/command-registry.ts:40
  • Mobile twins deep-link to the legacy /tracker-beta tree, sending mobile users to a parallel (non-canonical) rendering src/Web/src/app/features/insights-mobile/insights.page.ts:96
  • homeRoute() fallback scans a hand-picked 13-route subset, dumping users whose only permission is on an omitted page to /welcome src/Web/src/app/core/auth.ts:60
  • Six mobile/beta twins reach across feature boundaries into desktop feature folders for shared logic src/Web/src/app/features/family-finance-mobile/family-finance-mobile.page.ts:33
  • Both production container images run as root (no USER directive) src/Api/Dockerfile:18
  • No concurrency control on the Deploy workflow — overlapping runs can push :latest and roll out out-of-order .github/workflows/deploy.yml:16
  • workflow_dispatch always deploys origin/main HEAD regardless of the invoking ref .github/workflows/deploy.yml:44
  • Optional-secret fetch failures are silently swallowed and written as empty values, degrading auth on redeploy deploy/remote-deploy.sh:13
  • docker-compose plugin binary is curl-installed as root with no checksum or signature verification deploy/cloudformation.yaml:103
  • Developer's real secrets file (appsettings.Local.json) is not excluded by any .dockerignore, so a local API image build bakes live Google/JWT/Gemini/FatSecret/WorkoutX secrets into an image layer src/.dockerignore:2
  • ActivityEmitter.EmitOnceAsync at-most-once is a cross-scope check-then-insert with no unique index — concurrent duplicates slip through src/Api/Services/ActivityEmitter.cs:116
  • Every scheduler/forwarder BackgroundService runs on each API instance with no leader-election or DB advisory lock src/Api/Services/AgentScheduler.cs:44
  • A scheduled agent whose DeliverHourLocal falls inside its own quiet window is stamped-and-dropped every day and never fires src/Api/Services/AgentScheduler.cs:123
  • Security/signup Discord forwarding stalls permanently on a dead webhook or one poison audit entry (high-water mark never advances) src/Api/Services/NotificationBackgroundService.cs:165
  • MedicationLog dose 'upsert' is not backed by a unique index — concurrent taps create duplicate rows that double-count adherence src/Api/Endpoints/MedsEndpoints.cs:231
  • Client-supplied TakenAt/MeasuredAt are written to timestamptz columns without UTC-kind normalization (Npgsql throws on Unspecified/Local kind) src/Api/Endpoints/MedsEndpoints.cs:248
  • Health sleep/workout sync inserts the tracker row BEFORE its de-dup log, so a concurrent sync on a second API instance persists a duplicate SleepEntry/ExerciseEntry src/Api/Services/Health/HealthSyncMapper.cs:181
  • CompleteFinishedTimersAsync has no per-item try/catch — one NotifyFamily failure aborts the rest of the finished-timer batch after the timer was already stamped Done src/Api/Services/FamilyReminderService.cs:412
  • Identity auto/apply idempotency key ignores window START, so a second apply over a different window ending the same day is silently dropped and undercounts src/Api/Endpoints/IdentityEndpoints.cs:198
  • Poll re-vote does ExecuteDeleteAsync (auto-committed) then a separate SaveChanges Add, with no wrapping transaction — a failure between them permanently loses the caller's prior votes with nothing re-added src/Api/Endpoints/FamilyPollsEndpoints.cs:166
  • Location admin map fetches only the 2000 globally-most-recent fixes then groups by user, so users are silently omitted from oversight when a few users dominate recent activity src/Api/Endpoints/LocationEndpoints.cs:170
  • ChatRealtime.loadHistory documents "replaces the channel's message list" on initial open but actually merges, so tombstoned/removed messages and stale bodies survive a channel re-open src/Web/src/app/core/chat-realtime.ts:491
  • No optimistic-concurrency token on any entity; money read-modify-write drops updates src/Api/Data/UsageDbContext.cs:1087
  • UsageRecord cascades from IngestedFile via a required FK — purging a file destroys its billing rows src/Api/Data/UsageDbContext.cs:140
  • HouseholdMember.UserId has no FK to AppUser — deleting a user leaves a dangling membership row src/Api/Data/Entities/HouseholdMember.cs:18
  • FinanceAccount find-or-create backstop unique index is defeated by NULL Institution (code stores null, not the documented "") src/Api/Data/UsageDbContext.cs:996
  • The 'one OVERALL budget per household' invariant is unenforced: the overall budget is stored with Category = NULL, and the (HouseholdId, Category) unique index treats each NULL as distinct in Postgres, so duplicate overall budgets can be created and the endpoint's only guard never fires src/Api/Data/UsageDbContext.cs:1084
  • LogRedaction only masks a fixed set of secret KEY NAMES, so any PII value (email, coordinates, health text, names) in a body is stored verbatim src/Api/Infrastructure/LogRedaction.cs:16
  • BodyExcluded (email-bearing admin paths) is a denylist that must be manually kept in lockstep with every endpoint that emits emails; endpoints that return raw emails outside this list would be captured and replayed to activity.view holders src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLoggingMiddleware.cs:36
  • All backend NuGet packages use floating 9.0.* versions with no lock file — non-reproducible, mutable-at-restore supply chain src/Api/Ccusage.Api.csproj:12
  • ReporterCore (and the whole Agent/Reporter chain) also floats every dependency on 9.0.* with no lock file — a distinct, PII/GPS-collecting binary the API-only finding missed src/ReporterCore/Ccusage.Reporter.Core.csproj:13
  • CI restores floating NuGet ranges at build time with no lock file or --locked-mode, mutating the JWT-auth supply chain per-run .github/workflows/ci.yml:21
  • Desktop Agent (PII/GPS binary with floating System.Management) is never restored, built, or tested in any CI/CD pipeline src/Agent/UsageIq.Agent.csproj:45
  • Single-instance Postgres with no backups or EBS snapshot policy; volume deleted on termination deploy/cloudformation.yaml:95
  • Caddy enables gzip response compression over HTTPS on auth/secret-bearing responses (BREACH exposure) deploy/Caddyfile:7
  • ECR repositories are tag-mutable and deploy pins :latest, allowing silent image overwrite with no provenance deploy/cloudformation.yaml:30
  • Startup database migration is unguarded, so any transient DB blip or bad migration crash-loops the only API instance src/Api/Program.cs:410
  • No metrics of any kind, and the two observability queues silently drop entries with no counter src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLogQueue.cs:13
  • JWT signing key and DB password are generated once and explicitly never rotated; no rotation mechanism exists deploy/provision.sh:11
  • Entire wearable/health-sync action surface (/api/health/connect, /sync-now, /disconnect) is silently excluded from the RequestLog audit trail src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLoggingMiddleware.cs:25
  • Health sync stamps LastSyncUtc on a fully-failed pull, delaying recovery by the full 6h cadence instead of retrying next tick src/Api/Services/Health/HealthSyncScheduler.cs:156
  • RecordsAsync sorts by CostUsd/InputTokens/OutputTokens with no supporting index src/Api/Services/UsageQueries.cs:557
  • Calendar/Heatmap/Stats materialize up to 2,000,000 UsageRecord rows into process memory per request src/Api/Services/UsageQueries.cs:44
  • No list virtualization anywhere in the app; large lists render every row into the DOM src/Web/src/app/features/chat/chat.html:203
  • Anonymous /api/public/built-with invokes the row-materializing StatsAsync over the owner's (largest) lifetime dataset with no date filter and only header-based caching src/Api/Endpoints/PublicEndpoints.cs:52
  • Self habit-stats (ComputeUserStatsAsync) issues a per-habit nested query fan-out on every habit-page load src/Api/Endpoints/HabitEndpoints.cs:429
  • Root shell writes a signal on every pointermove/scroll/wheel, scheduling a full CheckAlways change-detection pass over the entire desktop tree src/Web/src/app/app.ts:854
  • ChatRealtime message cache grows unbounded for the whole session and is re-sorted on every read src/Web/src/app/core/chat-realtime.ts:491
  • Insights charts bind a method (scatterOption) in the template, rebuilding a fresh ECharts option every change-detection pass and forcing a full canvas re-render src/Web/src/app/features/insights/insights.page.ts:164
  • Committed default email-reveal key "Starbucks" unmasks real user emails src/Api/appsettings.json:42
  • Cycle overlay (predicted period/fertile spans) is visible to any household member holding only family.use — including child logins src/Api/Endpoints/CycleOverlayEndpoints.cs:106
  • Web Push endpoint is user-controlled with no scheme/host validation or redirect suppression (SSRF surface) src/Api/Services/WebPushSender.cs:147
  • App JWT (bearer + permissions) persisted in localStorage — readable by any XSS src/Web/src/app/core/auth.ts:151
  • No Content-Security-Policy on the SPA — no defense-in-depth against XSS/token theft src/Web/nginx.conf:13
  • Permissions-Policy disables geolocation/microphone/camera for the app's own origin, breaking GPS and voice features src/Web/nginx.conf:13
  • activity.view and users.view are defaultable, so open sign-up can auto-grant request-log and user-list/audit access src/Api/Auth/Permissions.cs:355
  • Only the "discord" HttpClient suppresses auto-redirects; every secret-bearing outbound client (Gemini, WorkoutX, Fitbit, Google, FatSecret, USDA, weather, geocoders, webpush) follows redirects, so a 3xx from (or a MITM of) the fixed upstream replays the API key to an attacker host src/Api/Program.cs:80
  • IpGeoService calls ip-api.com over plaintext http:// — the machine's public IP and the resolved coarse location travel unencrypted and can be silently tampered by an on-path attacker src/Api/Program.cs:174
  • Usage records.csv export writes attacker-controlled Model/Project/branch cells without formula-injection neutralization src/Api/Services/UsageQueries.cs:618
  • Disabling a user does not invalidate their live JWT; presence endpoints (auth-only, no IsEnabled re-check) stay reachable src/Api/Endpoints/UsersEndpoints.cs:177
  • Cycle overlay surfaces a CHILD member's predicted menstrual/fertile spans household-wide with a single self-serve toggle and no age/consent gate src/Api/Endpoints/CycleOverlayEndpoints.cs:54
  • Live GPS keeps broadcasting into an archived conversation via UPDATE/EXTEND (archived-channel check absent on the whole live-share lifecycle, not just START) src/Api/Services/ChatLocationShareService.cs:88
  • Re-opening a moderator-archived DM throws an unhandled 500 (First() over an empty archived-filtered result) src/Api/Endpoints/ChatEndpoints.cs:109
  • Disabled user keeps full API access on RequireAuthorization()-only endpoints (Presence, personal Discord) — disable is not enforced server-side src/Api/Program.cs:314
  • PresenceMiddleware marks a disabled user 'online' and touches presence on every authenticated request src/Api/Infrastructure/PresenceMiddleware.cs:22
  • Login-history endpoint exposes every user's IP, user-agent and device fingerprint under users.view — a DEFAULTABLE permission src/Api/Endpoints/UsersEndpoints.cs:132
  • TokenProtector uses one global key with no per-context associated data — all encrypted secrets are cross-context interchangeable src/Api/Services/TokenProtector.cs:30
  • Ingest key passed as a plaintext command-line argument (visible to other processes/users) Run-UsageIqReporter.ps1:81
  • reporter.key written as plaintext with no OS-level access control or encryption src/ReporterCore/ReporterConfig.cs:137
  • Log-tree walk follows directory junctions/symlinks with no cycle or reparse-point guard (infinite scan + read-escape outside the intended root) src/ReporterCore/LogScanner.cs:186
  • X-Ingest-Key is a custom header that HttpClient re-sends across auto-followed cross-host redirects, leaking the secret to a redirect target src/ReporterCore/IngestClient.cs:40
  • The sole frontend spec asserts against a deleted scaffold ('Hello, ccusage-web') and would fail if ever run src/Web/src/app/app.html:21
  • Unverified-Google-email rejection path (GoogleAuthService line 47) has zero test coverage — the fake validator hardcodes EmailVerified=true tests/Ccusage.Api.Tests/Integration/FakeGoogleTokenValidator.cs:23
  • No test proves an X-Forwarded-For from an UNTRUSTED (public) peer is ignored — the anti-spoofing property of the IP-trust config is unverified tests/Ccusage.Api.Tests/Integration/ForwardedHeadersIntegrationTests.cs:84
  • The sole frontend spec asserts a deleted scaffold title and would fail the moment the runner works src/Web/src/app/app.spec.ts:21

  • Standalone 'Add item' inputs rely on placeholder as their only name src/Web/src/app/features/family/lists.html:338

  • models.ts is a single 6675-line, 570-export type barrel src/Web/src/app/core/models.ts:1
  • CalendarAsync mixes an UNCAPPED day aggregate with a CAPPED per-row session pull, so under the 2M-row guard the same response reports complete cost/message totals but truncated sessions/active-minutes for the oldest days src/Api/Services/UsageQueries.cs:55
  • CSV export field quoting omits bare carriage return, so a field containing \r can corrupt row boundaries in strict CSV readers src/Api/Services/UsageQueries.cs:619
  • OptimisticFamily is a self-admitted hand-copy of OptimisticTracker's bump-then-reconcile pattern src/Web/src/app/features/family-beta/state/optimistic-family.ts:8
  • Insights domain metadata (KIND_META blurbs/labels) is copy-pasted verbatim between the desktop and mobile twins and can silently drift src/Web/src/app/features/insights-mobile/insights.page.ts:451
  • Two incompatible error envelopes across endpoints — RFC7807 ProblemDetails vs ad-hoc { message } JSON src/Api/Endpoints/BillEndpoints.cs:220
  • WebPushSender fans out to ALL of a recipient's push subscriptions in a sequential awaited loop with no per-recipient subscription cap src/Api/Services/WebPushSender.cs:143
  • Two unit-conversion factor tables feeding health goal math have already drifted (LB_PER_KG and oz differ) src/Web/src/app/features/tracker/units.ts:4
  • PageDef.home is declared and populated on ~35 pages but never read anywhere — dead metadata drifting from HOME_OPTIONS src/Web/src/app/core/page-registry.ts:57
  • Insights domain metadata (KIND_META labels/blurbs) copy-pasted verbatim between desktop and mobile twins src/Web/src/app/features/insights-mobile/insights.page.ts:451
  • Web (nginx) production image has no HEALTHCHECK, yet prod compose gates other startup on service health src/Web/Dockerfile:9
  • All GitHub Actions (including AWS OIDC/credential actions) are pinned to mutable major-version tags, not commit SHAs .github/workflows/deploy.yml:20
  • Schema migrations auto-apply at API startup on every deploy with no gate, backup, or ordering barrier deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml:20
  • Decrypted secrets are written to a persistent .env file and passed as container environment on a shared root host deploy/remote-deploy.sh:40
  • Caddy (the internet-facing TLS terminator) only 'depends_on: web' with no health condition and web has no healthcheck, so Caddy can start proxying before the SPA is ready deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml:53
  • MachineGeoBackfill OrderBy(GeoUpdatedUtc) claims 'nulls first' but Postgres sorts NULLS LAST ascending — never-resolved machines can be starved src/Api/Services/MachineGeoBackfillService.cs:61
  • DiscordForwarder per-user TokenBucket dictionary is never evicted — grows with distinct recipients for the process lifetime src/Api/Services/DiscordForwarder.cs:85
  • RequestLogWriter/AiUsageLogWriter Task.Delay in the catch-all is unguarded — shutdown cancellation escapes as an uncaught OperationCanceledException src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLogWriter.cs:56
  • Anonymous bill-item claim is a check-then-act with no concurrency guard (lost update) src/Api/Endpoints/BillEndpoints.cs:304
  • FamilyReminderService reminder/timer loops lack per-item try/catch: one NotifyFamily failure aborts the rest of the tick and permanently loses the already-stamped occurrence's notification src/Api/Services/FamilyReminderService.cs:343
  • 75 Hard cheat-days: removals are committed before the add-cap validation, so a rejected request still mutates state src/Api/Endpoints/HardChallengeEndpoints.cs:434
  • NotificationBell.surfaced Set grows unbounded for the lifetime of the app shell src/Web/src/app/features/notifications/notification-bell.ts:95
  • ChatRealtime.refreshChannels() unconditionally replaces the channel list, dropping optimistic ChannelAdded/unread updates that raced in src/Web/src/app/core/chat-realtime.ts:456
  • Fitbit token/API reads use the request CancellationToken but the rotated-refresh-token persistence is not atomic with the mint, so a cancel between refresh and re-store loses the rotated token and bricks the connection src/Api/Services/Health/FitbitHealthProvider.cs:330
  • Weekly recap guard is not advanced when the user's preference row vanishes between send and re-load, allowing a duplicate Discord recap on the next tick src/Api/Services/NotificationBackgroundService.cs:136
  • AI-usage 'to' filter treats a date-only value as an inclusive midnight bound, silently excluding same-day rows src/Api/Endpoints/ObservabilityEndpoints.cs:106
  • ShareAccess 'exactly one of the two FKs set' invariant is unenforced (no CHECK constraint) src/Api/Data/UsageDbContext.cs:340
  • HardChallengeDayTask.ChallengeId is a bare integer column with no foreign key, despite the entity doc asserting it is 'cascade-deleted with the owning challenge' src/Api/Data/UsageDbContext.cs:1418
  • GlobalExceptionHandler surfaces traceId to the client but never writes it into the log line, breaking client-to-log correlation src/Api/Infrastructure/GlobalExceptionHandler.cs:10
  • CapturingResponseStream.GetCapturedText decodes an arbitrary byte-truncated buffer, so redaction can silently miss a secret split across the 16KB capture boundary and can emit a partial value src/Api/Infrastructure/CapturingResponseStream.cs:19
  • GlobalExceptionHandler writes the 500 response after RequestLoggingMiddleware has already swapped the response body, but its generic body is fine — the real gap is that the exception message/stack is only ever emitted to one container's stdout with no correlation to the client traceId src/Api/Infrastructure/GlobalExceptionHandler.cs:10
  • EmailsRevealed claims a constant-time compare but FixedTimeEquals over unequal-length UTF-8 byte arrays short-circuits, leaking the configured reveal-key length via timing src/Api/Endpoints/UsersEndpoints.cs:300
  • WebPush 1.0.13 is an unmaintained package on the push-notification (auth-adjacent crypto) path src/Api/Ccusage.Api.csproj:20
  • Unpinned .NET SDK toolchain (9.0.x, no global.json) compounds the floating-package non-reproducibility .github/workflows/ci.yml:20
  • @angular/animations declared as a direct dependency but never imported directly anywhere in source src/Web/package.json:15
  • Deploy role can read SSM command results across all instances in the account deploy/cloudformation.yaml:152
  • Security group defines no egress rules, leaving default allow-all outbound from a PII/health/payments host deploy/cloudformation.yaml:44
  • App connects to Postgres as the superuser 'ccusage', and remote-deploy hardcodes an admin-email fallback deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml:27
  • Caddy ACME account/cert data and config live only on the single ephemeral host with no persistence guarantee deploy/cloudformation.yaml:95
  • All stateful Docker volumes share the single DeleteOnTermination root volume with no separate device deploy/cloudformation.yaml:95
  • No cross-service correlation/request id is propagated or logged; the client-facing traceId is not carried into logs src/Api/Infrastructure/GlobalExceptionHandler.cs:10
  • web container hard-depends on api service_healthy, but the health signal is DB-free liveness — a DB outage that 500s the API still shows web 'up', masking the real failure deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml:48
  • Only Jwt:Key is validated at startup; auth-critical Google:ClientId and the DB connection string are never validated, so misconfiguration degrades to silent partial outages rather than a fast fail src/Api/Program.cs:254
  • ip-api plaintext response parsed with an uncapped JsonDocument.ParseAsync — a MITM'd keyless upstream can force unbounded allocation on the geo path src/Api/Services/IpGeoService.cs:55
  • Observability-log prune counter is reset before the prune runs, so a transient DB failure lets RequestLog/AiUsageLog grow well past MaxRows before the next prune is attempted src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLogWriter.cs:45
  • ProjectsAsync/MachinesAsync/ModelsAsync run unfiltered full-table GROUP BY over all UsageRecords src/Api/Services/UsageQueries.cs:621
  • Chat runs an unconditional 1-second heartbeat timer that ticks even when no live-location share is active src/Web/src/app/features/chat/chat.ts:428
  • /api/ai-usage runs six-plus full-window aggregate scans with GROUP BY / filters on unindexed Feature, Outcome and Model columns src/Api/Endpoints/ObservabilityEndpoints.cs:133
  • Activity log page renders up to 300 table rows with no virtualization src/Web/src/app/features/logs/logs.ts:65
  • Chat groups() rebuilds all message-group objects on every message-signal change, defeating @for identity src/Web/src/app/features/chat/chat.ts:191
  • CalendarAsync mixes an UNCAPPED DB aggregate with a CAPPED in-memory sessionization, diverging silently at the cap boundary src/Api/Services/UsageQueries.cs:66
  • DisplayTzAsync re-queries AppConfig on every Heatmap/Stats call instead of using a cached config value src/Api/Services/UsageQueries.cs:102
  • Desktop shell template invokes un-memoized O(n) permission checks (each also re-parsing the JWT expiry Date) 50+ times per change-detection pass, amplified by the CheckAlways root that dirties on every pointer move src/Web/src/app/app.html:79
  • Every mounted chart fully re-renders (setOption notMerge=true) on any color-scheme change because the render effect depends on theme.scheme() src/Web/src/app/shared/chart.ts:252
  • Open sign-up defaults ON with no hosted-domain restriction — any Google account self-provisions src/Api/Program.cs:427
  • XFF trust (ForwardLimit=null + broad private KnownNetworks) is safe only while Kestrel stays unpublished src/Api/Program.cs:343
  • Location share can be started into an archived conversation (START skips the archived-channel check that message-send enforces) src/Api/Endpoints/ChatLocationShareEndpoints.cs:43
  • users.view and activity.view are defaultable, so open-signup defaults can be configured to auto-grant the user list, audit log, and PII-bearing request logs to every new account src/Api/Auth/Permissions.cs:355
  • POST /api/tracker/food/from-meal writes food entries onto another household member's private tracker src/Api/Endpoints/TrackerEndpoints.cs:226
  • Habit leaderboard viewall path enumerates every enabled user with per-user N+1 stat computation (no roster cap) src/Api/Endpoints/HabitEndpoints.cs:338
  • Anonymous bill-share item claim is a check-then-set with no uniqueness guard (double-claim race) src/Api/Endpoints/BillEndpoints.cs:304

  • AllowedHosts set to wildcard "*" (no Host-header allowlist) src/Api/appsettings.json:72
  • Config-write permissions (settings/sources/reporter/notifications manage) remain defaultable, so open-signup defaults can auto-grant deployment-wide config control src/Api/Auth/Permissions.cs:356
  • MedicationLog has no unique constraint, so the dose-log upsert and "repeat yesterday" idempotency can be defeated by concurrent writes src/Api/Data/UsageDbContext.cs:1267
  • Open-DM endpoint distinguishes 'user exists+enabled' (403) from 'nonexistent/disabled' (400), enabling instance-wide AppUser id enumeration by any chat user src/Api/Endpoints/ChatEndpoints.cs:94
  • UpdatePosition/Extend re-broadcast a live location pin into a conversation after it is archived (no archived-channel guard on the sharer-owned mutation paths) src/Api/Services/ChatLocationShareService.cs:81
  • Removing a chat contact edge does not revoke the ability to keep messaging in a pre-existing DM (send checks membership only, not current contact status) src/Api/Endpoints/ChatEndpoints.cs:219
  • Client-controlled source="login" bypasses the LocationEnabled opt-in gate on the server src/Api/Endpoints/LocationEndpoints.cs:55
  • HardChallenge PUT /day accepts an unbounded, out-of-window date with no range check src/Api/Endpoints/HardChallengeEndpoints.cs:214
  • WorkoutX GIF proxy streams the upstream-chosen Content-Type back to the browser verbatim with no image-type allowlist and no X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff (no global security-header middleware exists) src/Api/Endpoints/TrackerEndpoints.cs:1136
  • OAuth token/refresh responses are read via ReadAsByteArrayAsync with no size cap in the Fitbit and Google token exchanges, so a compromised/MITM'd token endpoint can force an unbounded allocation src/Api/Services/Health/FitbitHealthProvider.cs:376
  • My Data ZIP export Csv() helper is also formula-injection-unsafe across all free-text columns src/Api/Services/MyDataExportService.cs:412
  • Markdown link sanitizer allows protocol-relative (//host) external links, defeating the intended same-origin/allowed-scheme restriction src/Web/src/app/features/family/markdown.ts:28
  • Post-login navigation trusts an attacker-supplied ?returnUrl verbatim without validating it is an in-app path src/Web/src/app/features/signin/signin.ts:69
  • POST /api/meds/{id}/log accepts any ScheduledSlot in [0,11] without validating it against the medication's actual TimesPerDay/schedule src/Api/Endpoints/MedsEndpoints.cs:229
  • POST /api/meds/doses/repeat copies Missed/Skipped statuses verbatim onto the target day, fabricating adherence history the user never recorded for that day src/Api/Endpoints/MedsEndpoints.cs:322
  • Pact progress exposes per-member activity counts to invited-but-not-joined users src/Api/Endpoints/PactEndpoints.cs:238
  • Request-log body redaction/exclusion is path-allowlisted, so newly added email/secret-bearing routes silently leak into the Activity log src/Api/Infrastructure/RequestLoggingMiddleware.cs:36
  • 75-Hard day confession can never be cleared and ConfessionsUsed only ever increments (monotonic mutation) src/Api/Endpoints/HardChallengeEndpoints.cs:1225
  • IpGeoService calls ip-api.com over cleartext http:// with redirects enabled src/Api/Program.cs:174
  • WorkoutX GIF proxy reads unbounded upstream bytes and echoes the upstream Content-Type verbatim to the browser src/Api/Services/WorkoutXService.cs:129
  • Google Calendar OAuth token exchange reads the response body with no size cap (unbounded allocation on a compromised/MITM'd token endpoint) src/Api/Services/GoogleCalendarService.cs:501
  • Persisted auth session is JSON.parse-cast to AuthSession with no shape validation, so any localStorage tamper/XSS forges identity, permissions, and expiry the client trusts src/Web/src/app/core/auth.ts:179
  • A single newline-free giant file is loaded into one string by ReadLine (potential OOM) src/ReporterCore/LogScanner.cs:164
  • Family quick-add path sends the ingest key with no http/cleartext guard, unlike every other send path src/Agent/Views/QuickAddWindow.xaml.cs:66
  • Atomic-write temp files leave the plaintext key/config under a second filename that key-management flows ignore src/ReporterCore/ReporterConfig.cs:140
  • LogScanner skip-list uses backslash-delimited segments that never match on a non-Windows reporter, so node_modules/.tmp trees are scanned and parsed src/ReporterCore/LogScanner.cs:51
  • Deferred GPS rebuild runs as fire-and-forget Task that can stop/rebuild the engine concurrently with a Settings-triggered Reload, and swallows its own faults src/Agent/Services/AgentController.cs:228
  • Reveal toggle leaves the plaintext ingest key in the KeyPlain TextBox even after the user switches back to masked mode src/Agent/Views/SettingsWindow.xaml.cs:61

  • Auth-log polling helper (WaitForLog) uses a fixed 150ms Task.Delay loop with a hard ~7.5s ceiling — mild flake risk under CI load tests/Ccusage.Api.Tests/Integration/AuthIntegrationTests.cs:573
  • Double-post guard relies on a fixed 300ms sleep to 'give a second enqueue a chance to land' — a timing race that can pass while a real double-post exists tests/Ccusage.Api.Tests/Integration/AutomationsTests.cs:492
  • 'Exactly one Discord post' is asserted after a fixed 300 ms sleep — a slow second enqueue under CI load races the assertion tests/Ccusage.Api.Tests/Integration/AutomationsTests.cs:492
  • Non-delivery is proven by a fixed 2 s wait; slow SignalR delivery under load can flip a correct run red tests/Ccusage.Api.Tests/Integration/ChatIntegrationTests.cs:695

High severity 38

High Accessibility & Robustness Frontend accessibility CONFIRMED conf: high 2 verifiers

Mobile navigation drawer claims role=dialog/aria-modal but has no focus trap, no Escape, and stays keyboard/SR-reachable while closed

src/Web/src/app/features/shell/mobile-sidebar/mobile-sidebar.ts:29-149 Frontend accessibility
Evidence
<div class="scrim" [class.open]="nav.sidebarOpen()" (click)="nav.close()" aria-hidden="true"></div>
<aside class="drawer" [class.open]="nav.sidebarOpen()" role="dialog" aria-modal="true"
       aria-label="Navigation menu">
...
.drawer { position: fixed; ... display: flex; transform: translateX(-104%); transition: transform 280ms ...; }
.drawer.open { transform: translateX(0); }
Why it matters (enterprise impact)

The primary mobile navigation is the app's most-used surface. The drawer is permanently in the DOM (no @if / inert / aria-hidden toggle) and is only slid off-screen with translateX when closed, so every nav link and the Sign-out button remain in the Tab order and screen-reader virtual cursor even while the drawer is visually hidden — keyboard and SR users tab into invisible off-screen controls (WCAG 2.4.3 / 1.3.2). When open, aria-modal="true" is a lie: core/mobile-nav.ts is a bare signal with no Escape handler, no Tab focus trap, and no focus-move-into-drawer; the scrim is aria-hidden so keyboard users cannot dismiss it. Focus can escape behind the modal onto the page underneath, defeating the modal contract that the beta bottom-sheet and dialogA11y directive elsewhere honor.

Independent verifier — confirmation
mobile-sidebar.ts:27-30 `<div class="scrim" ... aria-hidden="true"></div>` / `<aside class="drawer" [class.open]="nav.sidebarOpen()" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="Navigation menu">` — no @if/inert/aria-hidden toggle; closed only via mobile-sidebar.ts:144 `transform: translateX(-104%);`. mobile-nav.ts:11-24 MobileNavService is a bare signal (open/close/toggle only) — no Escape, no focus trap. dialog-a11y.directive.ts exists and supplies the missing focus-trap+Escape quartet but is NOT applied to the drawer.

Verified against the actual code. The drawer <aside> (line 29) declares role=dialog + aria-modal=true but has no @if/inert/aria-hidden — it is mounted unconditionally in app.html:601-603 (gated only by shellMode==='mobile') and hidden solely by transform: translateX(-104%) (line 144). An off-screen-via-transform element keeps all its <a routerLink> nav links and <button> Sign out/Switch-to-desktop controls in the Tab order and SR virtual cursor while closed (WCAG 2.4.3/1.3.2). When open, aria-modal is unbacked: MobileNavService (mobile-nav.ts) is a bare signal with no Escape/focus-trap/focus-move, the component class has no HostListener or keydown/lifecycle focus logic, and the scrim is aria-hidden so keyboard users can't dismiss it. The codebase's own dialogA11y directive (dialog-a11y.directive.ts) provides exactly this missing focus-trap+Escape+focus-restore contract and is applied to other role=dialog overlays (meds/habits/finance) but is absent here — confirming a real gap against the app's own standard. High severity is justified: this is the primary mobile nav on an auth/PII/health-data app. Line adjusted to 29 (the aria-modal declaration); the cited 27-149 range correctly spans the template + CSS evidence.

Remediation (approach)

Gate the entire drawer with @if(nav.sidebarOpen()) (or apply the shared dialogA11y directive / bottom-sheet primitive) so it leaves the DOM when closed; alternatively, when closed set display:none/visibility:hidden AND mark the aside inert so its controls leave the tab order. When open, move focus into the drawer, trap Tab/Shift+Tab within it, wire Escape to nav.close(), restore focus to the hamburger trigger on close, and mark the rest of the page inert. Reuse the existing DialogA11yDirective which already implements this quartet.

High Architecture & Maintainability Backend architecture & debt CONFIRMED conf: high 2 verifiers

GeminiService is a 6955-line God class fronting every AI feature in the app

src/Api/Services/GeminiService.cs:53-60 God classes
Evidence
public sealed class GeminiService(
    IHttpClientFactory httpFactory,
    IOptions<GeminiOptions> options,
    IMemoryCache cache,
    ILogger<GeminiService> logger,
    IHttpContextAccessor? httpContextAccessor = null,
    Ccusage.Api.Infrastructure.AiUsageLogQueue? aiUsageQueue = null)
Why it matters (enterprise impact)

A single sealed class of 6955 lines with ~180 methods (80+ public async feature methods) owns macro estimation, meal/photo/label parsing, workout/goal/hydration/sleep coaching, chore balancing, calendar scheduling, poll generation, finance summaries, recipes, and more. Every new AI capability across unrelated domains (food, family, finance) bolts another prompt-builder + JSON parser onto the same file, which (a) guarantees constant merge contention on one file, (b) makes any single feature impossible to unit-test or reason about in isolation, and (c) forces every AI-touching endpoint to depend on one enormous surface. This is the highest-coupling type in the backend.

Independent verifier — confirmation
src/Api/Services/GeminiService.cs:53-60 `public sealed class GeminiService( IHttpClientFactory httpFactory, IOptions<GeminiOptions> options, IMemoryCache cache, ILogger<GeminiService> logger, IHttpContextAccessor? httpContextAccessor = null, Ccusage.Api.Infrastructure.AiUsageLogQueue? aiUsageQueue = null) {` — file is 6955 lines; 80 public async Task-returning feature methods spanning unrelated domains, e.g. EstimateMacrosAsync/PhotoMealAsync/ReadLabelAsync (food), SuggestWorkoutAsync/HydrationSuggestAsync/SleepInsightAsync (fitness), BalanceChoresAsync/ScheduleEventsAsync/PollOptionsAsync (family), FinanceSummaryAsync/ClassifyTransactionsAsync/MoneyCoachAsync (finance), ParseResumeFileAsync/TailorResumeAsync/GenerateCoverLetterAsync (resume), SummarizeChatAsync/ComposeChatAsync (chat).

Verified directly. wc -l reports exactly 6955 lines. The sealed-class primary constructor at lines 53-60 matches the quoted evidence verbatim. Grep confirms 80 public async Task/ValueTask methods (the ~180 total methods includes ~212 method declarations counting private prompt-builders/parsers). Sampling the 80 public method names shows they span at least six unrelated business domains (food macros, fitness/coaching, chore/family scheduling, finance/transactions, resume generation, chat) — each bolting a prompt-builder + JSON parser onto the same file. This is an unambiguous God class: the single highest-coupling type in the backend, on which every AI-touching endpoint depends. High severity is justified: it is a genuine maintainability/testability/merge-contention hazard, not merely a large-but-cohesive file. Remediation (prose): split by bounded context into focused services (e.g. NutritionAiService, FitnessAiService, FamilyAiService, FinanceAiService, ResumeAiService, ChatAiService), extracting the shared Gemini HTTP/caching/clamping/usage-logging plumbing into a thin GeminiClient/GeminiInvoker that each domain service composes, so new capabilities extend an isolated, unit-testable seam rather than this one surface.

Remediation (approach)

Split GeminiService along feature/domain seams into cohesive collaborators (e.g. a nutrition-AI service, a family/planning-AI service, a finance-AI service, a coaching-AI service), each depending on a thin shared GeminiClient that owns the HTTP call, key handling, caching, clamping, and usage-logging plumbing. Keep the injection-guard/clamp discipline in the shared client so it is not re-implemented per service. Register the smaller services in DI and have each endpoint module depend only on the one it needs.

High Architecture & Maintainability Frontend architecture & duplication CONFIRMED conf: high 2 verifiers

Api is a single 3438-line God service spanning ~35 unrelated domains

src/Web/src/app/core/api.ts:60-70 Core-service coupling / God service
Evidence
@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })
export class Api {
  private http = inject(HttpClient);
  private readonly base = '/api';
  private filterParams(f: UsageFilter): HttpParams {
Why it matters (enterprise impact)

One injectable holds 449 methods and 446 HttpClient calls covering fleet, bills, chat, location/GPS, users/permissions, tracker/health, meds, cycle (health data), family finance/payments, resume, search, push, etc. (region comments at lines 102-3335). Every component that needs any one endpoint injects the whole surface, so change-blast-radius is enormous, tree-shaking is defeated (the entire class loads with the first feature), merge conflicts concentrate here, and there is no domain boundary or ownership. For an app handling auth, PII, health and payment endpoints this concentration also makes security review of any single domain's client calls impractical.

Independent verifier — confirmation
src/Web/src/app/core/api.ts:59-62
@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })
export class Api {
  private http = inject(HttpClient);
  private readonly base = '/api';
--- wc -l api.ts = 3438; grep -c "this.http." = 446 ; region headers span lines 102 (Fleet) → 3335 (Web Push), 60+ distinct domains incl. bills, chat, GPS, users/permissions, tracker/health, cycle calendar, family finance/budgets/net-worth, resume, search, wearable health sync.

Verified by reading the file and measuring it. A single root-provided injectable class Api spans 3438 lines with 446 HttpClient calls (~435 Observable methods) across 60+ region-commented domains (fleet, bills, chat, location/GPS, users/permissions, tracker/health, cycle calendar, family finance incl. budgets/net-worth/payments, resume, search, push, wearable health sync). Every component injecting Api pulls the entire surface; change blast-radius, merge-conflict concentration, and absence of any domain boundary/ownership are real for an app handling auth/PII/health/payments. This is a legitimate architecture/maintainability High. Location, severity, and scale all check out. Only nuance: the finding's 'tree-shaking is defeated' sub-claim is weak (per-method tree-shaking is impossible regardless of splitting), but the God-service defect stands independently of it. Kept severity High; anchored line to the class declaration (60).

Remediation (approach)

Split Api into per-domain injectable clients (e.g. FleetApi, BillsApi, ChatApi, LocationApi, UsersApi, TrackerApi, FamilyFinanceApi, HealthApi) that each own their own endpoint group, sharing a small private HTTP/params helper. Keep them providedIn:'root' so lazy features pull only the client they use. Migrate features to inject the narrow client rather than the mega-Api. The existing region comments already delineate natural seams to cut along.

High Architecture & Maintainability Frontend architecture & duplication CONFIRMED conf: high 2 verifiers

users-mobile re-implements the entire sensitive permission-grant + email-reveal UI instead of sharing it with desktop users

src/Web/src/app/features/users-mobile/users-mobile.page.ts:1342-1394 Duplication between desktop and mobile twins
Evidence
toggleEmails(): void {
    if (this.emailsRevealed()) { this.hideEmails(); return; }
    ...
    this.revealKey = key;
    ...
    this.emailsRevealed.set(true);
Why it matters (enterprise impact)

users-mobile.page.ts (2087 lines) independently re-implements the permission catalog grouping, AI/token-spending group separation, role-preset seeding, staged-grant save, and the key-gated email-reveal flow that already exist in features/users/users.ts (1623 lines) — sharing zero code (no import from ../users). These are the app's most security-sensitive admin surfaces (granting token-spending AI permissions, disabling accounts, revealing other users' real emails). A fix to which grants require confirmation, or to the email-reveal gate, must now be made in two places and can silently drift between platforms. The mobile docstring (line 82) even asserts 'emails are NEVER revealed here … desktop-only', which is factually contradicted by the reveal implementation at lines 1342-1394 — evidence the twins have already diverged in understanding.

Independent verifier — confirmation
users-mobile.page.ts:1342-1388 `toggleEmails(): void { if (this.emailsRevealed()) { this.hideEmails(); return; } ... this.revealKey = key; ... this.emailsRevealed.set(true);` and `private didReveal(users, audit): boolean { const isOther = (e) => !!e && e.toLowerCase() !== mine; return users.some((u)=>isOther(u.email)) || audit.some((a)=>isOther(a.actorEmail)||isOther(a.targetEmail)); }` — verbatim duplicate of users/users.ts:733-785 (same didReveal, same applyRevealKey forkJoin(users,audit) reveal gate). Grep for `import ... '../users'` in the mobile file returned "No matches found" — zero shared code. Docstring line 82: "emails are NEVER revealed here (the live page's key-gated reveal is desktop-only)" is contradicted by the reveal impl.

Verified by reading both files. The mobile twin re-implements the key-gated email-reveal flow (toggleEmails/submitRevealKey/didReveal/revealKey, lines 1342-1394) with a byte-for-byte copy of the desktop `didReveal` reveal heuristic (users/users.ts:778-785), plus the security-relevant `nonDefaultable` allowlist ("mirrors the live nonDefaultable set", line 1031), AI-key grouping, and catalog/preset seeding — sharing zero code (grep for import from ../users: no matches). These are the app's most security-sensitive admin surfaces (revealing other users' real emails, granting token-spending AI perms, disabling accounts). A change to the reveal gate or the confirm-required grant set must be applied twice and can silently drift. Concrete proof of existing drift: the mobile docstring at line 82 asserts reveal is "desktop-only" and "emails are NEVER revealed here," yet the working reveal implementation directly below it reveals emails. Severity High is appropriate for security-policy logic duplicated across two maintained copies. Location and evidence quote are accurate.

Remediation (approach)

Extract the permission-catalog model, grant-staging logic, role-preset seeding, sensitive-action confirmation rules, and the email-reveal state machine into a shared injectable service (in core/ or a shared users lib) that both the desktop and mobile components consume, leaving only layout/markup per platform. Correct the stale mobile docstring so the documented PII contract matches the code.

High CI/CD & Supply Chain CI/CD pipelines & containers CONFIRMED conf: high 2 verifiers

Deploy workflow runs on push to main with no dependency on the CI test job (untested code ships to production)

.github/workflows/deploy.yml:16-18 CI/CD - missing test gate before deploy
Evidence
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:
...
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
Why it matters (enterprise impact)

deploy.yml and ci.yml are two independent workflows both triggered by push to main; deploy has no needs:, no workflow_run gate, and does not re-run the tests. They execute concurrently, so a commit that fails the .NET build/unit/integration tests (ci.yml 'test' job) is built into :latest images and rolled out to the live EC2 host at usageiq.online regardless. For an app handling auth, PII, health (cycle/meds) and payment data, broken or regressed code reaches production before CI can flag it.

Independent verifier — confirmation
deploy.yml lines 3-6, 16-20:
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:
...
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
--- no needs:/workflow_run gate; first real action is docker build & push (lines 31-34) then SSM rollout to EC2 (lines 41-52).
ci.yml lines 3-5, 34-45 (SEPARATE workflow):
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
...
  test:
    name: Test .NET API
...
      - name: Run unit + integration tests
        run: dotnet test tests/Ccusage.Api.Tests/Ccusage.Api.Tests.csproj -c Release

Verified by reading both workflow files. deploy.yml and ci.yml are two independent workflows both triggered by `push: branches: [main]`. The deploy job (deploy.yml:16-20) has no `needs:`, no `workflow_run:` gate, and runs no tests of its own — it goes straight to `docker build`/`push` of `:latest` images (lines 31-39) and an SSM roll-out to the live EC2 host (lines 41-70). ci.yml's `test` job (lines 34-45) runs unit + integration tests but only within the CI workflow; because GitHub Actions runs the two workflows concurrently and independently, a commit that fails CI still builds and deploys to production. There is no branch-protection or artifact-promotion mechanism visible that would prevent this. For an app handling auth/PII/health/payment data this is a legitimate High-severity missing-test-gate defect. Location: the deploy job definition at deploy.yml:16 is the anchor (the trigger block cited at 3-18 is also accurate). Remediation (prose): make the deploy either depend on CI success — e.g. gate deploy on a successful CI run via a workflow_run trigger keyed to CI completion+conclusion==success, or merge build/test/deploy into one workflow where the deploy job declares needs on the test/build jobs — and/or enforce required status checks via branch protection so untested commits never reach the deploy path.

Remediation (approach)

Make production deployment conditional on a green test suite. Either gate the deploy job on the CI workflow completing successfully (e.g. trigger deploy via workflow_run on CI's completion and check the conclusion, or merge build+test into the same workflow with the deploy job declaring needs on the api/test/web jobs), or require the CI checks as branch-protection status checks so unmergeable-if-red commits never reach main. Ensure the deploy path cannot proceed when any test job failed.

High CI/CD & Supply Chain CI/CD pipelines & containers CONFIRMED conf: high 2 verifiers

Production images built, pushed and deployed as mutable ':latest' tags — no immutable digest, breaks rollback and enables race/stale pulls

.github/workflows/deploy.yml:33-39 Docker - latest tags / no image pinning
Evidence
docker build -f src/Api/Dockerfile -t "$ECR_REGISTRY/usage-iq-api:latest" src
          docker push "$ECR_REGISTRY/usage-iq-api:latest"
...
          docker build -f src/Web/Dockerfile -t "$ECR_REGISTRY/usage-iq-web:latest" src/Web
          docker push "$ECR_REGISTRY/usage-iq-web:latest"
Why it matters (enterprise impact)

Every deploy overwrites the same mutable :latest tag (also referenced in deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml as ${ECR}/usage-iq-api:latest). There is no immutable, per-commit tag, so: (1) rollback to a known-good build is impossible — the previous image is gone; (2) 'docker compose pull' on the host can race the push and pull a half-updated or previous image with no way to prove which SHA is running; (3) incident forensics cannot map a running container back to a git commit. For a production system handling PII/health/payments this defeats auditable, reversible releases.

Independent verifier — confirmation
.github/workflows/deploy.yml:33-38
  docker build -f src/Api/Dockerfile -t "$ECR_REGISTRY/usage-iq-api:latest" src
  docker push "$ECR_REGISTRY/usage-iq-api:latest"
  ...
  docker build -f src/Web/Dockerfile -t "$ECR_REGISTRY/usage-iq-web:latest" src/Web
  docker push "$ECR_REGISTRY/usage-iq-web:latest"

deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml:21,44
  image: ${ECR}/usage-iq-api:latest
  image: ${ECR}/usage-iq-web:latest

deploy/remote-deploy.sh:60-62
  docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml --env-file .env pull
  docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml --env-file .env up -d --remove-orphans
  docker image prune -f >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

Verified across all three files. deploy.yml builds/pushes only the mutable :latest tag for both API and web images — no SHA/per-commit tag or digest, even though github.sha is available (it is used only in the SSM comment on line 50, never as an image tag). docker-compose.prod.yml (lines 21, 44) consumes those same :latest tags with no digest pinning. remote-deploy.sh pulls :latest, runs up -d, then docker image prune -f (line 62), which reclaims the now-untagged previous image — so the prior known-good build is not retained under any immutable reference, making rollback impossible and defeating commit→container forensics. The compose 'pull' (line 60) is also un-pinned, so it can race an in-progress push. The defect is real, correctly located, and not mitigated anywhere in the flow. High severity is justified for a production system handling auth/PII/health/payments where auditable, reversible releases are required.

Remediation (approach)

Tag and push images with an immutable identifier per build (the git SHA and/or a semver), push that alongside (or instead of) latest, and have docker-compose.prod.yml reference the specific tag/digest for the release being rolled out (passed in via the SSM deploy). Enable ECR tag immutability so a tag can never be overwritten, and keep prior images to allow rollback.

High CI/CD & Supply Chain CI/CD pipelines & containers CONFIRMED conf: high 2 verifiers

Deployed code (remote git checkout) can drift from the built images within a single run

.github/workflows/deploy.yml:44-53 Deploy race / artifact consistency
Evidence
cat > /tmp/ssm.json <<'JSON'
{"commands":["dnf install -y git ... cd /opt/usage-iq && (if [ -d .git ]; then git fetch origin main -q && git reset --hard origin/main -q; else git clone -q https://github.com/itdept-ops/usage-iq .; fi) && bash deploy/remote-deploy.sh"]}
JSON
Why it matters (enterprise impact)

The images are built on the runner from the checked-out ${{ github.sha }} and pushed as :latest, but the roll-out step tells the EC2 host to `git fetch origin main && git reset --hard origin/main` and then run remote-deploy.sh / docker-compose.prod.yml from whatever origin/main HEAD is at SSM-execution time. These two sources are resolved at different moments: the compose file, migration-triggering startup, and deploy script the host actually executes can come from a newer (or older) commit than the images it pulls. On two pushes in quick succession the host can run commit B's compose/scripts against commit A's :latest images, producing an env-var/schema/route mismatch in production that no rollback can reproduce. This is distinct from the previously-reported 'no concurrency' issue: even a single serialized run has no guarantee the remote checkout matches the built artifact, because it re-resolves main HEAD instead of the run's SHA.

Independent verifier — confirmation
.github/workflows/deploy.yml:33-34 (images tagged :latest only): `docker build -f src/Api/Dockerfile -t "$ECR_REGISTRY/usage-iq-api:latest" src` / `docker push "$ECR_REGISTRY/usage-iq-api:latest"`; and .github/workflows/deploy.yml:44 (rollout re-resolves main HEAD): `... cd /opt/usage-iq && (if [ -d .git ]; then git fetch origin main -q && git reset --hard origin/main -q; else git clone ... fi) && bash deploy/remote-deploy.sh`. Confirmed remote-deploy.sh:60-61 runs `docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml --env-file .env pull` + `up -d` and docker-compose.prod.yml:21,44 reference `${ECR}/usage-iq-api:latest` / `usage-iq-web:latest`.

Verified across all three files. Two independent resolution points with no shared SHA pin: (1) images are built from the runner's checkout at github.sha and pushed only as :latest (lines 33-34, 38-39, no immutable/SHA tag); (2) the SSM rollout does `git reset --hard origin/main` (line 44), resolving main HEAD at SSM-execution time, then runs that checkout's deploy/remote-deploy.sh + docker-compose.prod.yml which `pull` :latest from ECR. On two pushes in quick succession the host can execute commit B's compose/scripts (env-var wiring, migration-triggering api startup, service topology) against commit A's :latest images — a schema/route/env-var mismatch in production. Distinct from a concurrency finding: even a single serialized run has no artifact/checkout coupling because it re-resolves main rather than checking out the run's SHA. The ironic tell is line 50 tagging the SSM comment with ${{ github.sha }} while the actual command uses origin/main. No mitigation exists in the flow (no SHA-pinned image tag, no ref pin on the remote checkout, no digest verification). Enterprise impact is real for an app running DB migrations on startup. Severity High and location (line 44, the git reset in the SSM commands block, lines 41-53) are accurate.

Remediation (approach)

Deploy the exact commit that was built and pushed: have the SSM command check out ${{ github.sha }} (fetch that specific SHA and reset to it) rather than origin/main, and reference immutable image digests/tags derived from that same SHA so the remote checkout and the images are provably the same revision.

High Correctness Frontend correctness CONFIRMED conf: high 2 verifiers

Offline queue permanently discards queued mutations on a 401/403 (expired or missing token) — optimistically-acked data is silently lost

src/Web/src/app/core/offline-queue.ts:166-168 offline-queue correctness (dropped requests)
Evidence
const token = currentToken();
for (const item of pending) {
  try {
    ...
    if (token) headers['Authorization'] = `Bearer ${token}`;
    ...
    const res = await fetch(item.url, { method: item.method, headers, body: ..., credentials: 'include' });
    // 2xx = applied; 4xx = definitively rejected ... stop retrying.
    if (res.status < 500) {
      await this.remove(item.id);
    }
  } catch { break; }
}
Why it matters (enterprise impact)

The offline interceptor optimistically returns a synthetic 202 to the UI for whitelisted append-style mutations (food/exercise/hydration/coffee/supplement/sleep/weight/activity logs and chat messages), so the user believes the write succeeded. currentToken() (like AuthService.token) returns null once the persisted session's expiresAtUtc has passed. App-issued JWTs are short-lived; a device that logs entries offline and stays offline past token expiry — or a session invalidated by an admin force-logout / SessionVersion bump while items are pending — will flush with NO Authorization header. The server answers 401 (or 403), which is < 500, so the item is REMOVED from IndexedDB and never retried, even though a fresh login moments later would have replayed it successfully.
