  • Decompose the God classes and duplicated twins: break up GeminiService, the frontend Api service, and the 101-DbSet DbContext along domain seams, and extract the shared sensitive UI (permission-grant, email-reveal) so desktop and mobile stop re-implementing security-critical surfaces separately.
  • Fix the query-materialization architecture: replace the up-to-2M-row in-memory materialization on calendar/heatmap/stats and the unfiltered full-table aggregates with database-side aggregation and pagination, and add leader-election/advisory-locking so schedulers and forwarders do not double-fire across API instances.

Verified clean 771

Areas that reviewers specifically inspected and judged sound against enterprise standards. Grouped by module; expand to read.

Endpoints: tracker / AI / agents / misc 46
  • AI prompt-injection into privileged actions is well-defended: /ask-act re-derives each action's endpoint SERVER-SIDE from a frozen Type->route map (AskActEndpointFor, AiEndpoints.cs ~1996) and never trusts a model-emitted route; each chip is gated by CallerMayAct matching the target write's own permission, and the actual write still re-gates server-side. Voice intents map through the same frozen VoiceEndpointFor allow-list and write nothing (frontend re-posts through owner-scoped, gated endpoints).
  • AI cost is bounded: every /api/ai route is gated by tracker.ai (OFF by default) plus the 'ai' rate-limit policy; multimodal photo/audio routes additionally require ai.vision and the tighter 'ai-photo' policy; images/audio are validated for mime + decoded size (<=5MB / <=10MB) via TryValidateImage/TryValidateAudio before any upstream call; coaching endpoints are 6h-cached per user+date.
  • PII to Gemini is controlled: the Ask/Ask-Act/Insights/DayRecap snapshots are assembled server-side, each domain section is included ONLY when the caller holds that domain's permission, and identity comes from the JWT (never the body). Journal free-text is never sent to AI (only mood/energy/tag aggregate frequencies). No email/secret is placed in any AI snapshot.
  • Search (SearchEndpoints.cs) re-gates and re-scopes every domain independently: owner-scoped (recipes/automations/bills/foods by OwnerEmail/UserEmail), household-scoped by the caller's own membership row, chat limited to channels where the caller is a ChatChannelMember, people limited to contacts-union-household. The cycle health log is excluded, bills carry no amount, and LIKE wildcards are escaped (EscapeLike) — no cross-user leakage found.
  • Tracker read visibility (GET /api/tracker/day, HardChallenge, Habit reads) consistently routes other-user reads through TrackerVisibility.CanViewAsync (viewall OR target ShareWithContacts + mutual ContactGraph edge) and returns 404 (not 403) on a non-viewable target, never leaking existence; the client sends ?user={id} and email is resolved server-side.
  • All owner-scoped mutate/delete endpoints (tracker food/exercise/hydration/coffee/supplement/sleep/weight/activity, recipes, automations, bills, journal, habits, saved foods) filter by UserEmail/OwnerEmail == caller in the WHERE clause, so a foreign id is a 404 and can never be read or mutated (IDOR-safe). The food/copy and journal/copy bulk paths also filter sources by caller ownership.
  • Mass-assignment is guarded: OwnerEmail/UserEmail/OwnerUserId are always set from the JWT caller and never bound from the request body; automation WebhookUrl is SSRF-allowlisted to Discord + encrypted and never echoed; rule TriggerKind/ConditionOp/Action are validated against fixed enums/allow-lists.
  • PermissionFilter re-checks the DB on every request (user exists, IsEnabled, holds a required permission) rather than trusting JWT claims; every endpoint group pairs RequireAuthorization with the appropriate RequirePermission. AgentInbox/Inbox/Agents are strictly RecipientEmail/UserEmail == caller scoped.
  • Nudge endpoint is circle-gated (mutual contact OR household co-member else 404), accepts no free-text (fixed NudgeKind template only, no injection/mention path), is opt-out-respecting, rate-limited, and per-(sender,target) cooldowned.
  • Public bill-share read/claim surface exposes only items + per-person totals + intentionally-public payment handles (never an email, never an assignee's resolved user name, never another bill/user); token lookup is by SHA-256 hash with the token stored AES-GCM encrypted, and the routes are IP-rate-limited and kept out of the action log.
  • AiEndpoints: identity is always taken from the JWT (CurrentUserAccessor), never from the request body; every /ai read (suggest-foods, what-to-eat, plan-meals, daily/weekly/weight/sleep coach, ask, ask-act, day-summary, tracker-recap) is keyed strictly by caller.Email / caller household-membership. No endpoint accepts a target email/user id.
  • AiEndpoints ask-act: proposed action endpoints are SERVER-issued via AskActEndpointFor (a frozen Type->route map) and each chip is gated by CallerMayAct against the caller's own write permission; a model-emitted route is never trusted and the actual write re-gates server-side. grocery_add correctly requires BOTH FamilyUse and GroceryUse, matching GroceryEndpoints.
  • AiEndpoints: multimodal (image/audio) paths validate mime/size/base64 first and additionally require the OFF-by-default AiVision permission (checked in-handler because the route gate cannot see the body); photo-to-note additionally requires FamilyAi. Free text + images are treated as DATA and only clamped JSON is parsed back. Every /ai route is under RequirePermission(TrackerAi) + the 'ai' rate-limit policy (photo routes under the tighter ai-photo cap), bounding token cost.
  • AiEndpoints BuildAskSnapshotAsync: cross-domain snapshot includes a domain ONLY when the caller holds its permission (tracker.self, bills.use, family.use, dashboard.view); bills report only the caller's own OwnerEmail bucket, family-today uses display names, and no email/secret/other-user data enters the prompt.
  • SearchEndpoints: each domain sub-query is independently re-gated by its own permission AND re-scoped exactly like its source endpoint (recipes/automations/bills/foods by owner email; family notes/lists/chores/meals by the caller's own resolved household membership; chat by ChatChannelMembers membership; people = contacts union household members via DisplayName). Cycle health log excluded, bills searched by title-only with amounts redacted, no users/email domain. LIKE wildcards are escaped. No cross-user leakage found.
  • AgentsEndpoints + AgentInboxEndpoints: every read/write is keyed UserEmail/RecipientEmail == caller (lower-cased); the inbox handle/handle-all filter on the caller's own AgentNudge rows only; a foreign or non-agent id is a silent no-op. TimeZone is validated against the system TZ database. No email on the wire.
  • RulesEndpoints: strictly owner-scoped (OwnerEmail == caller on every read/update/delete; a foreign id is a 404 with no existence leak). OwnerEmail is never accepted from the body; the webhook URL is SSRF-validated to Discord, AES-GCM encrypted at rest, and never echoed (only a HasWebhook flag). Trigger/condition/action enums validated against allow-lists.
  • HealthEndpoints: OWNER-scoped by UserId == caller.Id + provider on status/connect/disconnect/settings/sync-now; the client secret and refresh token never appear in any response (refresh token stored AES-GCM encrypted); only the non-secret client id is surfaced and only when configured. Sync backfill is bounded by MaxBackfillDays.
  • RecipeEndpoints: reads are own OR (ShareWithContacts AND mutual-contact via ContactGraph) else 404; all writes (create/update/delete/share) are owner-only with foreign id -> 404. OwnerEmail is set to the caller, never bound from the DTO; shared recipes expose owner id + display name only, never email. Ingredient/step counts and text lengths are clamped.
  • GroceryEndpoints: household-scoped through GetOrCreateForCaller; item toggle/delete bind i.ListId == the caller's own resolved Groceries list, so a foreign itemId is a 404. Quantity add clamps 1..999 and reorder ignores foreign ids. No cross-household access.
  • JournalEndpoints: owner-scoped by caller email on every read/write; the copy endpoint loads the source with Id AND UserEmail == caller (foreign id -> 404) and always writes onto the caller's own target day. Free-text gratitude/reflection is never sent to AI; only aggregate mood/energy/tag frequencies reach Gemini (AggregateFacts), and /reflection floors to a deterministic plain summary without tracker.ai.
  • BillEndpoints: owner CRUD scoped by OwnerEmail (OwnedBillAsync/OwnedItemAsync); assign restricted to a mutual ChatContact; receipt AI requires AiVision and never stores the image. Public claim surface uses a 256-bit token looked up by SHA-256 hash, is per-IP rate-limited, and the public DTO exposes only items/per-person totals/payment handles with no owner email or user ids.
  • ApiEndpoints: every data route carries a specific RequirePermission/RequireAnyPermission (DB-checked); /me/export is scoped to caller.Email so even an admin exports only their own data; pricing/sources/settings mutations require the manage permission and map only fixed fields (no ModelPattern reassignment, timezone validated). /api/health liveness is intentionally anonymous.
  • HabitEndpoints: owner-scoped by Id + UserEmail == caller on every habit and habit-day read/write (foreign id -> 404); the day-write clamps value 0..1M and dates via DateOutOfRange; AutoSource restricted to None/Water/Workout. Leaderboard exposes id + display name only. AI coach floors to a plain deterministic recap without tracker.ai.
  • InsightsEndpoints + DayRecapEndpoints + WrappedEndpoints + TrophyEndpoints: all strictly owner-scoped (UserEmail/OwnerEmail == caller; usage by ReportedByUser == caller); DayRecap gates each domain (family/finance/location) by its own permission and household membership; cycle/mood is read only when the caller owns CycleDayLogs. Only pre-computed aggregate facts reach Gemini; DTOs carry userId + display name, never an email.
  • NudgeEndpoints: circle-gated (mutual contact OR household co-member) with self/stranger/unknown all returning a flat 404 (no existence leak); body carries only a fixed enum kind (no free-text -> no injection); opt-out and per-(sender,target) cooldown are friendly 200 no-ops; gated by ChatSend + the chat rate-limit policy.
  • InboxEndpoints: every operation scoped to RecipientEmail == caller; read/read-all mark only the caller's own rows; actor emails are resolved to id+name before hitting the wire (email-privacy). Preferences upsert maps only fixed notification-preference booleans.
  • TrackerEndpoints: all write/edit/delete/copy/move/commit paths are strictly owner-scoped (UserEmail == caller), with foreign/missing ids returning 404 not 403; the cross-user GET /day resolves ?user=id server-side and gates on CanViewAsync (404 on non-viewable). Profile PUT maps each field explicitly with per-field enum/range validation (no mass-assignment); priced food edits recompute macros server-side and ignore client-sent macros; day/commit re-validates and clamps every drafted item inside one transaction with cache-based idempotency.
  • SearchEndpoints: every domain sub-query is independently re-gated on its own permission AND re-scoped exactly like its source endpoint (recipes/bills/foods by owner email, family notes/lists/chores/meals by resolved household membership, chat by ChatChannelMember membership, people by contacts∪household). Household id is resolved only from the caller's own membership row, so cross-household leakage is not possible. LIKE wildcards are escaped (EscapeLike); cycle health log, bill amounts, and locations are deliberately excluded; no users/email domain exists. Overall + per-domain caps bound result volume. Verified clean.
  • AiEndpoints: identity is always taken from the JWT (CurrentUserAccessor), never the request body; every coaching/suggestion/insight read is owner-scoped by caller.Email server-side. Multimodal routes gate ai.vision (and photo-to-note additionally family.ai) IN the handler because the route gate can't see the body; images/audio validated for mime+decoded-size before any upstream call. All free text/images framed as DATA. ask/ask-act snapshots are per-domain permission-gated and carry no email/secret/other-user data. All routes rate-limited (ai / ai-photo); token-heavy narration routes are 6h-cached. ask-act endpoints are server-issued from a frozen Type→route map and chips filtered by CallerMayAct matching each write's own gate.
  • AiEndpoints /plan-meals/to-plan and /ask-act 'meal': the household is resolved via GetOrCreateForCallerAsync(caller) / GetForCallerAsync, so writes always target the caller's own household; MealsUse is re-checked. No household id is accepted from the body.
  • RulesEndpoints: strictly owner-scoped (OwnerEmail == caller on every read/write); foreign id → 404 (no existence leak); webhook URL SSRF-allowlisted to Discord + AES-GCM encrypted, never echoed; trigger/condition/action validated against fixed enums.
  • RecipeEndpoints: own recipes read/write owner-scoped; a shared recipe is visible only when ShareWithContacts AND ContactGraph.IsContactAsync(owner, caller); writes owner-only; only owner id + display name (never email) on the wire.
  • JournalEndpoints: owner-scoped by caller email on every read/write incl. the /{id}/copy IDOR guard (source loaded with Id==id AND UserEmail==caller); free-text gratitude/reflection never sent to AI — only mood/energy/tag aggregate frequencies reach Gemini; reflection floors to plain when tracker.ai absent.
  • GroceryEndpoints: household-scoped via GetOrCreateForCallerAsync; item operations bind i.ListId == the resolved Groceries list, so a foreign itemId → 404; quantity add clamps 1..999.
  • InboxEndpoints + AgentInboxEndpoints: every read/write scoped to RecipientEmail == caller; actor emails resolved to id+name (never raw email on wire); AgentInbox restricted to NotificationType.AgentNudge and reuses IsRead with no new column.
  • HabitEndpoints: owner-scoped by caller email; leaderboard/coach reuse the contacts-sharing query, expose userId+display name only (never email/habit title); habit.dayComplete emits streak only. (Pre-reported N+1 fan-out on stats/leaderboard not re-reported.)
  • TrophyEndpoints / WrappedEndpoints: every metric query owner-scoped (UserEmail / OwnerEmail / ReportedByUser == caller); DTO carries userId + display name only; all math is pure composers.
  • HardChallengeEndpoints: read of another user's challenge gated by CanViewAsync (viewall or contacts-sharing) with 404-on-forbidden; confessions nulled for a non-self viewer; writes owner-only; viewall leaderboard roster capped at 200. (Pre-reported cheat-day ordering + unbounded-date not re-reported.)
  • HealthEndpoints: OWNER-scoped by caller.Id + provider on every read/write; client secret and refresh token never returned (refresh token AES-GCM encrypted); OAuth client id (non-secret) surfaced only when configured; graceful 503/empty states.
  • BillEndpoints: owner CRUD scoped by OwnerEmail; assign target must be a real user AND a mutual contact (else 403); public claim surface uses 256-bit token, SHA-256 hash lookup, encrypted-at-rest token, per-IP 'share' rate limit, and exposes no email/other bill. Public DTO builds per-person buckets from public claimer names only (no Users-table name resolution). (Pre-reported claim race/lost-update not re-reported.)
  • DayRecapEndpoints: every domain read owner-scoped (UserEmail/ActorEmail == caller) and date-scoped; family/finance reads scoped to the caller's OWN household; each domain gated by its own permission; AI narration floors to null (never 503) and narrates only the deterministic facts.
  • NudgeEndpoints: target addressed by AppUser id (email-privacy), circle-gated (mutual contact OR household co-member) with flat 404 on stranger/self, fixed NudgeKind templates (no free-text/injection), opt-out is an unobservable 200 no-op, per-account chat rate limit + per-(sender,target) cooldown.
  • InsightsEndpoints: strictly owner-scoped series reads (UserEmail / ReportedByUser == caller); cycle/mood series read only when the caller owns CycleDayLogs; deterministic engine is the always-200 floor and AI narration (tracker.ai-gated, 6h-cached) narrates only computed numbers.
  • ApiEndpoints: /me/export is scoped to caller.Email so even an admin exports only their own data; usage/pricing/settings routes each carry a specific RequirePermission/RequireAnyPermission gate; settings PUT validates the timezone before persisting.
  • TrackerEndpoints profile PUT: TrackerProfileDto is mapped field-by-field with per-field validation/clamping (enums via TryParse-with-default, numerics via Positive/Clamp, LifeStage/Trimester bounded) — no mass-assignment; owner-scoped; goal-plan versioning in the same transaction with unique-violation race recovery. Food/exercise writes are owner-only; deletes/edits 404 on foreign id. /food/from-meal cross-user write validates BOTH caller and target as members of the meal's household (pre-reported separately).
Endpoints: chat / share / social 42
  • Share-link tokens (ShareEndpoints, WrappedShareEndpoints) are 256-bit CSPRNG values (RandomNumberGenerator.GetBytes(32), URL-safe base64) stored only as a SHA-256 hash; lookup is by hash and invalid/expired both return an indistinguishable 404 (ShareEndpoints.cs 86-90, WrappedShareEndpoints.cs 197-199). Tokens are unguessable and non-enumerable.
  • Public share reads rebuild scope server-side from the stored ShareLink/WrappedShareLink (owner, window, filter, card whitelist all BAKED) - the anonymous holder cannot widen scope or repoint the owner (ShareEndpoints.cs 92-98; WrappedShareEndpoints.cs 201-206, OwnerEmail forced to caller at create 127).
  • Wrapped public links default-exclude sensitive cards (weight/sleep/finance) via SafeCardKeys and filter to the baked whitelist server-side on read; ResolveWhitelist only narrows, never widens, and drops any requested non-safe key (WrappedShareEndpoints.cs 43-53, 116, 205-213, 314-319). No health/finance PII reaches an anonymous viewer.
  • Wrapped public read serves a frozen NarrativeSnapshot and makes NO live Gemini call, so an anonymous token cannot drive unauthenticated model-token spend (WrappedShareEndpoints.cs 225, 244-250).
  • GET /api/public/built-with exposes aggregate numbers only for a single deterministic owner account - no email/name/project/model list - and sets a shared 10-min cache; response is identical for every caller (PublicEndpoints.cs 30-68).
  • DM/chat membership is re-verified per request via IsMemberAsync against ChatChannelMembers; message history, catch-up AI, smart replies, send, react, edit, delete, and mark-read all 404 (never 403/leak) for a non-member (ChatEndpoints.cs 123, 152, 174, 219, 251, 279, 309, 323).
  • Chat AI catch-up/replies check MEMBERSHIP before the Gemini-configured check so a non-member cannot use the 503 as a channel-existence oracle (ChatEndpoints.cs 168-175).
  • DM open (/direct) enforces the contact-graph gate: a non-admin may only DM a mutual contact (ContactGraph.IsContactAsync); chat.contacts.manage bypasses, and target is resolved from an AppUser id validated as existing+enabled (ChatEndpoints.cs 90-108).
  • Location-share UPDATE/EXTEND/STOP are addressed by share id but enforce sharer-ownership in the service (OwnedBy = SharerEmail match); a non-owner gets null -> 404, so no IDOR on share ids (ChatLocationShareService.cs 84-145, 164-165).
  • Location-share duration/lifetime are clamped (per-step 8h, overall start+24h ceiling), so a 'temporary' share cannot be extended into a permanent pin (ChatLocationShareService.cs 28-39, 105-118).
  • Contacts admin routes (directory, contacts/user/{id}, add/remove) are all gated by chat.contacts.manage; contacts/me is self-only (chat.read). Owner/contact are addressed by AppUser id and resolved server-side; raw email never crosses the wire (ContactsEndpoints.cs 34,54,64,86 + ContactsForAsync 107-122).
  • People hub is any-of chat.read/family.use and includes each source ONLY when the caller holds that source's permission; household is resolved by the caller's own membership (private), city is gated to self or a household member who shares-to-household, and CanDm mirrors the DM gate (PeopleEndpoints.cs 45-107, 149-179).
  • Feed react/comment endpoints gate on the same CanSeeEventAsync visibility predicate (own event, or a sharing in-circle actor with viewer opted-in) and return 404 (not 403) when not visible, so out-of-circle event existence is never revealed (FeedEndpoints.cs 157-209, 215-286, 322-333).
  • Feed comment delete collapses not-found and not-author/not-moderator into a single 404 so a non-author cannot use the status code as an existence oracle (FeedEndpoints.cs 294-312).
  • Pact edit/delete/add-members are OWNER-only (403 on mismatch); progress read is participant-only (404 otherwise); join only transitions an Invited->Active and a Left member cannot silently re-add themselves; invites are constrained to the owner's mutual contacts (anti-spam) (PactEndpoints.cs 116-179, 183-206, 225-239, 305-306).
  • PermissionFilter re-checks the DB every request (user exists, IsEnabled, holds any required permission); chaining two RequirePermission filters yields AND semantics, correctly applied to the chat-AI routes (PermissionFilter.cs 18-32; ChatEndpoints.cs 162-163).
  • Public read access-recording is wrapped in try/catch so a concurrent revoke or write error cannot 500 a valid read or leak internal state (ShareEndpoints.cs 113-130; WrappedShareEndpoints.cs 230-247).
  • Attacker-supplied share filter arrays (project ids, model/source labels) are capped and de-duped before persistence so a stored share cannot carry an unbounded replay payload (ShareEndpoints.cs 145-162).
  • Emails are consistently kept off the wire across chat/feed/pact/people/share responses; identity is resolved to AppUser id + DisplayName.Format via SenderLookupAsync/ResolveActorsAsync/BuildOwnerResolverAsync.
  • Public share-link tokens (ShareEndpoints + WrappedShareEndpoints) are 256-bit CSPRNG (RandomNumberGenerator.GetBytes(32)), URL-safe base64, stored only as SHA-256 hash with the raw token AES-GCM-encrypted at rest via TokenProtector — unguessable and non-enumerable; lookup is a single equality on TokenHash.
  • Public read paths (/api/share/{token}, /api/share/wrapped/{token}, /api/public/built-with) all use AllowAnonymous + RequireRateLimiting('share') which partitions per real client IP (60/min); invalid vs expired collapse to an indistinguishable 404 (no existence/expiry oracle).
  • WrappedShare public exposure is PII-safe: sensitive card keys (weight-delta, sleep, bills) are default-excluded via SafeCardKeys, the whitelist can only be NARROWED not widened, the whitelist is re-applied SERVER-SIDE on the anonymous read, OwnerEmail is BAKED to the creator, and the narrative is a frozen snapshot (no live Gemini call / no unauthenticated token spend). Owner is a DisplayName-formatted name, never an email.
  • Chat message read/history/react/edit/delete/mark-read/AI-catch-up/AI-replies all re-verify channel membership per request via IsMemberAsync and return 404 (not 403) for non-members, so a non-member cannot read or post and channel existence is never leaked.
  • SignalR group membership (ChatHub.OnConnectedAsync / JoinChannel) is gated by chat.read + IsMemberAsync, and location-share broadcasts go only to the channel's group, so pins reach only conversation participants; channel members are never removable, so there is no stale-group leakage window.
  • Location-share update/extend/stop enforce sharer-ownership (OwnedBy on SharerEmail) and activeness before mutating/broadcasting; a non-owner gets an indistinguishable 404, so one user cannot drive or read another user's share by id.
  • Feed react/comment/comment-read/comment-delete all gate on the shared CanSeeEventAsync circle predicate (own event, or a ShareActivity-sharing contact when the caller opted into ViewActivityFeed) and return 404-not-403 for non-visible events; reactor/commenter identities are never exposed beyond count + caller's own flag; actor emails never cross the wire (ResolveActorsAsync → id + DisplayName).
  • PactEndpoints enforce owner-only edit/delete/add-members (403 on non-owner), participant-only progress reads (404 otherwise), the mutual-contact anti-spam constraint on invites (ContactGraph.IsContactAsync), and Left members cannot silently re-activate via /join; members cross the wire as id + DisplayName only.
  • ContactsEndpoints admin-curation routes are all gated by chat.contacts.manage and resolve owner/contact by AppUser id server-side; contacts are written/removed mutually; the /directory enumeration route is admin-only (chat.contacts.manage), not reachable by ordinary chat users.
  • PeopleEndpoints unions only the caller's OWN contacts and OWN household (each gated by the caller's own permission), honors appear-offline, gates coarse City to self/same-household-sharer exactly like PresenceEndpoints, and never emits an email.
  • Share/Wrapped access recording is best-effort in try/catch so a concurrent revoke or write error can never 500 a valid public read; IP is recorded only in the authenticated owner-visible access log.
  • Share tokens (ShareEndpoints.GenerateToken / WrappedShareEndpoints.GenerateToken) use RandomNumberGenerator.GetBytes(32) = 256 bits of entropy, are stored only as SHA-256 hashes (TokenHash), and both public reads are wrapped in RequireRateLimiting("share") — tokens are unguessable, non-enumerable, and rate-limited.
  • Public share read GET /api/share/{token} returns NotFound identically for invalid vs expired links (line 89-90), so expiry state is not an oracle; the served filter is rebuilt from the persisted ShareLink (holder cannot widen scope).
  • Wrapped public link (GET /api/share/wrapped/{token}) bakes OwnerEmail + window + card whitelist at create time and re-filters cards server-side to the baked whitelist; sensitive cards (weight-delta/sleep/bills) are excluded via SafeCardKeys and cannot be widened by the holder, and the narrative is a frozen snapshot with no live Gemini call — no unauthenticated token spend.
  • PublicEndpoints /built-with emits aggregate NUMBERS ONLY for a single deterministic owner (no email/name/project/model), so the response is identical for every caller and carries no PII to enumerate.
  • Chat message send (POST /channels/{id}/messages) re-verifies membership (channel.Members.All(...)) AND rejects archived channels; message history, catch-up, replies, reactions, and mark-read all re-check IsMemberAsync and return 404 (never 403) for non-members so channel existence is not leaked.
  • Chat edit/delete verify membership AND owner-or-moderator before mutating, and return 404 for a message in a channel the caller can't see.
  • DM open (POST /direct) enforces the mutual-contact gate for non-admins (ContactGraph.IsContactAsync) and only lets chat.contacts.manage admins bypass it.
  • ContactsEndpoints contact add/remove/directory are all gated by chat.contacts.manage (admin-only); the id-vs-existence distinction in responses is only reachable by an admin, so it is not an enumeration primitive for ordinary users.
  • PeopleEndpoints applies city-privacy (self or fellow household member who shares-to-household), honors AppearOffline, mirrors the DM gate for CanDm, and never puts raw email on the wire (DisplayName.Format throughout).
  • FeedEndpoints react/comment/thread reads all route through CanSeeEventAsync (own event, or a sharing contact when ViewActivityFeed is on) and translate not-visible to 404, never revealing an out-of-circle event; comment delete collapses missing/unauthorized to 404 so non-authors get no existence oracle.
  • Location-share GET /channels/{id}/location-shares re-checks IsMemberAsync (non-member 404); update/extend/stop are correctly restricted to the sharer via OwnedBy (ownership is the right authority for one's own share); coordinates are clamped to valid lat/lng ranges.
  • Pact invites are constrained to the owner's mutual contacts (ContactGraph.IsContactAsync) so a pact cannot be used as an unsolicited-invite/spam vector; join() will not let a Left/declined member silently re-activate.
Services & background workers 42
  • AgentScheduler.TickAsync: the coarse SQL floor (utcDate+2) SUPERSET filter plus the precise in-memory LastFiredLocalDate == localDate re-check correctly avoids excluding due agents in eastern timezones while re-dropping already-fired ones; stamp-first via ExecuteUpdate before NotifySystem gives crash-safe at-most-once per local date. The tracked-vs-untracked candidate load is harmless because no SaveChangesAsync is ever called on that scope's context (only ExecuteUpdate), so the in-memory mutations never double-persist.
  • AgentScheduler.InQuietHours: correct wrap-past-midnight math (s<e same-day, s>e wrapping) with empty-window (s==e) and null handling; StreakRescue defers without stamping while briefing/budget stamp-and-drop-for-the-day, which is the intended behavior.
  • FamilyReminderService.FireDueRemindersAsync/NextOccurrence: state advance (deactivate one-shot / advance recurring strictly past now) is persisted BEFORE NotifyFamily, so a crash/retry can't re-ping; NextOccurrence's while(next<=now) loop guarantees a long-overdue reminder fires once then schedules ahead rather than re-firing every tick.
  • FamilyReminderService.AnnounceEventsForHouseholdAsync: RECORD-FIRST insert of FamilyEventAnnouncement guarded by the unique (HouseholdId, GoogleEventId) index with a caught DbUpdateException + ChangeTracker.Clear on unique-violation is a correct race-safe announce-once.
  • HealthSyncScheduler.SyncConnectionAsync: stamp-cursor-first via ExecuteUpdate before mapping each day, backfill window capped at MaxBackfillDays, future-day clamp, and graceful back-off on AuthExpired/RateLimited (break, keep cursor) are all sound; per-user timezone resolution falls back cleanly.
  • DiscordForwarder: bounded DropOldest channel with SingleReader=true, per-user token bucket under a lock, 429 retry-after honored once and capped at 10s, all errors swallowed with metadata-only logging, webhook decrypted in-memory only and re-validated against the Discord allowlist at send time — the fire-and-forget contract (never block/fail notification creation) holds.
  • WebPushForwarder/WebPushSender: unconfigured deployments drop at the door (no DB work); sender fails CLOSED on a pref-load DB blip, gates on SurfaceBrowser server-side, prunes 404/410 gone subscriptions, and never throws into the caller; fresh scope per item is created correctly.
  • ChatNotificationService: fan-out resolves emails from ids server-side (email-privacy), enqueues Discord/WebPush only as fire-and-forget tails after the in-app rows are persisted, and the N+1-avoiding grouped unread queries are correct; ToggleReactionAsync handles both the concurrent-add unique-violation and concurrent-remove DbUpdateConcurrencyException by clearing the tracker and converging.
  • SyncCoordinator: SemaphoreSlim(1,1) with WaitAsync(0) for the timer (returns null if busy) and WaitAsync(ct) for manual correctly serializes manual + background syncs so they never overlap; the gate is always released in finally.
  • AutoSyncBackgroundService: re-reads enabled/interval from AppConfig every cycle (config changes without restart), enforces a 30s floor, skips if a sync is already running (waitIfBusy:false), and treats a config-read failure defensively; OperationCanceledException breaks the loop cleanly.
  • FamilyBriefingService.DeliverAsync: LastBriefingLocalDate stamp-first via ExecuteUpdate before the guaranteed bell delivery, AI narrative gated on someone holding family.ai and always falling back to the deterministic Compose() floor, chat post wrapped best-effort — idempotent within a household-local day and never fails on the AI or chat side.
  • ActivityEmitter.EmitAsync: correctly opens its OWN scope/DbContext (scope-agnostic despite being registered scoped) so the fire-and-forget tail outlives the request scope; call sites (_ = activity.EmitAsync/EmitOnceAsync) do NOT pass the request CancellationToken, so request completion cannot cancel the in-flight activity write, and the method swallows all exceptions so there is no unobserved-exception fault.
  • NotificationBackgroundService: '>= hour' (not '== hour') digest guards mean a restart spanning the configured minute still sends rather than dropping; LastDailySent/LastWeeklySent/LastThresholdSent/LastRecapSent advance only after a confirmed send inside their own SaveChanges (transient failures retry next tick); ForwardSecurityAsync advances the high-water mark only up to the last successfully-sent entry and never lets auth.* events carry an @everyone mention.
  • No async void, .Result, .Wait(), or GetAwaiter().GetResult() blocking-over-async anti-patterns were found in src/Api/Services; every hosted-service loop passes CancellationToken through and catches OperationCanceledException on the stopping token to break cleanly.
  • AgentScheduler.ExecuteAsync uses PeriodicTimer with a proper try/catch inside the loop, catches OperationCanceledException on the stoppingToken to break cleanly, logs other exceptions without crashing the loop, and swallows per-agent failures in a nested try/catch — no unobserved-exception loop crash and no async void.
  • AgentScheduler idempotency is genuinely stamp-first: StampFiredAsync runs an ExecuteUpdateAsync BEFORE notifier.NotifySystem, and the coarse SQL floor (utcDate+2) plus the precise in-memory LastFiredLocalDate == localDate re-check correctly handle the mixed-timezone superset without missing due agents east of UTC.
  • AgentScheduler.InQuietHours correctly normalizes hours mod 24, treats equal bounds as an empty window, and handles the wrap-past-midnight case (start>end) — the quiet-hours math itself is sound.
  • AgentScheduler resolves each distinct IANA timezone once per tick via a per-tick dictionary cache and falls back to UTC on unknown ids; DST spring-forward is safe because the check is 'Hour >= DeliverHourLocal' (not '=='), so a skipped local hour still fires.
  • FamilyReminderService.NextOccurrence always advances by at least one day per Step and loops while next <= now, so a long-dormant reminder cannot infinite-loop and fires exactly once before scheduling ahead.
  • FamilyReminderService.AnnounceEventsForHouseholdAsync records the FamilyEventAnnouncement FIRST and relies on a unique (HouseholdId, GoogleEventId) index, catching the unique-violation to skip notifying — a correct race-safe announce-once.
  • FamilyReminderService reminder/timer/chore-reset sub-passes each guard on count==0, resolve per-household timezones once, and compute weekly period start with correct Monday-based ISO offset; briefing and heads-up sub-passes swallow per-household so one bad household can't block the rest.
  • DiscordForwarder uses a bounded DropOldest channel with SingleReader, drains off the request path, swallows all per-item errors, and its TokenBucket uses a lock for the check-then-decrement so the per-user rate limit is thread-safe.
  • WebPushForwarder mirrors the DiscordForwarder pattern correctly: bounded DropOldest channel, drops at the door when unconfigured, resolves a fresh scope per item, and swallows scope/sender failures.
  • AutoSyncBackgroundService re-reads its enabled flag/interval each cycle, delegates overlap-prevention to SyncCoordinator's SemaphoreSlim (waitIfBusy:false → returns null when busy), clamps the interval to a 30s floor, and honors the stoppingToken on every Task.Delay.
  • SyncCoordinator correctly serializes manual and timer syncs with a 1-slot SemaphoreSlim, releases in a finally, and returns null immediately (WaitAsync(0)) when a run is already in progress.
  • RequestLogWriter drains in bounded batches, prunes only every PruneEvery inserts using MaxAsync/ExecuteDeleteAsync to keep ~MaxRows, and recovers from a failed batch with a short delay rather than crashing the loop; _sincePrune is mutated only by the single reader loop so no race.
  • IngestWriteService handles concurrent inserts robustly: within-batch de-dup, index-backed existence check, a bounded unique-violation retry loop that re-filters the remainder, per-row isolation fallback, and get-or-create project/file that adopt the existing row on a concurrent-create unique violation.
  • IngestWriteService.UpsertMachineInfoAsync clamps all client fields, trusts only the server-observed publicIp, protects a precise 'agent' GPS fix from coarse IP-geo downgrade, catches the unique-violation to retry as an ExecuteUpdate, and clears the ChangeTracker in a finally so the shared scoped context stays clean for the usage-write path.
  • NotificationBackgroundService digests use '>= hour' with a per-item Last*Sent-date guard advanced only on a confirmed send inside its own SaveChanges, so a transient failure retries next tick and a restart spanning the minute still sends — correct at-most-once-per-day within a single instance.
  • WeeklyRecapComposer is strictly owner-scoped (all loads keyed by the lower-cased email), re-validates opt-in + webhook + SSRF allowlist at send time, decrypts only in memory, masks the email in logs, and its Aggregate is a pure DB-free function.
  • ChatNotificationService.NotifySystem/NotifyFamily null-guard the recipient collections, dedupe, resolve ids→emails for enabled users only (email-privacy), skip when nothing to write, and enqueue Discord/web-push fire-and-forget so mirror delivery never blocks the caller.
  • AgentScheduler.InQuietHours correctly handles midnight-wrapping windows (inclusive start, exclusive end) and empty/equal windows; the coarse SQL floor (utcDate+2) plus in-memory precise LastFiredLocalDate==localDate re-check is sound for offsets UTC-12..UTC+14.
  • AgentScheduler/HealthSyncScheduler/FamilyReminderService/NotificationBackgroundService all correctly resolve a fresh scoped UsageDbContext per tick via IServiceScopeFactory (no DbContext captured across ticks/threads); the singleton services hold only the scope factory + logger.
  • All BackgroundService ExecuteAsync loops wrap the tick body in try/catch with an OperationCanceledException-on-shutdown break, so a tick-level exception logs and the loop survives (no unobserved-exception crash of the hosted service); no async void anywhere.
  • DiscordForwarder and WebPushForwarder use bounded DropOldest channels with SingleReader, drain off the request path, and swallow per-item errors; the token bucket's TryTake is correctly locked. (Bucket eviction growth already reported in a prior round.)
  • SyncCoordinator correctly serializes manual + background syncs with a SemaphoreSlim(1,1); the timer path uses WaitAsync(0) to skip when busy and always releases in finally.
  • RuleEvaluator is strictly self-scoped (OwnerEmail == actor), wraps its whole body in try/catch, and sanitizes @everyone/@here to a fixed point post-substitution; no cross-user delivery path exists.
  • IngestWriteService.InsertNewAsync handles unique-violation races with a bounded retry that re-filters against the DB and falls back to per-row inserts, and clears the ChangeTracker on each branch to keep the shared context clean.
  • HealthSyncMapper's DailyActivity (steps/resting-HR) path is convergent under concurrency because DailyActivity has a UNIQUE (UserEmail, LocalDate) index (UsageDbContext.cs:792) — the duplicate risk is confined to the record-keyed SleepEntry/ExerciseEntry paths.
  • IpGeoService correctly rejects private/loopback/link-local/CGNAT/unique-local IPs before any outbound call, caches ~24h, fixes the host, and never throws into the caller.
  • FamilyReminderService.NextOccurrence always steps strictly past 'now' (guaranteeing loop termination and no re-fire storm after downtime); FamilyEventAnnouncement uses record-first with a unique (HouseholdId, GoogleEventId) index for race-safe announce-once.
  • RequestLogWriter drains a bounded DropWrite queue, batch-inserts, and prunes to a recent window by max-Id; failures log and back off without crashing the loop.
Endpoints: admin / users / fleet / ingest 40
  • PermissionFilter (src/Api/Auth/PermissionFilter.cs) re-reads the user from the DB every request via CurrentUserAccessor, enforces both IsEnabled and 'holds ANY of the required permissions' with a 403, so RequirePermission/RequireAnyPermission are real DB-backed authorization checks, not just claim assertions.
  • JWT pipeline (Program.cs OnTokenValidated, lines 289-322) enforces the 'sv' SessionVersion claim against the user's current SessionVersion (missing claim = 0) and re-loads the enabled user, so force-logout (POST /api/users/{id}/logout bumping SessionVersion) genuinely invalidates all outstanding tokens on the next request.
  • All privilege-escalation guards in UsersEndpoints are present: user create/update/delete/set-home/force-logout are each RequirePermission(UsersManage); ValidPermissions filters to catalog keys; IsLastAdmin (serializable transaction) prevents removing/disabling/deleting the last enabled admin; PUT /api/access-policy filters defaults through IsDefaultable so open-signup can never auto-grant users.manage.
  • Self-permission-grant is not possible: PATCH /api/auth/profile and /api/auth/home only ever mutate the caller's own row (matched by JWT id) and never touch the Permissions collection; there is no self-service permission-write path.
  • Email-privacy gate (EmailsRevealed in UsersEndpoints.cs) uses CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals for a constant-time compare of X-Email-Reveal-Key, returns false when no key is configured, masks other users' emails in both GET /api/users and GET /api/audit (including regex-masking email-shaped substrings in free-text audit Detail), and always shows the caller their own email.
  • Observability endpoints (GET /api/logs, GET /api/ai-usage) are gated by activity.view and ai.usage.view respectively; both resolve raw UserEmail to {userId, name} server-side and never emit the raw email on the wire, and /api/logs is itself in the middleware Excluded list so the log endpoint's own responses aren't re-logged.
  • Ingest key auth (IngestKeyFilter) hashes the X-Ingest-Key with SHA-256, matches only non-revoked keys, attributes rows to the server-resolved key owner (never a client-supplied user), derives the public IP from the connection (not a client header), and the ingest endpoint is AllowAnonymous + rate-limited by design; ingest-key management routes enforce in-handler ownership (reporter.manage sees all, otherwise key.UserId == caller.Id) on list/delete.
  • FleetEndpoints are all gated by reporter.manage; user-dimension operations accept only AppUser IDs and resolve them to emails server-side (raw emails never accepted from the client); destructive delete/reassign reject empty/no-op inputs; revoke-keys resolves the email from the userId before matching legacy keys, so it cannot be steered to revoke another user's keys.
  • LocationEndpoints authorization is correct: POST/GET-me/DELETE-me/settings require location.self, the admin map requires location.view-all, turning on household sharing additionally requires location.share, and the opt-in LocationEnabled toggle gates all non-login capture; the admin map resolves owner emails to userId+name and never emits raw emails. (Its response body handling in the request-logging middleware is the subject of the High finding, but the endpoint's own authz is sound.)
  • PresenceEndpoints correctly resolves online emails to public identity, honors appear-offline for others, and gates coarse-city / auto-context visibility on household membership + the user's share flags; SavedViews and Push and per-user Discord (/api/notifications/me/discord) are all strictly caller-scoped (owner taken from the JWT, cross-user access returns 404/no-op), and the per-user Discord webhook is SSRF-validated + encrypted at rest with only a masked hint exposed.
  • RequestLoggingMiddleware already excludes /api/users, /api/audit, /api/access-policy, /api/chat/contacts and /api/chat/directory bodies from capture (PII), fully excludes /api/share, /api/bill-share, /api/auth/me, /api/presence, /api/logs, and derives ClientIp from the connection rather than a spoofable header; LogRedaction whole-value-redacts /api/auth, /api/notifications, /api/shares, /api/ingest-keys and /api/family/calendar bodies and field-redacts common secret keys.
  • Privilege escalation via self-grant is prevented: PUT /api/users/{id} and POST /api/users are gated by users.manage (PermissionFilter re-reads DB each request), IsLastAdmin runs inside a Serializable transaction + execution strategy so the last-admin demotion/delete can't be raced, and ValidPermissions filters to catalog keys. There is no self-service permission-write path — PATCH /api/auth/profile and /api/auth/home only touch display/presence/home on the caller's own row, never Permissions or IsEnabled.
  • access-policy open-signup defaults are correctly filtered through IsDefaultable on both read and write, and IsDefaultable hard-excludes UsersManage, so open sign-up can never auto-mint an administrator (verified in Permissions.cs).
  • EmailRevealKey gate (EmailsRevealed) uses CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals (constant-time, and returns false rather than throwing on length mismatch), only reads the key from the X-Email-Reveal-Key header (never query/URL), and returns false when no key is configured. GET /api/users and GET /api/audit both honor it, and GET /api/audit additionally scrubs email-shaped substrings from free-text Detail via MaskEmailsInText when the gate is closed; the caller's own actor/target email is the only one shown unmasked.
  • Force-logout (POST /api/users/{id}/logout) is users.manage-gated, bumps SessionVersion (the source of truth), and the JWT OnTokenValidated handler in Program.cs fails any token whose 'sv' claim != user.SessionVersion, so stale tokens are rejected on the next request; the SignalR SessionRevoked push is best-effort and wrapped in try/catch.
  • Ingest key auth (IngestKeyFilter): the raw key is SHA-256 hashed and matched against a non-revoked IngestKey row; owner email is server-derived from the key's linked user and stashed in HttpContext.Items — the ingest handler attributes rows to that owner, never to a client-supplied user. Endpoint is AllowAnonymous + rate-limited + filter-gated. Generated keys carry 256 bits of entropy.
  • Ingest-key management (/api/ingest-keys) enforces ownership in-handler: list/delete scope to key.UserId==caller.Id unless the caller holds reporter.manage; keys are always owned by their creator (UserId + CreatedByEmail from the JWT), and the raw key is returned exactly once.
  • ObservabilityEndpoints GET /api/logs is gated by activity.view and GET /api/ai-usage by ai.usage.view; both resolve raw UserEmail to {AppUser.Id, Name} server-side and never put the raw email on the wire (the ai-usage 'user' filter accepts an AppUser id, never an email, and an unknown id yields an empty window).
  • FleetEndpoints group is gated by reporter.manage; user-dimension operations take AppUser ids and resolve them to raw emails server-side (client never sends emails), destructive delete rejects an empty names array (no WHERE-less delete), reassign rejects the single-source no-op, and revoke-keys resolves the user id before matching keys by UserId OR legacy CreatedByEmail.
  • LocationEndpoints authorization is correct: POST/GET-me/DELETE-me/settings require location.self, turning ON household sharing additionally requires location.share, and GET /api/location/admin requires location.view-all; the admin map returns userId+name only (never raw email). All self routes scope strictly to caller.Email.
  • PresenceEndpoints: coarse-city visibility is correctly gated (self always; others only when the target shares-to-household AND is in the caller's household), appear-offline hides a user from others' rosters, auto-context rides only on the target's opt-in, and no raw email is emitted (public identity resolved via Users lookup; ScrubName strips any email-shaped fallback name).
  • SavedViewsEndpoints are strictly owner-scoped (every query filters v.UserId==user.Id; cross-user id → 404, never leaking existence) and gated by dashboard.view OR calendar.view, with input length/count hardening.
  • NotificationsEndpoints: admin config (webhook/routes/test/snapshot) is gated by notifications.manage; the webhook is masked on read and its body redacted in logs via LogRedaction's /api/notifications whole-value redaction; the per-user /api/notifications/me/discord group is auth-only but strictly self-scoped by caller.Email and SSRF-validates + encrypts the user's own webhook.
  • PushEndpoints: the VAPID public key route is intentionally anon (public key only), and subscribe/unsubscribe are gated by chat.read with the owner always taken from the JWT (never the body); unsubscribe scopes deletion to OwnerEmail==caller AND endpoint.
  • PermissionFilter re-checks the DB on every request (user must exist, be enabled, and hold any required permission), so enabled-state and permission changes take effect immediately; RequireAnyPermission correctly implements OR semantics.
  • UsersEndpoints PUT/DELETE /api/users use Serializable transactions + a last-admin guard (IsLastAdmin checks other enabled admins), so the last administrator cannot be removed, disabled, or deleted — no lockout / self-demotion-to-zero-admins race.
  • Self-permission-grant is not possible: PUT /api/users/{id} permissions come only from the request and are filtered by ValidPermissions/Permissions.IsValid; there is no self-service permission-write path (PATCH /api/auth/profile and /api/auth/home only touch display/home fields, never Permissions).
  • Open-signup defaults cannot mint an admin: PUT /api/access-policy filters DefaultPermissions through Permissions.IsDefaultable, which explicitly excludes UsersManage (and ChatModerate/AI/Location/etc.).
  • EmailRevealKey gate (EmailsRevealed) uses a constant-time FixedTimeEquals compare, returns false when no key is configured, reads the key only from the X-Email-Reveal-Key header (never query string), and is applied consistently to GET /api/users and GET /api/audit incl. actor/target masking + free-text Detail email scrubbing.
  • Admin set-home (PATCH /api/users/{id}/home) validates the route against the TARGET user's persisted permissions via HomeRoutes.CanAccess, so an admin cannot pin a user to a page they cannot reach; self set-home (PATCH /api/auth/home) validates against the caller.
  • Ingest attribution is server-derived: the owner email comes from IngestKeyFilter's resolved key owner (HttpContext.Items), never from the client body, and the recorded public IP is Connection.RemoteIpAddress, not a client-sent value.
  • Ingest keys are stored only as SHA-256 hashes; the raw key is returned exactly once on create; GenerateKey uses RandomNumberGenerator with 256 bits of entropy.
  • IngestKey management (list/create/delete) correctly scopes reporter.self/reporter.view callers to their own keys (k.UserId == caller.Id) while reporter.manage sees all; DELETE re-checks ownership before revoking.
  • SavedViewsEndpoints scopes every read/update/delete to v.UserId == caller.Id and returns 404 (not 403) for another user's id, so it neither mutates nor leaks the existence of other users' views; inputs are length/count-capped and CSV-sanitized.
  • PushEndpoints derive the subscription owner from the JWT (never the body), scope DELETE to the caller's own OwnerEmail+endpoint, and handle the unique-endpoint race idempotently; the private VAPID key is never returned (only the public key, anon by design).
  • Notifications personal-Discord webhook is SSRF-validated (DiscordWebhookValidator) + encrypted at rest (TokenProtector) with only a masked hint returned; the admin webhook PUT body is redacted in the request log (LogRedaction covers webhookUrl/discordWebhookUrl and /api/notifications is a secret path).
  • LocationEndpoints GET/DELETE /me and /settings are self-scoped by caller.Email; the admin map (/admin) requires location.view-all and emits userId+name only (raw owner email never on the wire); turning on household sharing additionally requires the location.share grant.
  • PresenceEndpoints resolve online emails to public identity only, honor AppearOffline for others, and gate coarse-city visibility on the target's LocationShareHousehold AND shared-household membership (city suppressed otherwise); ScrubName strips an email local-part fallback.
  • ObservabilityEndpoints /api/logs (activity.view) and /api/ai-usage (ai.usage.view) resolve raw request-user emails to {userId,name} and never emit the raw email; the ai-usage user filter accepts an AppUser id and resolves it server-side, and an unknown id yields an empty window rather than an error.
  • RequestLoggingMiddleware excludes public-token routes (/api/share, /api/bill-share) and auth from the log, and LogRedaction whole-redacts /api/auth (Google token in / JWT out) and field-redacts token/password/secret/apiKey/webhook fields in JSON + urlencoded + query bodies.
Backend auth core 38
  • JWT signing key is validated at startup (Program.cs 254-258) and again at issuance (GoogleAuthService 233-235): the API refuses to start / issue with a missing or <32-byte key, and never falls back to a hardcoded key, so token forgery via a default key is not possible.
  • JWT validation (Program.cs 264-275) correctly enforces issuer, audience, signing key, HmacSha256 as the only allowed algorithm (blocks alg=none/RS-HS confusion), and lifetime with a 2-minute clock skew; MapInboundClaims=false keeps the raw 'email'/'sv' claim names.
  • Session-version force-logout logic (Program.cs 294-322) correctly treats a missing sv claim as 0, fails the token when tokenSv != user.SessionVersion, and only stashes the loaded user for reuse on success — the deferred-to-403 path for unknown users matches the PermissionFilter behavior and does not grant access.
  • PermissionFilter (PermissionFilter.cs 18-32) re-reads the user from the DB every request via CurrentUserAccessor, enforces IsEnabled, and requires ANY of the listed permissions — no wildcard/bypass; a disabled or under-permissioned user is rejected with 403.
  • IngestKeyFilter (IngestKeyFilter.cs 22-58) authenticates the reporter by SHA-256 hashing the provided key and matching a non-revoked IngestKey row; only the hash is stored, missing/invalid keys are 401, and the LastUsed side-write is wrapped so it cannot fail the request or bypass auth.
  • The ingest request-body size cap (Program.cs 538-547) is applied in middleware BEFORE model binding / the endpoint filter, so an unauthenticated caller cannot force a large parse before the key check.
  • Swagger is registered but only mapped under app.Environment.IsDevelopment() (Program.cs 521-525), so the full API schema is not exposed to anonymous callers in production.
  • CORS (Program.cs 248-250) binds WithOrigins to a single configured origin (prod sets https://${DOMAIN}) and does NOT call AllowCredentials; combined with Bearer-token (non-cookie) auth, AllowAnyHeader/AllowAnyMethod is acceptable and not a wildcard-with-credentials misconfiguration.
  • ChatHub (ChatHub.cs) is [Authorize], derives identity from the email claim, and re-checks chat.read/chat.send against the DB (including IsEnabled) on every hub method plus channel membership — capability is not trusted from the connection alone.
  • SignalR access_token is lifted from the query string only for paths under /api/hubs (Program.cs 283-286), never for normal API routes; and access_token query params are redacted globally in the request log (LogRedaction RedactQuery + SecretPairRegex), so hub tokens do not land in the Activity log.
  • Google ID token validation (IGoogleTokenValidator.cs) constrains the audience to the configured client id, and GoogleAuthService additionally requires EmailVerified and a stable Subject, binds email<->GoogleSubject on first login, and rejects a later subject mismatch for the same email (recycled-address protection).
  • Auto-provision default permissions are filtered through Permissions.IsDefaultable (GoogleAuthService 179-181), which excludes users.manage, all AI keys, all location keys, family/finance/cycle, chore keys, and other sensitive gates — so open sign-up can never mint an admin or grant private-data access by default.
  • The X-Email-Reveal-Key comparison (UsersEndpoints.cs 294-303) uses CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals (constant-time) and returns false when no key is configured, so the key cannot be recovered by timing and defaults closed when blank.
  • Client-observed IP for logs uses HttpContext.Connection.RemoteIpAddress after UseForwardedHeaders (RequestLoggingMiddleware.ClientIp 140-142) rather than trusting the raw header directly, which is the correct pattern.
  • RequestLoggingMiddleware drops request/response bodies for email-bearing admin paths (/api/users, /api/audit, /api/access-policy, chat contacts/directory) and excludes public share/bill-share/ingest token routes, preventing PII and live tokens from being replayed on the Activity page.
  • JWT signing: the API hard-fails at startup on a missing/weak Jwt:Key (>=32 bytes required) both in Program.cs and again in GoogleAuthService.IssueToken; ValidAlgorithms is pinned to HmacSha256 so alg-confusion/'none' is not accepted, MapInboundClaims=false avoids claim remapping, and issuer/audience/lifetime are all validated with a bounded 2-minute ClockSkew — sound.
  • Session-version enforcement over HTTP is correct: OnTokenValidated re-loads the user each request, treats a missing 'sv' claim as 0 (no mass-logout on deploy), and calls ctx.Fail when tokenSv != user.SessionVersion; the logout endpoint bumps SessionVersion transactionally.
  • IngestKeyFilter authenticates by SHA-256 hash matched against a non-revoked IngestKey row via a DB index equality (not a byte-by-byte secret compare in app code), so there is no app-level timing side channel; the LastUsed side-write is wrapped in try/catch and never affects the auth decision.
  • SignalR access_token handling is correctly scoped: OnMessageReceived only lifts ?access_token into the token slot for paths under /api/hubs, never for normal API routes; the token is redacted from the logged query string by LogRedaction (access_token is in SecretKeys).
  • ChatHub authorization is DB-backed on every method (OnConnectedAsync + SendMessage/ToggleReaction/MarkRead/typing/JoinChannel all re-check IsEnabled + the chat permission and channel membership), so permission revocation and account-disable take effect on the hub immediately (only the SessionVersion bump is missed — reported).
  • Swagger is registered but only mapped under app.Environment.IsDevelopment(), so the full API schema/UI is not exposed to anonymous callers in production.
  • CORS uses a single configured origin (Cors:AllowedOrigin) with AllowAnyHeader/AllowAnyMethod and does NOT set AllowCredentials; since auth is a bearer Authorization header (not cookies) this is not a credentialed-CORS exposure, and the localhost fallback only ever loosens to a non-attacker origin.
  • Public share / wrapped-share / bill-share / built-with reads are all AllowAnonymous + RequireRateLimiting and are 404-indistinguishable on invalid/expired tokens; wrapped links bake owner/window/whitelist server-side (holder cannot widen), filter sensitive cards to a server-side whitelist, and serve a frozen narrative snapshot with no live Gemini call.
  • Ingest request body is capped (4 MiB) by middleware that runs BEFORE model binding for POST /api/ingest, closing the pre-auth large-parse vector; the ingest rate-limit policy is generous but keyed per-IP as intended for the anonymous machine credential.
  • The auto-provision race is handled correctly: a DbUpdateException from the unique Email/GoogleSubject index is caught, the tracker cleared, and the committed row reloaded to fall through to the existing-user path; GoogleSubject binding rejects a later login presenting the same email under a different Google subject.
  • Per-email rate-limit policies (notif-test/chat/ai/photo/search/gif) are all attached to RequireAuthorization endpoints where OnTokenValidated guarantees an email claim, so the documented IP fallback partition is effectively unreachable for those authenticated policies.
  • JWT validation (Program.cs 260-324) correctly enforces ValidateIssuer/ValidateAudience/ValidateIssuerSigningKey, pins ValidAlgorithms to HmacSha256 only (no 'none'/RS-to-HS confusion since only a symmetric key is registered), sets MapInboundClaims=false consistently with the raw 'email' claim reads everywhere, and uses a 2-min ClockSkew. Jwt:Key is length-validated (>=32 bytes) at startup AND again at issuance (GoogleAuthService.IssueToken) and never falls back to a default key.
  • Jwt:Issuer and Jwt:Audience are committed non-empty defaults ('usage-iq') in appsettings.json, so ValidateIssuer/ValidateAudience are meaningful and issuance uses the same values — no null-issuer bypass.
  • SignalR access_token lifting (Program.cs 281-287) is correctly scoped: ctx.Token is only set when path.StartsWithSegments('/api/hubs'), so normal API routes still require the Authorization header; the query token is also redacted from RequestLog query strings via LogRedaction.SecretPairRegex (access_token is in the SecretKeys list).
  • ChatHub enforces identity (email claim) + DB-backed chat.read/chat.send re-checks (including u.IsEnabled) on connect and on every hub method, and every method independently re-verifies channel membership via ChatEndpoints.IsMemberAsync — MarkRead only advances the cursor and only to a message that belongs to the channel.
  • IngestKeyFilter is sound: SHA-256 hash lookup against non-revoked keys only, no timing side channel of interest (constant work, DB-indexed), server-derives owner email (never trusts client attribution), and the best-effort LastUsed side-write is wrapped so it can never fail the request. The /api/ingest body size is capped (4MB) before model binding runs.
  • PermissionFilter re-reads the user from the DB every request (via CurrentUserAccessor), enforces existence + IsEnabled + ANY-of the required permissions, and returns 403 (not 500) on missing/disabled — no bypass in the ANY-permission logic.
  • IsDefaultable() correctly excludes users.manage, chat.moderate, chat.contacts.manage, all AI keys, all Location keys, Family/cycle keys, chore keys, bills/automations/agents, and the private tool keys; GoogleAuthService.ParseDefaultPermissions re-filters the stored CSV through IsDefaultable so a stray privileged key in the persisted policy can never auto-grant on provision, and PUT /api/access-policy re-filters on write.
  • EmailsRevealed uses CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals for the X-Email-Reveal-Key comparison (constant-time), and the reveal key travels only in a header that RequestLoggingMiddleware does not capture.
  • Swagger UI/JSON is registered only under app.Environment.IsDevelopment(), so the full API schema is not exposed to anonymous callers in production.
  • Admin bootstrap/seed is idempotent and last-admin-protected: admins are promoted to always-enabled with the full catalog, allowlisted emails are create-only viewers, and PUT/DELETE /api/users guard against removing/disabling the last enabled users.manage account inside a Serializable transaction.
  • GoogleAuthService pins each account to its Google subject (bind-on-first-login, reject-on-mismatch), requires EmailVerified + a subject, records denials to the audit log, and issues the JWT with the user's current SessionVersion so a fresh login always carries the up-to-date stamp.
  • Auth-body/secret logging is handled: LogRedaction whole-value-redacts /api/auth, /api/notifications, /api/shares, /api/ingest-keys and /api/family/calendar bodies+queries, and field-redacts token/id_token/access_token/secret/webhook keys elsewhere; the Google ID token and issued JWT are never persisted verbatim.
Endpoints: family / health / identity 36
  • CycleEndpoints: every read/mutation (GET /, POST /period, DELETE /period/{id}, PUT /day-log, DELETE /day-log, PATCH /settings, GET /note) is owner-scoped by `UserEmail == caller.Email`. Delete/clear routes bind BOTH the id/date AND the caller's email in the WHERE (ExecuteDeleteAsync), so a guessed id can never touch another user's period or day-log — the classic IDOR is properly closed.
  • CycleEndpoints private intimate day-log data (Intimacy/Protected/Notes) is confirmed OWNER-ONLY: it is never included in the overlay projection and the AI FactsSummary/PatternFacts deliberately emit only aggregate counts/frequencies (never intimacy, protected status, notes, or a dated row).
  • Cycle /note is correctly double-gated (cycle.track group + family.ai on the route) and always 200s with a deterministic floor; cycle content is not sent to the AI as raw rows.
  • CycleOverlayEndpoints household scoping is sound: memberIds is derived from HouseholdMembers WHERE HouseholdId == caller's own household (resolved via GetForCallerAsync, which only ever resolves the caller's own household by m.UserId == caller.Id). optedInOthers is intersected with memberIds AND requires OverlayToFamily==true, so non-members and non-opted-in members never appear. Only PREDICTED spans are returned; identity is userId+display name, never email. Window is hard-clamped to <=92 days.
  • OverlayToFamily defaults to false (CycleProfile.cs line 32) — sharing predicted spans to family is a genuine explicit opt-in, separate from tracking.
  • MedsEndpoints (meds + vitals): all CRUD is owner-scoped by UserEmail == caller.Email. PUT/DELETE bind id AND caller email; POST /{id}/log and /doses/repeat verify med ownership and stamp UserEmail = caller.Email on every written row (the /doses/repeat IDOR guard — source logs loaded WHERE UserEmail == caller, copies stamped caller — is correct and idempotent).
  • MedsEndpoints inputs are validated + clamped (enum IsDefined checks on Status/Kind/Form, value/date range checks, length truncation, slot clamp). Meds/vitals are gated by tracker.self; vitals /insight additionally checks tracker.ai in-body before any AI call and only sends aggregate stats.
  • IdentityEndpoints: every roles/time/rules mutation is owner-scoped by UserEmail == caller.Email; role/rule ownership is re-verified before attributing time (POST /time, PATCH /time, POST /rules), and DELETE /roles/{id} cascades dependent rows with an owner-scoped WHERE. Import commit + auto/apply validate that every referenced RoleId is in the caller's ownedRoleIds set (foreign-role injection blocked), and re-derive minutes server-side rather than trusting client values.
  • IdentityEndpoints auto-ingest household scoping is correct: chores are restricted to the caller's own household (GetForCallerAsync, read-only, no auto-create) AND ByUserId == caller.Id; workouts are UserEmail == caller. No other member's or household's data can feed the signals. Calendar import reads only the caller's own connected calendar (cal.ListEventsAsync(caller.Id, ...)).
  • Identity role colours are restricted to a fixed server palette (free-text hex rejected), preventing CSS injection into the chart.
  • PermissionFilter re-checks the DB every request (user exists, IsEnabled, holds the required permission) — permission/enabled changes take effect immediately; no reliance on stale JWT claims for authorization.
  • Email scoping key is consistent: JWT email is trimmed+lower-cased in CurrentUserAccessor, and AppUser.Email is stored lower-cased on user creation (UsersEndpoints line 136), so UserEmail equality comparisons cannot be bypassed by case variation.
  • All four endpoint groups are actually mapped in Program.cs (lines 601-604) and every route inherits its group's .RequireAuthorization() + .RequirePermission(...) filter; no route in scope is left ungated.
  • CycleEndpoints: every read and mutation is owner-scoped by caller.Email. DELETE /period/{id} and DELETE /day-log bind the id/date AND UserEmail in the WHERE (ExecuteDelete), so a guessed id can never touch another user's row. PUT /day-log upserts by (UserEmail, LocalDate) and the (UserEmail,LocalDate) unique index backs it. No {id} is trusted as a write key on any cycle path.
  • CycleEndpoints /note: gated by cycle.track AND family.ai; only an AGGREGATE FactsSummary (predictions + counts/frequencies) is sent to Gemini — PatternFacts deliberately omits intimacy/protected/notes and never emits a dated row. Falls back to a deterministic plain floor and always 200s.
  • CycleOverlayEndpoints: household resolved via GetForCallerAsync (caller's OWN household only, read-only, no auto-create); other subjects restricted to memberIds of that household AND CycleProfile.OverlayToFamily == true AND UserId != caller; caller included only if they hold cycle.track. Output is userId + display name (never email) and only PREDICTED spans (raw CyclePeriod rows never leave the server). Window clamped to <=92 days. The known family.use-vs-cycle.track child-visibility gap is a prior-round finding and is excluded.
  • CycleProfile has an IsUnique() index on UserEmail, so the overlay's profiles.ToDictionary(p => p.UserId) cannot throw on a duplicate profile row — GetOrCreateProfileForUpdateAsync cannot create a second profile for the same user.
  • MedsEndpoints: all med/log/vital reads and mutations are owner-scoped by caller.Email. PUT/DELETE meds and vitals bind id AND UserEmail. POST /{id}/log verifies med ownership (id AND UserEmail) before writing, and the log row is stamped UserEmail = caller.Email. POST /doses/repeat loads sources WHERE UserEmail == caller and stamps every copy UserEmail = caller.Email, so no cross-user read or write is possible. Adherence()/insight only ever aggregate the caller's own rows and send AGGREGATE stats to AI.
  • MedsEndpoints /insight: tracker.self gates the route and tracker.ai additionally gates only the AI narration; without tracker.ai or with Gemini off it serves the deterministic plain floor. Only StatsSummary aggregates (adherence %, per-kind avg/min/max/slope) reach the model — never a raw reading, dated row, or note.
  • IdentityEndpoints: every roles/time/rules read and mutation is owner-scoped by caller.Email; POST /time, PATCH /time, POST /rules, import/commit, and auto/apply all re-verify RoleId ownership against the caller before attributing time, and DELETE /roles cascades dependent time-entries + rules with an explicit owner-scoped WHERE. Calendar import/preview reads only the caller's OWN Google calendar (caller.Id). Auto-suggest/apply are self-scoped: workouts by caller.Email, chores restricted to the caller's OWN household AND ByUserId == caller.Id, and minutes are always re-derived server-side (client-supplied minutes are never trusted).
  • IdentityTimeEntry filtered-unique index on (UserEmail, SourceEventId WHERE NOT NULL) correctly backs the idempotent import/auto-apply dedup, and the per-row unique-violation fallback paths detach added entities before retrying, so a concurrent race cannot double-count.
  • CurrentUserAccessor resolves the caller from the JWT email claim, lower-cased; CyclePeriod/CycleProfile/Medication/etc. all store UserEmail lower-cased, so the overlay's email-keyed period grouping (OrdinalIgnoreCase) matches consistently and cannot mismatch on casing.
  • CycleEndpoints: every route (GET /, POST /period, DELETE /period/{id}, PUT /day-log, DELETE /day-log, PATCH /settings, GET /note) is owner-scoped by caller.Email in the WHERE clause; DELETE binds both id AND UserEmail so a guessed id can never touch another user's row. No IDOR on the cycle log itself.
  • CycleEndpoints GET /note narration: only aggregate PatternFacts (counts/frequencies over moods/symptoms/energy) reach Gemini — intimacy, Protected status, and free-text notes are deliberately excluded from FactsSummary/PatternFacts; verified no raw intimate row leaks to the AI.
  • CycleOverlayEndpoints: identity is exposed as userId + DisplayName.Format only, never email; only PREDICTED spans (never raw CyclePeriod rows) are returned; a member with no OverlayToFamily opt-in and no profile row never appears; the caller's own overlay is gated on holding cycle.track. Window is clamped to <=92 days so the range can't be enumerated unbounded.
  • CycleOverlayEndpoints: optedInOthers is intersected with memberIds (current household membership), so a user who LEFT the household but kept OverlayToFamily=true is correctly dropped — the stale CycleProfile flag does not leak once membership ends.
  • MedsEndpoints: all /api/meds and /api/vitals routes are owner-scoped by caller.Email; PUT/DELETE/log all bind id AND UserEmail (cross-user id -> 404/NotFound). RepeatDoses loads sources WHERE UserEmail==caller and stamps every copy with caller.Email, so no cross-user copy is possible.
  • MedsEndpoints GET /vitals/insight: only aggregate stats (adherence %, per-kind avg/min/max/slope) reach Gemini via StatsSummary — never a raw reading, dated row, or note; tracker.ai is re-checked from caller.Permissions before any AI call and the endpoint always 200s with a deterministic floor.
  • MedsVitalsMath.Adherence caps per-day Taken at DosesDueOn(med,day), so duplicate/over-logged rows cannot push the adherence numerator past the day's scheduled doses — the aggregate % is protected even though the underlying rows lack a unique index.
  • IdentityEndpoints: every role/time/rule mutation is owner-scoped by caller.Email; POST /time, PATCH /time, POST /rules, import/commit, and auto/apply all re-validate that the target RoleId is owned by the caller (AnyAsync r.UserEmail==caller.Email) before attributing time, so time can't be written to a foreign role.
  • IdentityEndpoints /suggest and /auto/apply: workout minutes are self-scoped by UserEmail; chore points are restricted to the caller's OWN household (GetForCallerAsync, read-only, no auto-create) AND ByUserId==caller.Id, so no other member's or other household's activity feeds the signal; minutes are re-derived server-side on apply (client-supplied minutes are ignored).
  • IdentityEndpoints import: role colours are restricted to a fixed palette allowlist (free-text hex rejected), preventing CSS injection into the chart; calendar import only proposes roles the caller still owns and dedups via the filtered UNIQUE (UserEmail, SourceEventId) index with per-row unique-violation fallback.
  • FamilyEndpoints: household mutations (rename, add member, remove member, candidate list) are all OWNER-only via IsOwnerAsync; add-member validates the target's permission matches the requested role, rejects 'owner', and enforces one-household-per-user; the owner can't be removed. Leaderboard is household-scoped and exposes only shareable ActivityEvent counts + DisplayName, never email or private metrics.
  • CurrentHouseholdAccessor.GetForCallerAsync only ever resolves the caller's OWN household (by caller.Id membership) and never another user's; there is no endpoint that accepts a household id from the client, so cross-household addressing is structurally impossible in scope.
  • Permission-filter coverage verified: cycle routes gated by CycleTrack (note adds FamilyAi), overlay by FamilyUse, meds/vitals by TrackerSelf (insight AI re-checks TrackerAi), identity by IdentityMap — every mapped route in scope carries RequireAuthorization plus a RequirePermission.
  • AppUser.Email carries a UNIQUE index (UsageDbContext line 221), so the overlay/meds/cycle email keying cannot collide across users; CycleDayLog and CycleProfile have unique (UserEmail,LocalDate)/(UserEmail) indexes enforcing one-row-per-owner.
Injection / committed secrets / config 36
  • SQL access is EF Core / LINQ throughout. The only raw SQL is CostRecomputeService.RecomputeLocalDatesAsync using ExecuteSqlInterpolatedAsync with an interpolated FormattableString ($"...{ianaTimeZone}..."), which EF parameterizes — the timezone is bound as a parameter, not concatenated, so it is NOT SQL-injectable even though the value comes from user input (ApiEndpoints.cs:221).
  • Grep for FromSql/ExecuteSql/FromSqlRaw/ExecuteSqlRaw/string-concatenated SQL across src/Api found no raw string-built SQL; all data access uses parameterized LINQ (Where/Contains IN-lists, ExecuteUpdateAsync with SetProperty).
  • No unsafe deserialization: grep found no BinaryFormatter, JsonConvert/DeserializeObject, or TypeNameHandling in src/Api. JSONL ingestion parses line-by-line via System.Text.Json (safe by default, no polymorphic type resolution).
  • No XXE surface in the API: no XmlReader/XmlDocument/LoadXml/XmlSerializer usage. The OFX/QFX bank import (BankImportParsers.cs) is a hand-rolled tag scanner, not an XML parser, so no external-entity exposure.
  • No command injection: grep found no Process.Start/ProcessStartInfo/new Process anywhere in src/Api.
  • Ingestion file handling is not attacker-path-controlled: JsonlIngestionService enumerates files under server-configured RootPath (Directory.EnumerateFiles with a *.jsonl filter), and ProcessFileAsync opens read-only FileStreams on those enumerated paths — no user-supplied filename is concatenated into a path.
  • ResumeDocumentService temp-file writes (TryWriteTempImage line 803, font read line 1516) use Path.Combine with a Guid-derived name or a fixed Assets/fonts filename — no user-controlled path segment, so no path traversal.
  • IngestWriteService (POST /api/ingest write path) rigorously sanitizes and length-clamps every untrusted field (Sanitize/Clamp/ClampTokens, DedupKey<=300, token ceilings), rejects unknown source kinds (IsKnownSource), derives the reporter identity server-side rather than trusting the client, and uses server-observed public IP — sound untrusted-input hardening.
  • SanitizeMachine strips control chars, '/' and '\' and bounds length before the value is used in the synthetic remote:// file path — mitigates path/label injection via the machine name.
  • JWT signing key is fail-fast: Program.cs lines 254-258 throw on startup if Jwt:Key is missing or < 32 bytes and never falls back to a known key; appsettings.json ships a blank Jwt:Key and prod injects Jwt__Key from SSM (docker-compose.prod.yml line 28).
  • EmailRevealKey comparison uses CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals (constant-time) and returns false when the configured key is empty (fail-safe), and the reveal endpoints are gated by RequirePermission(UsersManage) — the residual risk is solely the committed default value, not the comparison logic or an anonymous path.
  • Secrets in appsettings.json are shipped blank/placeholder for Google, Fitbit, Gemini, WebPush, OpenWeather, and Payments, with real values documented to come from appsettings.Local.json (git-ignored) or SSM env vars; docker-compose.prod.yml confirms these are injected from ${...} SSM-sourced variables.
  • appsettings.Local.example.json contains only placeholder values (YOUR_GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID, GENERATE_A_LONG_RANDOM_SECRET, example.com emails) — no real secret committed there.
  • SQL injection: the only interpolated raw SQL in scope is CostRecomputeService.RecomputeLocalDatesAsync ($"...AT TIME ZONE {ianaTimeZone}..."). It uses ExecuteSqlInterpolatedAsync (parameterized, so the value is bound not concatenated) AND its sole caller (ApiEndpoints.cs PUT /settings, line 209-221) first validates the timezone with TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById and rejects unknown values before calling it. Doubly mitigated — verified clean.
