  • CostRecomputeService.RecomputeCostsAsync uses ExecuteUpdateAsync with strongly-typed SetProperty expressions (no raw SQL, no interpolation) — clean.
  • No SQL injection surface elsewhere in src/Api/Services or src/Api/Data: grep for FromSql/ExecuteSqlRaw/FromSqlRaw/FromSqlInterpolated found no other raw-SQL call sites; all migration migrationBuilder.Sql() calls are static developer-authored DDL/DML with no user input.
  • Unsafe deserialization: every deserializer in scope is System.Text.Json (UsageDbContext MedicationSchedule value converter, MyDataExportService, GeminiService, ResumeEndpoints). No BinaryFormatter, no Newtonsoft TypeNameHandling, no XmlSerializer/XmlReader/XmlDocument/LoadXml anywhere in src/Api — no XXE and no polymorphic-type deserialization vector.
  • Command injection: no Process.Start / ProcessStartInfo / shell invocation anywhere in the API — verified by grep across src/Api.
  • Path traversal in ingestion: JsonlIngestionService.Directory.EnumerateFiles walks source.RootPath (admin-set via SourcesManage) with a fixed *.jsonl mask and reads files read-only (FileShare.ReadWrite, FileAccess.Read); ProjectResolver only derives display labels from paths and never opens attacker-named paths. The remote ingest path never touches the filesystem (synthetic remote:// IngestedFile.Path). No user-controlled path is concatenated into a file open.
  • Discord webhook SSRF (contrast to the already-reported WebPush issue): DiscordNotifier.IsValidWebhook enforces https + a fixed host allowlist (discord.com/discordapp.com/canary/ptb) + /api/webhooks/ path, the poster re-checks the URL at send time, uses an HttpClient with redirects disabled, and re-validates the FINAL response host against the allowlist — a properly hardened SSRF allowlist.
  • TokenProtector: AES-GCM (256-bit, 16-byte tag) with a per-message random 12-byte nonce, key domain-separated via SHA-256 over a labeled string + the app JWT key; Unprotect fails closed (returns null) on tamper/short blob. Cryptographically sound given a strong Jwt:Key (sourced from SSM in prod).
  • IngestWriteService untrusted-payload hardening is otherwise solid: DedupKey/Model/Cwd/SessionId/etc are length-clamped, tokens are Math.Clamp'd to int.MaxValue so CostUsd cannot overflow numeric(18,8), the Source label is server-derived from an allowlisted KindToSource map (client cannot inject an arbitrary Source), and publicIp is server-observed not client-trusted.
  • SanitizeMachine strips control chars and path separators (/ and \) and bounds length before the machine name is used in the synthetic remote:// path and as an attribution label — no path-segment injection via machine name.
  • Committed-secrets posture beyond the already-reported EmailRevealKey/dev-DB-password/AllowedHosts findings: all real secrets (Jwt:Key, Google/Fitbit/Gemini/OpenWeather/WebPush private keys) are blank in the committed appsettings.json and documented to come from git-ignored appsettings.Local.json / SSM env vars; Payments handles are placeholder strings. appsettings.Local.example.json contains only placeholder tokens, not live values.
  • Raw SQL grep across src/Api found exactly one interpolated statement: CostRecomputeService.RecomputeLocalDatesAsync uses ExecuteSqlInterpolatedAsync with the timezone bound as a PARAMETER (not string-concatenated), and its only caller (ApiEndpoints PUT /settings) first validates the value with TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById and rejects unknown zones with a 400 — no SQL injection vector.
  • No FromSqlRaw/ExecuteSqlRaw/FromSqlInterpolated string-concatenation anywhere in src/Api; all other data access is LINQ/EF Core parameterized. All GROUP BY/aggregate queries in UsageQueries are LINQ, not raw SQL.
  • Deserialization uses System.Text.Json exclusively (ResumeEndpoints Deserialize, UsageDbContext MedicationSchedule value converter, MyDataExportService, WebPushSender). No BinaryFormatter, no Newtonsoft TypeNameHandling, no XmlSerializer/XmlDocument/XDocument — no XXE or polymorphic-deserialization surface. Resume DataJson round-trips into a fixed ResumeDataDto with a null-safe try/catch default.
  • Ingestion file handling (JsonlIngestionService) enumerates *.jsonl only under server-owned seeded RootPaths, opens each with FileStream(FileAccess.Read); paths come from Directory.EnumerateFiles, not from user input — no path traversal. ProjectResolver only string-parses cwd into a display name/root, never touches the filesystem with it.
  • Resume headshot temp-file write (ResumeDocumentService.TryWriteTempImage) builds the path from Path.GetTempPath()+a fresh Guid+a whitelisted extension mapped from a fixed MIME switch — attacker cannot influence the filename/extension, no traversal.
  • Finance CSV import (FamilyFinanceEndpoints /import, /import/parse) takes the CSV as an in-body string (req.Content), enforces a UTF-8 byte-size cap, and clamps the filename to 260 chars used only as a label — there is no server-side file path from the client, so no path traversal in the import path.
  • DiscordNotifier.IsValidWebhook enforces https + a fixed host allowlist (discord.com/discordapp.com/canary/ptb) + /api/webhooks/ path, re-checks the FINAL host after the POST, and the 'discord' HttpClient is configured with AllowAutoRedirect=false — SSRF-hardened. RulesEndpoints ApplyWebhook and the per-user webhook both route through DiscordWebhookValidator.IsValid, so automation-rule webhooks cannot target arbitrary hosts. Automation actions are a closed enum with sanitized templates, no user-supplied endpoint.
  • IpGeoService and ReverseGeocodeService both use a FIXED named-client BaseAddress (never user host), URL-escape their query values, and IpGeoService rejects private/loopback/link-local/CGNAT IPv4 and IPv6 ULA/link-local/mapped-private before any lookup — no SSRF/LAN leak. GoogleCalendarService builds every path from a constant PrimaryCalendar plus Uri.EscapeDataString(eventId) — no path/query injection on the fixed Google host. WorkoutX GetGifAsync validates the id to 1-8 digits before interpolation.
  • JWT signing key is fail-fast validated at startup (rejects missing or <32-byte keys, never falls back to a known key). TokenProtector derives a domain-separated SHA-256 key from Jwt:Key and uses AES-GCM with a random 12-byte nonce and 16-byte tag — cryptographically sound at-rest protection (the key-rotation-bricks-tokens tradeoff was already reported).
  • EmailsRevealed uses CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals for a constant-time comparison of the reveal key and treats an empty configured key as 'never reveal'; the key travels only in the X-Email-Reveal-Key header and is not logged. The committed default 'Starbucks' weak secret itself was already reported in prior rounds.
  • ForwardedHeaders trust is bounded to private KnownNetworks (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16) with Kestrel not publicly reachable, so X-Forwarded-For spoofing of the trusted middle of the chain is not possible; CORS uses a single configured origin (default http://localhost:4200), not a wildcard.
  • MyDataExportService is strictly owner-scoped (every query filters by caller email/id) and its Csv() helper correctly quotes fields containing comma/quote/newline AND bare carriage-return (\r) — the bare-\r omission is unique to UsageQueries.Csv and was already reported there. The formula-injection gap in both CSV writers was already reported.
Backend architecture & debt 34
  • Auth pattern is CONSISTENT across endpoint modules: every non-public MapGroup applies .RequireAuthorization() plus a RequirePermission/RequireAnyPermission at the GROUP level (verified FleetEndpoints, InboxEndpoints, PeopleEndpoints, RecipeEndpoints, SavedViewsEndpoints, and the group definitions in Tracker/Ai). PublicEndpoints correctly uses .AllowAnonymous().RequireRateLimiting("share") for its intentionally-anonymous share routes. No mutating endpoint was found lacking an auth/permission gate.
  • PermissionFilter re-checks the DB every request (user exists + IsEnabled + holds any required permission) and returns a consistent 403 JSON shape; CurrentUserAccessor resolves the user per-request by JWT email and caches within request scope, reusing the AppUser the JWT pipeline already loaded. This is a sound, single authorization primitive.
  • API error shapes are consistent: 343 error responses across endpoints use the same new { message = ... } object shape; only 1 BadRequest("raw string") was found. No competing error envelopes (error/detail/title) in the endpoint layer.
  • Visibility gating is already correctly centralized: CanViewAsync and DisplayTzAsync are delegated to the shared Services.TrackerVisibility from both TrackerEndpoints and HardChallengeEndpoints (a genuine single-source-of-truth), so the tracker read-authorization is NOT duplicated even though other timezone callers failed to reuse the same helper.
  • GeminiService keeps a clean layering boundary in one respect: it does NOT take or touch UsageDbContext/DbContext (no db. access, no SaveChangesAsync) — it is purely an HTTP/AI facade, so persistence concerns are correctly kept out of it even though the class is oversized.
  • TODO/FIXME/HACK debt is effectively absent: a grep for TODO|FIXME|HACK|XXX across src/Api surfaced only benign 'for now' documentation notes in XML comments (e.g. one-household-per-user, temporary-location-share semantics), not unfinished-code markers.
  • GeminiService documents and appears to enforce prompt-injection discipline (user text/images embedded strictly as DATA, model JSON parsed and every numeric CLAMPED to sane bounds, fixed non-user-controlled host so no SSRF, key sent only via x-goog-api-key header and never logged) — the AI security posture at the service boundary is sound.
  • FamilyFinanceEndpoints bank-import stage/commit (StageAsync/CommitStagedAsync, lines 561-788): dedup is FITID-authoritative with cross-format content-hash bridging, the commit is a single atomic SaveChanges wrapped in a unique-violation retry (409), and money bounds are enforced against numeric(18,2) overflow — sound.
  • FamilyMealsChores chore approve path (lines 1050-1174): the credit award is guarded by an atomic conditional ExecuteUpdate gated on Status=='submitted' inside an execution-strategy transaction, so exactly one of two concurrent approvers wins and awards credits once; re-approve is idempotent. This is a correct check-then-act-free design and is the reference the Bill claim path should mirror.
  • HardChallenge leaderboard (lines 496-548): batches per-person stats in a constant number of queries and caps the roster at MaxLeaderboardPeople=200 with a viewall guard — no N+1 and no unbounded enumeration (the correct counter-example to the flagged Habit leaderboard).
  • ContactGraph (IsContactAsync/EnsureMutualAsync/BridgeHouseholdAdultAsync): emails normalized lower-case, self-pairs skipped, mutual write idempotent with unique-violation swallow, children excluded from the household auto-bridge — the single source of truth is coherent (its single-directional read correctly relies on the mutual-write invariant already flagged separately).
  • CyclePredictionService is pure/deterministic: avgCycle clamped [15,60], avgPeriod [1,14], OverlaySpans bounded to 24 projected cycles, heavy-flow start confirmation guarded by ConfirmGapDays so it never double-counts — math is sound.
  • PermissionFilter re-reads the DB each request and rejects null/disabled users before the permission check; CurrentUserAccessor lower-cases the email claim and reuses the auth-pipeline-loaded AppUser — consistent identity resolution.
  • AuthEndpoints self-service routes (/profile, /home, /client-info) are strictly self-scoped (matched by caller.Id/Email), sanitize free text, and validate home routes against the caller's own permissions — no IDOR.
  • PublicEndpoints /built-with is aggregate-numbers-only, owner-scoped, anonymous+rate-limited, and cache-safe with no PII on the wire.
  • IngestKeyFilter authenticates the reporter via an unsalted SHA-256 hash of a 32-byte random key matched to a non-revoked row, with a best-effort last-used stamp that never fails ingest — appropriate for a high-entropy machine credential.
  • ChatHub OnConnectedAsync and every mutating hub method re-check IsEnabled + the relevant chat permission against the DB (HasReadAsync/HasSendAsync filter u.IsEnabled), mirroring PermissionFilter — new hub connections by disabled users are aborted (the residual gap is only for already-established connections, noted as a finding).
  • WrappedShareEndpoints scopes every management route to CreatedByEmail == caller.Email; ShareEndpoints management is id-only but correctly gated behind the admin shares.view/shares.manage permissions on a single-owner concept — the difference is intentional, not an IDOR.
  • UsageQueries.WriteRecordsCsvAsync streams via AsAsyncEnumerable() row-by-row (not buffered), and CapturingResponseStream only retains the first 16KB while writing through — the CSV export is not a memory-buffering risk.
  • GlobalExceptionHandler returns a generic 500 with no stack trace; the framework's UseExceptionHandler only invokes it when the response has not started, so a HasStarted guard is not required.
  • PermissionFilter (Auth/PermissionFilter.cs) re-checks the DB every request, enforces IsEnabled, and RequirePermission chaining correctly ANDs multiple filters while RequireAnyPermission ORs — the authorization primitive itself is sound and consistently applied via .RequirePermission on route groups.
  • CurrentUserAccessor (Auth/CurrentUserAccessor.cs) reuses the auth-pipeline-stashed AppUser and otherwise loads by lower-cased email with AsNoTracking + Include(Permissions); no email is exposed on the wire.
  • IdentityEndpoints (Endpoints/IdentityEndpoints.cs) is thoroughly owner-scoped: every POST/PATCH /time and /rules re-verifies the supplied RoleId belongs to the caller (AnyAsync r.UserEmail==caller.Email) before writing, and all reads/deletes filter UserEmail==caller.Email — no cross-user RoleId write is possible.
  • FamilyQuickAddEndpoints dual-auth (ingest-key OR JWT) resolves the acting user server-side from the key's owner (never a client-named user), enforces family.use on both paths, and is rate-limited — no weaker than the JWT-gated family group.
  • FamilyFinance import commit (CommitStagedAsync) is atomic (single SaveChanges), household-scoped, FITID/DedupHash-aware, and converts a unique-index race into a clean retryable 409 — the money-import path is well hardened.
  • Family allowance moves (RecordMoveAsync) are an append-only credit ledger (balance is derived via GroupBy/Sum, not a mutable field), household-scoped, and re-verify the target is a CHILD member of THIS household before writing — no cross-household allowance tampering and no read-modify-write lost update on a balance.
  • HardChallenge writes (POST /, PUT /day, POST /tasks) are keyed strictly to caller.Email (self-only); only READS use the CanViewAsync visibility gate — the write/read authorization split is correct.
  • Bill item assignment verifies the target is a real user AND a mutual contact (ContactGraph.IsContactAsync) before assigning, and the public claim/share DTO never exposes an owner email or de-anonymizing id.
  • MedsEndpoints medication-log upsert binds the new/updated log row to UserEmail==caller.Email after an owner-scoped med lookup; sibling vitals and dose-repeat endpoints all filter UserEmail==caller.Email — the vertical is consistently owner-only.
  • SearchEndpoints independently re-gates every domain by its own permission and re-scopes owner/household/membership exactly as each source endpoint does; sensitive fields (cycle log, bill amounts, locations, emails) are deliberately excluded, and LIKE wildcards are escaped.
  • CurrentHouseholdAccessor auto-provision race is genuinely closed by a real UNIQUE index on HouseholdMember.UserId (UsageDbContext.cs L847) with a catch-on-unique-violation fallback that returns the winning household.
  • ActivityEmitter fire-and-forget correctly opens its OWN DI scope + DbContext (never borrowing the about-to-be-disposed request scope) and call sites pass no CancellationToken, so request completion cannot cancel or corrupt the emit; failures are swallowed and never fail the user-facing action.
  • Discord webhook egress is SSRF-hardened at save time (DiscordWebhookValidator/IsValidWebhook host allowlist) and at send time with a post-redirect final-host re-check; automations rules reuse the same single validator, so there is one definition of a valid webhook.
  • PushEndpoints /subscribe always takes the owner from the JWT (never the body), caps field sizes, and converts a concurrent same-endpoint unique race into an idempotent converging update.
Services: SSRF / secrets / HTTP 33
  • TokenProtector (TokenProtector.cs 22-38): AES-GCM with a fresh 12-byte RandomNumberGenerator nonce per Protect() call, a 16-byte auth tag, and a SHA-256 domain-separated 256-bit key derived from Jwt:Key. No nonce reuse (random per-call), tag length pinned to 16, Unprotect() length-guards (>=28) and returns null on any tamper/decrypt failure. Sound.
  • DiscordNotifier SSRF allowlist (DiscordNotifier.cs 29-33, 176-218): single IsValidWebhook checks https scheme + fixed AllowedHosts set + /api/webhooks/ path; re-validated at send time; the 'discord' HttpClient disables auto-redirect (Program.cs 80-81) AND the code re-checks res.RequestMessage.RequestUri.Host against the allowlist after the call. Both save-time (DiscordWebhookValidator) and send-time use the same allowlist. This is the correct SSRF-hardened pattern.
  • DiscordForwarder (DiscordForwarder.cs 82-159): decrypts per-user/rule webhook only in-memory at send time, re-validates via DiscordWebhookValidator.IsValid before posting, per-user token-bucket rate limit, respects 429 retry-after (capped 10s), logs metadata only (recipient+kind), never the URL or message text. Sound.
  • GoogleCalendarService (GoogleCalendarService.cs): all HTTP targets are compile-time constants (oauth2.googleapis.com token + www.googleapis.com base); refresh token stored AES-GCM-encrypted via TokenProtector, decrypted only in-memory to mint access tokens; client secret + tokens never logged (PostTokenAsync deliberately logs status only, not body); eventId is Uri.EscapeDataString-encoded before path interpolation. No SSRF, no secret leakage.
  • FitbitHealthProvider (FitbitHealthProvider.cs): fixed api.fitbit.com host, encrypted refresh token re-stored on every rotation, token-endpoint body never logged (status only), Basic-auth built from config secret only. Path segments are server-formatted dates, not raw user input. Sound.
  • FatSecretFoodService (FatSecretFoodService.cs) + WeatherService + UsdaOptions/WorkoutXOptions/FatSecretOptions: hosts come from options with fixed defaults (config/admin-controlled, never request input); keys/secrets travel as headers or the appid query param and are never logged; all user free-text (search term, location, barcode) is Uri.EscapeDataString-encoded into query params only, never the host/path. Graceful-null on all failures.
  • GeminiService (GeminiService.cs 5309-5344, 5899-5905): fixed BaseAddress (generativelanguage.googleapis.com), model id sanitized (SanitizeModel: length<=64 + [A-Za-z0-9-._] only, else default) before path interpolation, API key sent only as x-goog-api-key header, key/body never logged (status only), prompt cached by SHA-256 hash. No SSRF or injection.
  • IpGeoService (IpGeoService.cs 32-109): fixed host, and IsPublic() correctly filters loopback/10.0.0.0-8/172.16-31/192.168/169.254/100.64-127 CGNAT plus IPv6 link-local/unique-local/IPv4-mapped before any lookup, so a LAN/loopback IP is never sent upstream. IP is Uri.EscapeDataString-encoded into the path.
  • ReverseGeocodeService (ReverseGeocodeService.cs 42-106): fixed Nominatim host, lat/lng validated to numeric ranges and rounded, invariant-culture formatted into query params (never host), 1 req/s throttle + cache, graceful-null. No SSRF.
  • WorkoutX GIF proxy (WorkoutXService.cs 110-145): the gif id is validated by IsValidGifId (1-8 digits only) before path interpolation, preventing traversal to other resources or an open proxy; host is fixed; key sent only as X-WorkoutX-Key header. The proxy only serves provider GIFs, not arbitrary URLs — not an open proxy.
  • Web Push secret handling (WebPushOptions.cs, WebPushSender.cs): PrivateKey correctly treated as a secret (never returned by /api/push/vapid-public, which exposes only PublicKey), no-op when unconfigured, payload is minimal (title/body/link only, no email/secret), and all send failures are swallowed with metadata-only logs. The secret handling itself is sound — only the endpoint-host validation (the reported finding) is missing.
  • HttpClient timeouts: every outbound named client sets an explicit Timeout (Program.cs: discord ConnectTimeout 5s + 10s CTS in DiscordNotifier, usda/fatsecret/workoutx 15s, gemini 60s, weather 10s, ipgeo 8s, nominatim 10s, google-calendar 15s, fitbit 20s, webpush 15s) and no service does uncapped retries (Gemini retries once with a 900ms delay). No unbounded-retry or missing-timeout defect found.
  • TokenProtector uses AES-GCM with a fresh 12-byte RandomNumberGenerator nonce per Protect() call (no static/reused nonce), a full 16-byte auth tag that is verified on Unprotect, and a domain-separated SHA-256("usage-iq:share-token:"+Jwt:Key) 256-bit key. Unprotect fails closed (returns null) on any CryptographicException/tamper. Nonce-reuse and auth-tag handling are sound.
  • DiscordNotifier.IsValidWebhook enforces https + an explicit host allowlist (discord.com/discordapp.com/canary/ptb) + a /api/webhooks/ path prefix, and is the single definition reused by DiscordWebhookValidator for both save and send. The "discord" HttpClient disables auto-redirect (Program.cs:80-81) AND the poster re-checks the response's final host against the allowlist (DiscordNotifier.cs:213-218) — layered SSRF defense is correct.
  • DiscordWebhookValidator.Hint never emits the full webhook token (only id prefix + last 4 of token) and returns null for invalid/non-Discord URLs.
  • DiscordForwarder decrypts the webhook only in-memory at send time, re-validates it with DiscordWebhookValidator.IsValid before posting, logs metadata only (recipient/kind, never URL or message text), and applies a per-user token-bucket rate limit that a rule-webhook override cannot bypass.
  • Refresh tokens for Google Calendar and Fitbit are stored AES-GCM-encrypted via TokenProtector; the Fitbit rotating refresh token is re-encrypted and re-stored on every refresh; neither the client secret nor the refresh/access token is ever logged or returned (token endpoints log status code only).
  • UsdaFoodService, WorkoutXService, WeatherService, GeminiService, FatSecretFoodService all keep the host fixed (BaseAddress or BaseUrl from non-user config) and never interpolate user input into the host; keys travel as headers or query params and are explicitly never logged (only status codes are logged on failure).
  • WorkoutX GIF id is constrained to digits at the route ({id:int:min(0)}) AND re-validated by IsValidGifId (<=8 digits) before interpolation into the upstream path, preventing path traversal to other resources.
  • IpGeoService.IsPublic rejects loopback/private/CGNAT/link-local IPv4 ranges and IPv6 link-local/unique-local/IPv4-mapped-private before any lookup, so a LAN address is never sent upstream and the lookup can't be steered to internal space via the IP argument.
  • Every outbound service degrades gracefully (returns null/empty, never throws into the request path) and each named client has an explicit Timeout (Gemini 60s, others 8-20s), so a hung upstream can't stall the app indefinitely.
  • WebPushSender/WebPushForwarder send only a minimal { title, body, url } (plus optional relative-path action urls) payload — never a secret, VAPID private key, or email — and prune 404/410 subscriptions; the SurfaceBrowser opt-in is enforced server-side (fail-closed).
  • DiscordNotifier.IsValidWebhook enforces https + a fixed AllowedHosts set (discord.com/discordapp.com/canary/ptb) + a /api/webhooks/ path, and the same allowlist is re-checked at send time; the discord HttpClient sets AllowAutoRedirect=false and the post path re-verifies res.RequestMessage.RequestUri.Host against AllowedHosts — the Discord SSRF surface is well closed.
  • DiscordWebhookValidator delegates to the single DiscordNotifier.IsValidWebhook definition and its Hint() only exposes an 8-char id prefix + last-4 of the token, never the full token.
  • WeatherService, UsdaFoodService, FatSecretFoodService: API keys travel only as query params / Basic-auth headers, are never logged (only the status code is), hosts are fixed BaseAddress/config values, user text is URL-encoded and only read back as data — no injection into host/path.
  • ReverseGeocodeService (Nominatim) uses a fixed https BaseAddress, coordinates are clamped/rounded, a descriptive User-Agent is sent, and a process-wide 1 req/s gate + long cache respect the upstream policy; the lat/lng cannot influence the host.
  • IpGeoService correctly skips loopback/private/link-local/CGNAT/IPv6 ULA/IPv4-mapped-private addresses before any lookup, escapes the IP into the path, caches ~24h, and returns null on every failure (no throw into caller).
  • GeminiService inline_data path takes base64+mime supplied by the caller (server-side), never a fetched URL, so there is no URL-based SSRF; SanitizeModel restricts the model id (which lands in the URL path) to [A-Za-z0-9._-], length<=64, blocking path traversal, and the model comes from config not user input.
  • FatSecret/Fitbit token exchanges send the client secret in a Basic Authorization header (stripped by the framework on cross-origin redirect), and none of the food/health services log request bodies or secrets.
  • AuditLogger.Detail is only ever fed non-secret metadata at its call sites (fleet revoke counts, ingest key name+prefix) — no webhook URLs or tokens are audit-logged.
  • WebPushSender sends only title/body/link (no secrets/email), enforces the server-side SurfaceBrowser opt-in (fail-closed), prunes 404/410 subscriptions, and swallows all errors; the VAPID private key stays in options and is never logged.
  • DiscordForwarder decrypts webhook URLs only in-memory at send time, re-validates them with DiscordWebhookValidator before posting, applies a per-user token-bucket rate limit even on the rule-webhook override path, and logs metadata only.
  • GoogleCalendarService free/busy and family-events paths use each member's own minted token and expose only userId+name+times, never emails; SafeReadBodyAsync caps error-body reads at 4 KB and CaptureCalendarError only surfaces secret-free classifications.
Endpoints correctness & validation 33
  • Transaction usage vs the EF retry execution strategy is correct everywhere it appears: UsersEndpoints.cs (PUT/DELETE user, lines 162-208), TrackerEndpoints.cs (move-day weight/activity re-date, lines 1447-1512 and 1285-1288), and FamilyMealsChoresEndpoints.cs (chore claim, lines 1085-1089) all obtain db.Database.CreateExecutionStrategy() and open BeginTransactionAsync INSIDE strategy.ExecuteAsync — no bare user-initiated BeginTransaction under EnableRetryOnFailure was found in src/Api/Endpoints.
  • Pagination bounds are consistently clamped: AgentInboxEndpoints (Math.Clamp(limit??100,1,200)), FeedEndpoints (Math.Clamp(limit??50,1,100)), LocationEndpoints (Math.Clamp(limit??100,1,HistoryCap=500)), TrackerEndpoints weight window (Math.Clamp(days,1,365)), UsageQueries.RecordsAsync (page=Math.Max(1,page); pageSize=Math.Clamp(pageSize,1,500)), and FamilyFinance /transactions (pageNum = page>0 ? page : 1). No negative Skip or unbounded Take on user input was found.
  • Integer/enum parsing is safe: every enum is parsed via Enum.TryParse with a defined fallback (TrackerEndpoints profile block lines 684-716) or a dedicated TryParse* helper that returns 404/400 (AgentsEndpoints.TryParseKind, NudgeEndpoints.TryParseKind, FamilyCalendarEndpoints.TryParseRecurrence). int/double/DateOnly parsing uses TryParse throughout — no bare int.Parse/DateOnly.Parse-that-throws on request input.
  • DateTime/DateOnly UTC handling is deliberate: DisplayTz conversions use TimeZoneInfo.ConvertTimeFromUtc against the configured display zone (TrackerEndpoints weight history), FamilyPollsEndpoints forces DateTimeKind.Utc on inbound option times (lines 115-116), and FamilyCalendarEndpoints SpecifyKind(Utc) on recurrence/window bounds (lines 635,649,664). No local/UTC mixing bug surfaced.
  • Date range validation guards fat-fingered years: CycleEndpoints.DateOutOfRange (-5y/+1y), HabitEndpoints.DateOutOfRange, and HardChallengeEndpoints start-date range check (lines 147-148) all reject out-of-range dates with a 400 before persisting, and period logging rejects EndDate < StartDate (CycleEndpoints.Validate lines 284-290).
  • Money/overflow validation is thorough: FamilyFinanceEndpoints bounds every signed amount against MoneyCap=1e14 and rounds to 2dp before hitting numeric(18,2) (balance entry lines 1802-1803, savings contribute lines 2052-2054), and BillEndpoints.ClampMoney clamps to [0,1_000_000] — guarding against a 500-on-overflow.
  • String truncation on user input is uniformly guarded with an explicit length check before the range operator (title[..200], text[..500], name[..64], etc.) across FamilyPolls, FamilyQuickAdd, FamilyRemindersTimers, Notifications, Cycle, Resume — no unchecked Substring/[..n] that could throw ArgumentOutOfRange.
  • File-upload / base64 validation in ResumeEndpoints checks mime allow-list, strips the data-URL prefix, applies a cheap pre-decode length bound, wraps Convert.FromBase64String in try/catch(FormatException) returning 400, and re-checks the decoded byte length against GeminiService.MaxImageBytes (lines 448-510) — no unhandled FormatException or size-bypass.
  • Anonymous ingest surface (IngestEndpoints POST /api/ingest) validates source against an allow-list, returns 200 on empty rows, 400 when Rows.Count exceeds MaxRowsPerBatch, and attributes rows to the key-owner rather than any client-supplied user — the client public IP is not trusted (server RemoteIpAddress used instead).
  • Public bill/share read paths return an indistinguishable 404 for missing-or-disabled tokens and a 409 for settled bills (BillEndpoints lines 283,296-298), so status codes correctly avoid leaking token existence while distinguishing terminal states.
  • FamilyFinance transactional commit (CommitStagedAsync) does all account find-or-create + transaction inserts + batch flip + staged-row removal in ONE SaveChangesAsync (implicit EF transaction, retry-strategy-safe) and correctly catches unique violations to return a retryable 409; the FITID/DedupHash guards are consistent between stage and commit.
  • All user-initiated multi-statement transactions in scope (UsersEndpoints, TrackerEndpoints move-day, FamilyMealsChores) correctly wrap BeginTransactionAsync inside db.Database.CreateExecutionStrategy().ExecuteAsync, honoring the Npgsql EnableRetryOnFailure execution-strategy rule.
  • FamilyFinance money inputs (budget/savings limits, balance entries, contributions) are bounded by TryNormalizeLimit/MoneyCap (1e14) against the numeric(18,2) column, and the savings contribute path bounds BOTH the single amount and the resulting running total before persisting — no numeric-overflow 500.
  • FamilyFinance month parsing (TryParseMonth) uses DateTime.TryParseExact 'yyyy-MM' with InvariantCulture and produces a [from, toExclusive) half-open window; DateOnly comparisons are used throughout, avoiding UTC/local drift on the ledger reads.
  • Bill owner CRUD, item, assign, and settle endpoints are consistently owner-scoped via OwnedBillAsync/OwnedItemAsync (email + id), item delete is scoped by a correlated Bills.Any subquery, and money is clamped via ClampMoney to [0, 1e6]@2dp.
  • Journal PUT /day and POST /{id}/copy both correctly catch the unique (email, date) violation, clear the change tracker, reload the winner, and re-apply the partial fields — the exact pattern CycleEndpoints is missing.
  • MedsEndpoints and CycleEndpoints enum inputs are validated with Enum.IsDefined (MedicationLogStatus, MedicationForm, VitalKind) and clamp numeric/date inputs (Value1/Value2 range vs numeric(8,2), slot clamp, date-range guards) before persistence.
  • HardChallenge viewall roster is capped at MaxLeaderboardPeople (200) and the leaderboard uses batched constant-query stat loading (ComputeStatsBatchAsync) rather than per-user N+1, and confessions are nulled for non-owner viewers.
  • Nudge endpoint validates the fixed kind before any existence check (400), rejects self/unknown targets as a flat 404 (no existence leak), and gates on ChatSend + the chat rate-limit policy; the per-(sender,target) cooldown is a check-then-act but is a low-impact anti-spam control already backstopped by the rate limiter.
  • FamilyFinanceEndpoints commit path (CommitStagedAsync) correctly wraps the account-create + deduped transaction inserts + batch flip + staged-row removal in a SINGLE SaveChangesAsync and catches Postgres unique-violation (23505) to return a clean retryable 409 — atomic and race-safe.
  • FamilyFinanceEndpoints money amount inputs (budgets, savings targets, balance snapshots, contributions incl. running total) are bounded by MoneyCap (1e14) with clean 400s, preventing numeric(18,2) overflow into unhandled 500s.
  • FamilyFinanceEndpoints /ai/money-coach, /ai/summary and /ai/budget-check cache keys omit finance.ai but the non-finance.ai (floor) path returns BEFORE the cache is read/written, so only AI-enriched DTOs are ever cached — no cross-permission cache poisoning.
  • FamilyFinanceEndpoints NetWorthDirectionAsync correctly compares the two filtered non-zero trend points (not the raw tail), avoiding a spurious up/down when the latest month nets to exactly 0.
  • FamilyMealsChoresEndpoints chore approve uses a conditional ExecuteUpdate gated on Status=='submitted' inside the retrying execution strategy's transaction, so exactly one concurrent approver wins the flip and credits are awarded exactly once (loser short-circuits to the idempotent OK).
  • TrackerEndpoints move-day weight/activity re-date correctly wraps delete-target + re-date-source in db.Database.CreateExecutionStrategy().ExecuteAsync with BeginTransaction/Commit — atomic and retry-safe; the day-builder commit likewise wraps its multi-entity batch in the strategy.
  • UsersEndpoints transaction usages open Serializable transactions inside CreateExecutionStrategy().ExecuteAsync (correct pattern under EnableRetryOnFailure).
  • CycleEndpoints is strictly owner-scoped (every read/write/delete binds UserEmail == caller.Email), validates dates via DateOutOfRange (5y back / 1y forward), normalizes mood/symptoms to fixed vocab, clamps energy, and enum-guards CycleFlowLevel — input validation is thorough.
  • IdentityEndpoints role/time/rule mutations are consistently owner-scoped by email, clamp Minutes to 1..1440, restrict role colours to a fixed palette (rejecting free-text hex), and enum-map source safely; calendar-import commit has a per-row unique-violation fallback.
  • LocationEndpoints NormalizeSource defaults any unknown source to 'manual' (only an exact 'login' string bypasses the opt-in gate), and PATCH /settings correctly requires location.share before enabling household broadcast.
  • ShareEndpoints and WrappedShareEndpoints hash tokens with SHA-256, store AES-GCM-encrypted copies, are 404-indistinguishable on invalid/expired links, bake owner/window/whitelist server-side (holder can't widen), and wrap best-effort access recording in try/catch so a write error never 500s a valid public read.
  • HealthEndpoints sync-now advances the cursor only to the last day actually pulled Ok (never jumping past skipped days on a mid-window RateLimited/AuthExpired break), and never leaks the client secret/refresh token.
  • PactEndpoints invite/join/leave enforce mutual-contact anti-spam, forbid a Left member from silently re-activating via join (Conflict), and gate progress reads to participants (404 otherwise).
  • NudgeEndpoints validates the fixed kind first (400), rejects self/stranger with a flat non-leaking 404, honours a per-(sender,target) cooldown, and carries no free-text into notifications.
Frontend correctness 33
  • app.ts (the always-mounted shell) is exemplary: every long-lived RxJS stream (timer-based sync/presence/me/userCount polls, router.events NavigationEnd) is guarded with takeUntilDestroyed(); the ResizeObserver is disconnected via DestroyRef.onDestroy; all polls wrap the inner API call in catchError(() => of(fallback)) so a transient error never kills the stream; presence/online lists are coalesced to [] so a null body can't make a signal non-iterable.
  • auth.interceptor.ts correctly attaches the bearer only to /api requests, rethrows via throwError, and bounces to /login on 401 while excluding /api/auth/* to avoid a logout loop on the login call itself.
  • shared/chart.ts (ECharts wrapper) disposes correctly: ngOnDestroy cancels the pending rAF, disconnects the ResizeObserver, and calls chart.dispose(); the resize handler is rAF-debounced to avoid ResizeObserver-loop errors.
  • location/location-map.ts (Leaflet wrapper) disposes correctly: ngOnDestroy disconnects the ResizeObserver and calls map.remove(); popup titles/subtitles are HTML-escaped; a fitTo before init is deferred and flushed once ready.
  • family/family-locations.ts cleans up thoroughly: ngOnDestroy clears the 60s clock interval, removes the matchMedia change listener, and cancels the rAF playback loop; the Api .subscribe() calls are on HttpClient observables that complete after one emission (no leak).
  • chat/chat.ts is disciplined: presence poll, queryParamMap, and the 1s share-heartbeat all use takeUntilDestroyed(); ngOnDestroy flushes typing state and stops the position-push interval; late-response guards (selectedId re-check) prevent cross-channel state bleed after rapid navigation.
  • chat-realtime.ts manages the SignalR lifecycle safely: start() re-checks the auth guard after the async dynamic import to handle a concurrent stop()/logout race; stop() nulls the connection, clears all typing timers, and resets every cached signal so the next user builds a fresh connection with no cross-user leakage; refreshChannels()/refreshInbox() coalesce null bodies to [] and never fire the live-notification surface (so history is not replayed as toasts).
  • pulse-ticker/pulse-ticker.store.ts ref-counts consumers via attach(DestroyRef) and clears both the poll interval and the notification debounce on the last detach; refresh() throttles to avoid a poll+notification double-fetch and swallows errors via catchError.
  • tracker-beta/state/optimistic-tracker.ts uses firstValueFrom throughout; the MatSnackBar onAction()/afterDismissed() subscriptions complete when the snackbar closes; deferred deletes are correctly gated on the undo flag so a tapped Undo never fires the server delete.
  • tracker/tracker.ts read-only auto-refresh interval is created inside an effect keyed on viewUser(), stopped on every effect re-run, and additionally cleared via DestroyRef.onDestroy; all dialog .afterClosed().subscribe() calls are on completing observables.
  • widget/widget.ts and people.ts both guard their timer-based polls with takeUntilDestroyed() and wrap inner API calls in catchError.
  • viewport.ts and sw-update.ts install matchMedia/SwUpdate listeners in providedIn:'root' singletons that live for the app lifetime, so the absence of teardown is acceptable (not a leak).
  • challenge-store.ts and (spot-checked) tracker-store.ts are pure signal stores using firstValueFrom with try/catch/finally; no dangling subscriptions.
  • auth.interceptor.ts: the 401 bounce correctly excludes /api/auth/* (so the login/config/google calls don't self-logout) and rethrows the error untouched; catchError is scoped and does not leak. Sound.
  • location-map.ts (Leaflet wrapper): ngOnDestroy disconnects the ResizeObserver and calls map.remove(); marker/trail layers are cleared via clearLayers() on every render, so no marker/listener accumulation. Leaflet is disposed correctly.
  • chart.ts (ECharts wrapper): ngOnDestroy cancels the pending rAF, disconnects the ResizeObserver, and disposes the chart instance; the resize is rAF-debounced to avoid ResizeObserver-loop errors. Disposal is sound.
  • app.ts: every long-lived timer()/router.events subscription is guarded with takeUntilDestroyed(); the barRO ResizeObserver is disconnected via DestroyRef.onDestroy. HostListeners are declarative (auto-removed). No shell-level subscription leak found.
  • family/finance.ts: all ~10 HTTP subscriptions pipe catchError(...) AND takeUntilDestroyed(this.destroyRef), and coalesce null bodies (?? []). Disciplined error handling and teardown.
  • chat-realtime.ts: stop() clears every typing timer and resets all cache signals; start() re-checks the auth/connection guard after the async dynamic import() to defeat the logout/concurrent-start race; refreshInbox/loadPreferences run outside the connection try/catch and swallow their own errors so a REST blip can't tear down a healthy hub. _liveNotification is set ONLY in onReceiveNotification, so backlog is never replayed as toasts.
  • location-capture.ts: periodic setInterval self-cancels on disable/permission-denied inside the tick, startPeriodic() always stopPeriodic()s first (idempotent), and PERMISSION_DENIED latches to stop re-prompting. No timer leak; privacy gating is enforced client-side.
  • offline.interceptor.ts: correctly queues ONLY on HttpErrorResponse status===0 (true network failure, bytes never left) and rethrows all real 4xx/5xx untouched; the queueable allowlist is narrow and append-safe, and Authorization is stripped before persisting. The status===0 safety hinge is sound.
  • pulse-ticker.store.ts: ref-counted attach/detach clears the poll interval and the notify-debounce timeout on last detach; refresh() is throttled to 3s and the feed subscribe pipes catchError. No leaked interval when the dashboard unmounts.
  • auth.guard.ts / permission enforcement: guard returns a UrlTree redirect (not a raw boolean) preserving returnUrl; isAuthenticated() is expiry-checked. Sound.
  • notification-bell.ts: showToast's ref.onAction().subscribe completes when the snackbar dismisses (finite), and browser Notification construction is wrapped in try/catch. Only the unbounded surfaced Set (reported) is a concern.
  • ChartComponent (src/Web/src/app/shared/chart.ts) disposes correctly: ngOnDestroy cancels the pending rAF, disconnects the ResizeObserver, and calls chart.dispose(); the resize is rAF-debounced to avoid ResizeObserver loop errors. No echarts leak.
  • LocationMap (src/Web/src/app/features/location/location-map.ts) ngOnDestroy disconnects its ResizeObserver and calls map.remove(); Leaflet layers/markers are re-created via clearLayers on each render, so no marker/listener accumulation across renders.
  • App shell (src/Web/src/app/app.ts) uses takeUntilDestroyed() on every long-lived timer/router-events subscription (the 15s clock, syncStatus, /me poll, presence, userCount) and disconnects barRO via DestroyRef.onDestroy — the polling observables are correctly torn down.
  • FamilyLocations (src/Web/src/app/features/family/family-locations.ts) ngOnDestroy clears its clock setInterval, removes the matchMedia change listener, and cancels the rAF playback loop; its .subscribe() calls are one-shot completing HTTP requests, so they do not leak.
  • finance.ts (src/Web/src/app/features/family/finance.ts) consistently applies takeUntilDestroyed(this.destroyRef) to its RxJS subscriptions (10 occurrences verified).
  • ChatRealtime.stop() thoroughly clears all cached signal state (channels/messages/typing/locationShares/unread/notifications/prefs/liveNotification) and clears all typing safety timers, so a logout leaves no stale cross-user chat state or dangling setTimeout handles.
  • AuthService.applyMe/applyProfilePrefs/applyHomeRoute all guard on a null session before writing, so an in-flight /me poll resolving after logout cannot resurrect a session.
  • offline-queue.ts flush() correctly strips any snapshotted Authorization header and re-attaches a fresh bearer at replay time, and all IndexedDB access is feature-detected and guarded to degrade to a no-op.
  • NotificationBell live-surface effect reads preferences() freshly each fire and dedupes by notification id; the liveNotification wrapper's seq re-fires the effect for repeated ids as intended.
Test coverage & quality 32
  • Ingest-key auth is thoroughly tested (tests/Ccusage.Api.Tests/Integration/IngestIntegrationTests.cs): no-key and bogus-key -> 401, revoked key rejected on next request, unknown source -> 400, malformed/oversized rows fail-soft not fatal, and attribution is server-derived from the key OWNER (never a client-supplied value).
  • IDOR / ownership scoping on ingest keys is covered: reporter.self sees/deletes ONLY their own keys, cannot delete another user's key (403), while reporter.manage sees/deletes all (IngestIntegrationTests Reporter_self_sees_and_deletes_only_their_own_keys / Reporter_manage_sees_and_deletes_all_keys).
  • JWT / session-version (force-logout) invalidation is well tested (AuthIntegrationTests): stale sv token -> 401, freshly-stamped sv accepted, missing-sv token accepted while SessionVersion==0 but rejected after a force-logout, tampered/wrong-key token -> 401, unknown user -> 403, disabled user revoked on next request.
  • Email-privacy masking is directly and adversarially tested (EmailVisibilityIntegrationTests + fleet/ingest/share/log tests): GET /api/users and /api/audit mask OTHER users' emails to null without the reveal key, caller always sees their own, and payloads are asserted to contain no '@' / no email at all; request log and shares carry userId+name, never email.
  • Cross-household IDOR / household scoping is covered on the backend: family manage endpoints require family.use (403 otherwise), non-owner members cannot add members or rename, cannot add an owner of another household, and FamilyRemindersTimerTests explicitly asserts cross-household isolation; foreign ids are silent no-ops / 404 across AgentInbox, Chat, ChoreMarketplace, FamilyMealsChores.
  • Health-data (cycle) privacy scoping is tested (CycleTests): the family overlay requires family.use, returns only opted-in members' predicted spans, and excludes non-opted members; FamilyLocations similarly excludes in-household-but-not-sharing members.
  • Negative/authz tests are pervasive and per-permission (AuthIntegrationTests): view-vs-manage splits, any-of permission routes, export-only unlocks CSV only, Google sign-in subject pinning (different subject for a bound email -> 403), open-signup provisioning, and last-administrator protection.
  • Test isolation against shared-state flake is handled deliberately: a single WebAppFactory is shared via one xUnit collection because Program.cs reads config from process-global env vars (documented in IntegrationCollection), each test provisions fresh Guid-suffixed users for order-independence, and the auth rate-limit partition cap is raised so the serialized suite doesn't 429.
  • Testcontainers startup is sound: one PostgreSQL container started once in InitializeAsync before env vars are set and disposed in DisposeAsync; external HTTP (Discord webhook, Web Push) is rerouted to in-memory capturing handlers so no network is hit and sends are deterministic.
  • Background timer logic is tested deterministically rather than with sleeps: the production FamilyReminderService loop is removed in the test host and TickAsync is invoked directly with a fixed clock (FamilyRemindersTimerTests.RunTick), avoiding time/Task.Delay flakiness for the reminder/timer path.
  • No assert-nothing tests observed in the integration files reviewed: every test makes concrete status-code and/or payload assertions; the .NET suite is broad (unit parsers/pricing/redaction plus ~90 integration classes).
  • Ingest-key auth is thoroughly tested: no-key 401, bogus-key 401, revoked-key 401 on next request, unknown-source 400, malformed-row drop, overflow clamp, key-owner-driven attribution, reporter.self owner-scoping (can't delete another's key -> 403), reporter.manage sees all, and adversarial no-email-on-wire checks (IngestIntegrationTests).
  • JWT/session-version (force-logout) coverage is strong: tampered-key token -> 401, unknown user -> 403, disabled-user immediate revoke, stale sv rejected while fresh sv accepted, missing-sv accepted at v0 but rejected after a bump, force-logout gated by users.manage + audited (AuthIntegrationTests).
  • Email-masking privacy is well covered server-side: GET /api/users and /api/audit mask other users' emails to null without the reveal key, keep the caller's own real, wrong key still masks, correct key reveals; plus adversarial raw-body no-'@' checks across Fleet, ingest-key list, cycle overlay, family, reminders, and location-share payloads.
  • IDOR / household + owner scoping is exercised across features: cycle periods/day-logs are owner-scoped (cross-user read/delete/upsert isolation, another user's delete -> 404), family household add/rename/remove gated by owner role and cross-household reminder/timer isolation returns 404 (existence not leaked), chat location-share restricted to conversation participants (404 for outsiders).
  • Google sign-in identity binding is covered: invalid token -> 401, unprovisioned+open-signup-off -> 403, subject pinning (a different Google subject for a bound email -> 403), open-signup auto-provision with default perms, and audit rows for auto-provision / disabled-user denial.
  • Test host isolation is deliberately engineered and sound: single shared WebAppFactory/collection to avoid process-global env-var clobbering, per-test fresh users via Guid emails for order-independence, and network side effects (Discord, Web Push) rerouted to in-memory capturing handlers.
  • The action-log redaction tests assert real secret-suppression: auth bodies -> [redacted], share tokens -> [redacted], Discord webhook URL -> [redacted], and query-string access_token -> [redacted] with raw-secret absence checks.
  • The FamilyReminderService background tick is driven deterministically (TickAsync with an injected 'now' and the hosted loop removed in the factory), avoiding real timer flake; no-double-fire and recurrence-advance are asserted.
  • Ingest-key auth is thoroughly tested: no key -> 401, bogus key -> 401, revoked key rejected on next request, unknown source -> 400, malformed rows dropped not fatal, oversized token clamped not a poison pill, and server-derived owner attribution (never client-supplied) is asserted (IngestIntegrationTests.cs).
  • Ingest key management authz is well covered: creation/list/delete gated by reporter.self vs reporter.manage vs settings.manage, self-owner can only see/delete own keys (A cannot delete B's key -> 403), manager sees/deletes all — a genuine IDOR/ownership matrix (IngestIntegrationTests.cs 322-426).
  • JWT/session-version invalidation is deeply tested: force-logout bumps SessionVersion + audits it; a stale sv=0 token -> 401 after logout while a fresh sv=1 -> 200; a token with NO sv claim is accepted while version is 0 but rejected after a bump (no mass-logout on deploy, correct revocation). Tampered-key token -> 401 (AuthIntegrationTests.cs 249-313, 34-40).
  • Email-masking privacy is directly and adversarially tested on GET /api/users and GET /api/audit: masks OTHER users to null without the X-Email-Reveal-Key, keeps the caller's own real, wrong key still masks, correct key reveals all; plus 'no @ anywhere on the wire' adversarial checks on fleet, ingest-keys, household, candidates, cycle-overlay, and location-share payloads (EmailVisibilityIntegrationTests.cs, and NotContain("@") assertions across the suite).
  • Household/IDOR scoping is strong: add-member requires owner role, cross-household add rejected (user already in a household -> 400), can't add a non-family.use user, can't remove the owner, non-owner can't rename/add, candidates never include the caller and drop out once joined (FamilyIntegrationTests.cs).
  • Cycle (health/PII) owner-scoping is rigorously tested: a caller only sees/deletes their OWN periods and day-logs (cross-user delete -> 404 with count unchanged, cross-user upsert writes only the caller's row), the family overlay surfaces ONLY opted-in members' predicted spans and never leaks intimate/mood/notes/symptoms/email, and cycle endpoints require cycle.track (403) + auth (401) (CycleTests.cs).
  • GPS location-share authz + IDOR is tested: start requires both location.self AND chat.send, view requires chat.read, only conversation participants can see/start/update/stop (non-participant -> 404, existence not leaked), expired/stopped shares reject updates, and payloads carry userId/name not email (ChatLocationShareTests.cs).
  • The IP trust boundary (spoofing defense) is tested: multi-hop X-Forwarded-For unwinds only through trusted private proxies to the leftmost public client, and with no forwarded header falls back to the observed peer — a real regression guard against a spoofable client IP (ForwardedHeadersIntegrationTests.cs).
  • Google sign-in identity pinning is covered: unprovisioned email forbidden when open-signup off (and NOT audited to prevent log-flooding), auto-provision binds the Google subject and rejects a different subject reusing the email, disabled-user login is audited (AuthIntegrationTests.cs 387-566, 776-815).
  • Action-log privacy/redaction is tested end-to-end: auth-route bodies -> [redacted], query-string secrets (access_token) redacted, Discord webhook + share tokens redacted and never appear raw, health/polling routes excluded, and the log carries userId+name never userEmail (AuthIntegrationTests.cs 568-724).
  • Testcontainers startup flakiness is deliberately mitigated: a single shared WebAppFactory/container across a serialized xUnit collection (avoids parallel env-var clobbering and duplicate containers), the auth rate-limit partition is raised to 100000 to avoid shared-partition 429 flakes, and background timers (FamilyReminderService) are removed so per-tick counts are deterministic (WebAppFactory.cs).
  • Async-log/notification assertions use bounded polling loops (WaitForLog ~7.5s, AiUsageLog/Habit 50x100ms) that fail with a clear message on timeout rather than a bare fixed sleep — a reasonable pattern for eventually-consistent hosted writers (AuthIntegrationTests.cs 571-581, AiUsageLogIntegrationTests.cs 279-285).
  • PresenceTracker unit tests are real assertions (online/offline windowing, email lowercasing/collapse, re-touch refresh, ordering, blank-input rejection) — not assert-nothing (PresenceTrackerTests.cs); the concurrency hardening test fires 8 simultaneous first-reads and asserts none 500 on the unique-violation race (Hardening_InboxPrefRaceTests.cs).
Frontend security 31
  • Markdown-to-HTML rendering (src/Web/src/app/features/family/markdown.ts) is the single trust boundary behind every [innerHTML]/bypassSecurityTrustHtml use (family notes, note-editor preview, family-notes-mobile). It HTML-escapes the entire source FIRST (& < > " ') then applies regex transforms that only emit a fixed tag set, and its link rule runs URLs through safeUrl() which allows only http/https/mailto or /,# and drops javascript:/data:. Every bypassSecurityTrustHtml call I traced feeds renderMarkdown output, so the DomSanitizer bypass is sound.
  • auth.interceptor.ts attaches the bearer token ONLY when req.url.startsWith('/api') (relative same-origin path), so the token is never sent to third-party/cross-origin absolute URLs — no token-leak-via-interceptor issue.
  • auth.guard / permissionGuard / anyPermissionGuard correctly check isAuthenticated() (with expiry) and hasPermission before allowing routes, redirecting to /login (with returnUrl) or /welcome; client-side enforcement is backed by the server RequirePermission filter, so these guards are UX-layer only as intended.
  • Open-redirect vectors are contained: signin returnUrl and all navigateByUrl callers (inbox/search/notification deepLinks, pwa notification target) route through the Angular Router, which resolves values as in-app paths (never a network navigation to an attacker origin). pwa.onNotificationClick additionally requires a leading '/'.
  • All target="_blank" anchors across the SPA (20 occurrences in 16 files) carry rel="noopener" (several rel="noopener noreferrer"), including the user-controlled bill payment [href] links — no reverse-tabnabbing exposure.
  • No eval / new Function anywhere in src/app. The only postMessage/message listener (offline-queue.ts) reads solely a string 'type' field from the service worker to trigger a queue flush — no origin-trust or data-injection risk.
  • Fitbit OAuth (settings-health/fitbit-oauth.ts) uses PKCE S256 with a high-entropy random state persisted in sessionStorage and strictly validated on callback (returnedState !== storedState -> reject), and strips code/state from the URL via history.replaceState so a refresh can't replay the code — CSRF and code-replay are handled.
  • Admin email-privacy: the decrypt key for the Users table lives in component memory only (users.ts:295, users-mobile.page.ts:949) and is explicitly never written to localStorage or a URL, matching the email-privacy rule.
  • sensitive-data-in-localStorage review: apart from the session token/permissions (reported), localStorage holds only non-sensitive UI state (theme/scheme, platform override, tour-seen flags, one-time AI privacy-notice flags, hub/widget layout, command-palette recents, offline-disabled flag) and the pantry hand-off in sessionStorage — no PII/health/payment payloads persisted.
  • auth.interceptor.ts: token is attached only when req.url.startsWith('/api') AND a valid token exists; '//api.evil.com' does NOT satisfy startsWith('/api') (2nd char '/' != 'a'), and all API calls use relative same-origin paths (verified: no absolute apiBase/environment URL config exists in core/), so the bearer cannot leak cross-origin.
  • offline.interceptor.ts: on replay-snapshot it explicitly strips the Authorization header (case-insensitive) so a stale token is never persisted into IndexedDB; the queue re-attaches a fresh bearer at flush time.
  • markdown.ts renderMarkdown: escape-first design is sound — escapeHtml runs on the whole source before any inline/block rule, all emitted tags are a fixed known-safe set, and the link branch rejects javascript:/data: schemes; no attribute-breakout path found in the bold/italic/code/strike/heading/list handlers.
  • DomSanitizer.bypassSecurityTrustHtml is used ONLY on renderMarkdown() output (notes.ts, note-editor-dialog.ts, family-notes-mobile.page.ts); no bypassSecurityTrustUrl/ResourceUrl/Script anywhere and no raw user string is ever passed to bypassSecurityTrustHtml.
  • Chat (the largest user-authored surface) renders message text via interpolation only — no innerHTML, no renderMarkdown, no bypassSecurity — so chat is not an XSS sink.
  • All [innerHTML] bindings in the SPA (notes, note-editor-dialog, family-notes-mobile) bind either renderMarkdown output or a pre-sanitized SafeHtml; no direct [innerHTML]=userString found.
  • All target=_blank anchors carry rel=noopener (most also noreferrer); no reverse-tabnabbing gap. All [href]/[src] bindings (avatars, mapUrl, weatherIconUrl, photoUrl data: images) rely on Angular's built-in URL/resource sanitization.
  • PwaService.onNotificationClick and ShareTargetPage both correctly require target.startsWith('/') before router.navigateByUrl, preventing cross-origin/scheme redirects from push payloads and Web-Share params.
  • Fitbit OAuth (fitbit-oauth.ts) uses PKCE S256 (no client secret in the SPA), a high-entropy state CSRF token validated on callback, sessionStorage (not localStorage) for the verifier, one-shot clearing of verifier/state, and history.replaceState to strip ?code so a refresh can't replay a spent code.
  • offline-queue.ts serviceWorker 'message' listener only reacts to two fixed message-type strings and only triggers a local flush(); SW messages are same-origin by spec, so no origin-spoof risk, and no data from the message is trusted.
  • localStorage contents audited: only the (already-reported) session plus non-sensitive UI prefs (theme, scheme, platform mode, tour-seen flags, command-palette recent command ids, layout ordering, one-time notice flags). No PII/health/location/payment data persisted client-side beyond the session.
  • auth guards (authGuard, permissionGuard, anyPermissionGuard, platform.guard) all re-check auth.isAuthenticated()/hasPermission and set returnUrl to the internal state.url; they are client-side UX gates backed by the server's RequirePermission filter (defense-in-depth intact).
  • auth.interceptor.ts: the app JWT is attached ONLY when req.url.startsWith('/api') (a same-origin relative path), so the bearer is never sent to third-party/cross-origin URLs — no token-leak-to-external-host via the interceptor.
  • All markdown rendered with [innerHTML] (family/notes, note-editor-dialog, family-notes-mobile) flows through renderMarkdown(), which HTML-escapes the entire source FIRST (escapeHtml on &<>"') and only re-emits a fixed tag set; link hrefs are scheme-checked (safeUrl drops javascript:/data:) AND re-escaped, and attribute breakout via quotes is prevented — the bypassSecurityTrustHtml calls operate on already-sanitized HTML. XSS via the note markdown path is sound (the only residual is the already-reported protocol-relative // link allowance).
  • No eval / new Function / document.write / insertAdjacentHTML / outerHTML anywhere in the SPA, and the only postMessage reference is a comment — no dynamic-code or DOM-injection sink.
  • SignalR chat hub URL is same-origin ('/api/hubs/chat') and the JWT is supplied via accessTokenFactory as ?access_token=, so the token stays same-origin in the WebSocket handshake and does not leak cross-origin.
  • Public unauthenticated page public-bill.html renders all user-authored fields (title, item names, claimedByName, person names) via {{ }} interpolation (auto-escaped) and payment URLs via Angular-sanitized [href] — no [innerHTML], no XSS sink on the public surface.
  • core/pwa.ts onNotificationClick correctly restricts push-notification navigation to targets that start with '/' (rejecting absolute/protocol-relative URLs) — this handler is the correct reference pattern.
  • Avatar <img [src]="u.picture"> bindings in app.html all set referrerpolicy="no-referrer" and rely on Angular's URL sanitizer, so third-party avatar hosts get no Referer and no javascript:/data: src survives.
  • localStorage/sessionStorage writes across the SPA (theme, platform, tour-seen, command-palette recents, hub/widget layout, one-time notice flags, Fitbit PKCE verifier in sessionStorage) hold no bearer tokens or PII beyond the already-reported auth session — no additional sensitive-data-in-storage exposure.
  • permission.guard / any-permission.guard / auth.guard are client-side gates that only createUrlTree to /login or /welcome; the returnUrl they capture is state.url (the app's own in-app path), and real enforcement is the backend RequirePermission filter — the guards themselves introduce no bypass.
  • router.navigateByUrl is used for all in-app redirects (notification/search/command-palette/pwa); Angular's navigateByUrl does not perform cross-origin browser navigation, so returnUrl/deepLink values cannot drive a window-level external redirect (the returnUrl trust issue was already reported for signin).
EF entities & migrations 30
  • Decimal precision for money/tokens is sound throughout: UsageRecord.CostUsd is decimal(18,8), ModelPricing rates are decimal(12,4), FinanceTransaction/Budget/SavingsGoal/BalanceSnapshot money is decimal(18,2), FamilyChore/Completion credits are decimal(10,2), VitalReading is (8,2). No money/token value is stored as float or left at EF's default precision (UsageDbContext.cs:115,156-160,1003-1004,1077,1090-1091,1103).
  • DateTime/timestamptz handling is fully consistent: every DateTime column is *Utc-suffixed and explicitly HasColumnType("timestamp with time zone"); a grep for non-Utc `public DateTime` entity properties returned nothing, so there is no Npgsql Kind-mismatch exposure.
  • Filtered unique indexes are used correctly and pervasively for optional-key uniqueness: AppUser.GoogleSubject, Bill.ShareTokenHash, ChatChannel.DirectKey, FinanceTransaction.(HouseholdId,Fitid) and .(HouseholdId,DedupHash WHERE Fitid IS NULL), HealthImportLog.SourceRef, IdentityTimeEntry.SourceEventId, HardChallenge active-run WHERE Status=0 — all guard nullable/partial keys without breaking non-participating rows.
  • Orphan-preservation cascades are deliberate and correct where usage/money history must outlive a parent: IngestKey.UserId is SetNull (usage-bearing keys survive user deletion, UsageDbContext.cs:391-393), ExerciseEntry.ExerciseId is SetNull (a logged workout survives library-activity deletion, 719-722), FamilyCreditEntry.ChoreCompletionId is SetNull (money history survives chore deletion, 978-981).
  • Owned-child cascades are appropriate: Household->Members, ChatChannel->Members/Messages, Bill->Items, FamilyList->Items, Recipe->Ingredients, Poll->Options->Votes, Habit->Days, HardChallenge->Days/Tasks, Medication->Logs all cascade to the true owning aggregate root.
  • Seed/bootstrap logic in Program.cs (lines 406-509) is idempotent: AppConfig/IngestionSources/ExerciseLibrary seed only when absent or empty; admin promotion is upsert-and-additive (never removes perms, always re-enables break-glass admins); allowed-email seed is create-only via AnyAsync guard. Runs after MigrateAsync inside a scoped context.
  • The WeightSlots migration (20260619053719) safely converts the (UserEmail,LocalDate) unique index to (UserEmail,LocalDate,Slot) by adding Slot NOT NULL default 0 — because the prior index was already unique per day, existing rows stay unique under the new key, so the new unique index cannot fail on live data.
  • Concurrency-token absence was verified to be repo-wide (grep for IsRowVersion/IsConcurrencyToken/xmin/RowVersion = 0 matches) and confirmed to matter only for the read-modify-write money/claim paths; the analytic/append-only tables (UsageRecord, logs) are not affected.
  • RequestLog documents bodies as truncated + secret/auth-route redacted before persistence (RequestLog.cs:4-5,26) and the table is writer-pruned to a recent window, so it is not an unbounded PII sink from the schema's perspective.
  • TrackerProfile.BaselineReviewedUtc lacks an explicit .HasColumnType("timestamp with time zone") mapping in UsageDbContext, but its migration (20260625041826_RobustGoalFields.cs ln 14-18) creates it as 'timestamp with time zone' because Npgsql defaults DateTime -> timestamptz. So despite the missing explicit map it is correctly timestamptz at the DB level — the prior round's 'DateTime columns consistently timestamptz' verdict holds even for this outlier.
  • FinanceTransaction.ImportId (entity ln 64) is a plain required long with an index only (UsageDbContext ln 1058 is inside the FinanceStagedTransaction block, not FinanceTransaction); the committed ledger has NO cascade FK to FinanceImport, so deleting/discarding an import cannot destroy committed transactions. The delete-import endpoint (FamilyFinanceEndpoints ln 524-545) additionally blocks committed batches and only removes staged rows. Sound.
  • FinanceStagedTransaction -> FinanceImport cascade (UsageDbContext ln 1060-1061) is correct and desired: discarding a batch removes only its staged (uncommitted) rows.
  • Households and FinanceImports are never hard-deleted anywhere (no .Remove call found), so the FinanceAccount->FinanceTransaction/FinanceBalanceSnapshot cascades and Household->members cascade are latent-only, not currently triggerable data-loss paths.
  • Projects are never deleted (no Projects.Remove found), so the UsageRecord->Project cascade is latent-only.
  • Program.cs seed logic (ln 407-509) is idempotent: AppConfig/IngestionSources/ExerciseLibrary are seeded only when absent/empty; admin bootstrap is upsert-and-promote (break-glass), allowlisted emails are create-only. MigrateAsync is applied before seeding.
  • The RenameBetaAccessToPlatformMobile data migration (20260627005511) correctly de-duplicates users holding BOTH legacy perms before the UNIQUE(UserId,Permission) rename to avoid a collision (step 1 deletes tracker.beta when beta.access exists, then renames).
  • IngestKey.UserId is int? and uses OnDelete SetNull (UsageDbContext ln 391-393) — nullable FK + SetNull is valid, correctly orphaning usage-bearing keys instead of destroying them when a user is deleted.
  • ExerciseEntry.ExerciseId and SleepEntry/DailyActivity Source are nullable/defaulted appropriately; ExerciseEntry->ExerciseLibrary SetNull (ln 721-722) with nullable ExerciseId preserves logged workouts when a library activity is deleted (name is snapshotted).
  • Money/decimal precision is explicitly set on all financial columns (FinanceTransaction/Budget/SavingsGoal/BalanceSnapshot at 18,2; Bill/BillItem/FamilyList at numeric(12,2); FamilyChore/FamilyCreditEntry/FamilyChoreCompletion at 10,2; ModelPricing 12,4; UsageRecord.CostUsd 18,8) — no unspecified/default-precision money columns found.
  • Filtered-unique indexes for nullable business keys are consistently and correctly applied (GoogleSubject IS NOT NULL, ShareTokenHash IS NOT NULL, ChatChannel.DirectKey IS NOT NULL, FinanceTransaction Fitid IS NOT NULL / DedupHash WHERE Fitid IS NULL, HealthImportLog SourceRef IS NOT NULL, IdentityTimeEntry SourceEventId IS NOT NULL) — these correctly avoid the NULL-distinct trap (unlike the FinanceAccount.Institution case which does not use a filter).
  • All DateTime columns in OnModelCreating are consistently mapped to 'timestamp with time zone' (timestamptz) — verified across UsageRecord, AppUser, ShareLink, MachineInfo, Finance*, Cycle*, Medication*, etc.; no bare 'timestamp without time zone' found.
  • Deliberate/documented cascade choices are safe: IngestKey and ExerciseEntry FKs use DeleteBehavior.SetNull to orphan rather than destroy usage-bearing rows; FamilyCreditEntry.ChoreCompletionId uses SetNull to preserve the allowance money-history when a chore is deleted.
  • MedicationLog's cascade from Medication is dormant in practice: the DELETE /meds/{id} endpoint (MedsEndpoints.cs:202-209) soft-deletes via SetProperty(m => m.Active, false) and never physically removes the medication row, so the cascade never fires and adherence history is not lost.
  • Money/precision mappings are explicit where it matters: FinanceTransaction/StagedTransaction Magnitude+RawAmount numeric(18,2), FinanceBudget.LimitAmount / FinanceSavingsGoal / FinanceBalanceSnapshot / Bill / BillItem / FamilyChore(Completion) all carry explicit HasPrecision; ModelPricing per-MTok columns are (12,4); UsageRecord.CostUsd is (18,8).
  • SleepEntry.Hours and SupplementEntry.ProteinG/CarbG/FatG are decimals with no explicit precision, mapping to unqualified Postgres 'numeric' (arbitrary precision) — no truncation/data-loss risk, only a minor inconsistency.
  • Filtered-unique indexes are correctly used for singleton/optional-key rows: AppUser.GoogleSubject, Bill.ShareTokenHash, ChatChannel.DirectKey, FinanceTransaction.Fitid, HealthImportLog.SourceRef, IdentityTimeEntry.SourceEventId — all filtered to IS NOT NULL, which avoids the NULL-distinct trap that FinanceBudget's overall-budget index falls into.
  • The RenameBetaAccessToPlatformMobile data migration is written defensively and idempotently: it de-duplicates users holding both legacy permission keys before the UNIQUE(UserId,Permission)-colliding UPDATE, and scrubs the retired keys from DefaultPermissionsCsv in any CSV position.
  • BankTransactionImport migration correctly drops the old unbounded unique DedupHash index and recreates it filtered to Fitid IS NULL in the same migration that adds the (all-NULL) Fitid column, so all pre-existing rows remain covered and the split (Fitid vs DedupHash) dedup backstop is consistent.
  • DiscordCategoriesDropStoreDefault only alters the column default (not stored data) and its rationale is sound — dropping the store default lets an explicit all-off mask (0) persist rather than being rewritten to 127 by EF's 'unset' handling.
  • Admin bootstrap seed (Program.cs:465-508) is idempotent and safe: admins are upserted (always re-enabled + granted any missing permission keys) so the owner can't be locked out, and allowlisted emails are create-only guarded by AnyAsync(email).
Frontend accessibility 30
  • index.html declares <html lang="en">; app.html provides a proper skip-to-content link (line 3) targeting <main id="main-content" tabindex="-1"> (line 704).
  • Icon-only buttons are consistently named: a full scan of every <button> whose only content is a mat-icon/svg found zero without aria-label/aria-labelledby/title (e.g. Quick add, Snap, command palette, close, refresh, pagination buttons all carry aria-label + matTooltip).
  • Images are handled correctly: decorative avatars use alt="" and referrerpolicy="no-referrer"; meaningful images (weather icon, exercise GIF, meal-picker avatars, headshot) bind [alt] to a descriptive name.
  • The hand-rolled role="dialog" overlays (habits editor/calendar, finance savings-goal modal) all apply the dialogA11y directive, which implements the full modal quartet: opener capture + restore, focus-move-in, Tab/Shift+Tab trap, and Escape-to-dismiss.
  • The shared beta-ui BetaBottomSheet (app-bs-sheet) is a robust modal primitive: role=dialog + aria-modal + tabindex=-1, focus trap, opener restore, Escape/scrim dismiss, and a prefers-reduced-motion collapse.
  • Material dialogs (tracker add-food/profile/etc.) use mat-dialog-title/mat-dialog-content, inheriting CDK focus-trap, aria-modal, and Escape handling.
  • Non-interactive click handlers are mitigated: <div (click)> cases are scrims (aria-hidden) or $event.stopPropagation()/role=presentation guards; the logs.html <tr (click)> row-toggle has a keyboard-accessible <button aria-label="Expand row" aria-expanded> equivalent in the last cell.
  • Async/status regions are extensively announced: aria-live="polite", role="status", and role="alert" are used across the mobile pages, chat-beta recap/typing regions, and family AI assistant panels for loading/error/AI-result states.
  • Data visualizations expose text alternatives: SVG/bar charts use role="img" with a computed [attr.aria-label] (identity-map chart line 64, finance cache bar, dashboard cache-bar line 275).
  • Form fields across agents, automations, habits, journal, finance goal modal, and tracker inline-edit are wrapped in <label> elements with <span> field labels (implicit labeling), and mat-slide-toggle/checkbox controls carry per-item [attr.aria-label].
  • Icon-only buttons app-wide (bills, chat, grocery, people, family, notification-bell, meds) consistently carry aria-label and wrap their mat-icon with aria-hidden="true" — accessible-name coverage for icon buttons is sound.
  • All <img> elements checked (family avatars, weather icons, about portrait, exercise GIFs, presence widget) supply a bound [alt] or are decorative with aria-hidden; about.ts falls back to a role=img monogram with aria-label. No missing-alt defects found.
  • The command palette implements a correct ARIA combobox+listbox: role=combobox/aria-expanded/aria-controls/aria-activedescendant on the input, role=listbox/option rows, Escape/Arrow/Enter handling, and a Tab focus trap in command-palette.ts (lines 325-353).
  • The beta-ui bottom-sheet primitive (bottom-sheet.ts) implements a full modal contract: role=dialog/aria-modal, focus-move-in on open, Tab/Shift+Tab trap, Escape dismiss gated by dismissable(), and focus restore to the opener on close.
  • The guided-tour overlay (guided-tour.ts) is a properly built role=dialog: focus moves into the card each step, Tab is trapped, Escape skips, and focus is restored on close.
  • Form inputs sampled in bills.html and ai-day-builder-dialog.html that at first looked placeholder-only actually carry aria-label (or bound [attr.aria-label]) on adjacent lines; Material dialogs (add-food, add-exercise, profile) rely on CDK focus-trap and per-field aria-labels.
  • index.html sets <html lang="en">; the no-flash theme bootstrap and keyboard-inset scripts do not interfere with the accessibility tree.
  • notification-bell uses a persistent visually-hidden aria-live="polite" aria-atomic region for unread-count changes and keeps the visual badge aria-hidden so the count is announced exactly once; the MatMenu root is relabeled region to avoid a nested-region duplicate.
  • Heading structure is broadly healthy: 104 of ~109 feature templates contain an <h1>, and per-page section headings descend from it; no systemic heading-order defect found.
  • Non-interactive elements with (click) are limited to scrims (aria-hidden, backed by Escape/keyboard elsewhere) and stopPropagation wrappers; the one clickable <tr> in logs.html has a redundant keyboard-focusable expand button, so no keyboard-only trap.
  • <html lang="en"> is present in src/Web/src/index.html:2 — the document language is declared.
  • Skip-to-content link exists and correctly targets the focusable `<main id="main-content" tabindex="-1">` landmark (app.html:3, 704) — the plumbing is sound; only the programmatic focus-on-navigation is missing.
  • No <img> elements without an alt attribute were found across src/Web/src/app (grep for `<img` without `alt=` returned no matches); avatars correctly use decorative alt="".
  • Icon-only <button> elements consistently carry aria-label (quick-add, snap, command-palette trigger, presence, transport buttons, toast close) and their mat-icons are aria-hidden — no unlabelled icon buttons surfaced.
  • The command palette (command-palette.html/.ts) is a correctly-built combobox+listbox: aria-controls, aria-expanded, aria-activedescendant, role=option/aria-selected, Escape/Arrow/Enter handling, focus-into-input on open, and focus-restore to the opener on close.
  • Non-interactive click targets with role/keyboard gaps were largely absent: the only div-with-(click) in family/*.html is the finance scrim, which is aria-hidden and duplicated by a real close button.
  • Range slider in family-locations.html has an explicit aria-label ("Scrub replay time"); the row-exclude checkbox in finance.html has a dynamic aria-label; the negate/keep checkboxes are wrapped in <label> with text.
  • Legacy hand-rolled role=dialog overlays (habits editor/calendar) correctly apply dialogA11y with aria-modal + aria-label; the sensitive cycle/meds editors use Angular Material MatDialog, which provides its own focus trap and restore.
  • Heading order on sampled complex pages (finance.html, tracker.html, chat.html) is well-formed: a single <h1> followed by <h2> section headers, no skipped levels observed.
  • Mobile hamburger drawer OPEN state has correct focus management in app.ts (focus first item on open, Escape closes + restores focus to burger, Tab trapped) — the defect is only that the CLOSED state is not removed from the a11y tree.
Query performance & indexes 29
